31.1 Dockerfile Best Practices for Automation Optimized container images
📋 Dockerfile Optimization Checklist 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              DOCKERFILE BEST PRACTICES                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│ ✅ DO:                          ❌ DON'T:                        │
│ ───────                         ─────────                        │
│ • Use specific base tags        • Use :latest in production     │
│ • Multi-stage builds            • Install dev deps in prod      │
│ • .dockerignore file            • Run as root                   │
│ • Combine RUN commands          • Copy unnecessary files        │
│ • Non-root user                 • Store secrets in image        │
│ • Health checks                 • Ignore layer caching          │
│ • Pin package versions          • Large base images             │
│                                                                  │
│ LAYER OPTIMIZATION:                                             │
│ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│ │ 1. OS packages (rarely change)        ←── Cache friendly    │ │
│ │ 2. Dependencies (package.json, etc)                         │ │
│ │ 3. Application code (changes often)   ←── Rebuild often     │ │
│ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Optimized Dockerfile
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --only=production

COPY . .
RUN npm run build

# Production image
FROM node:20-alpine AS runtime
RUN addgroup -g 1001 -S app && adduser -S app -u 1001
WORKDIR /app

COPY --from=builder --chown=app:app /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder --chown=app:app /app/node_modules ./node_modules

USER app
EXPOSE 3000
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s \
  CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1

CMD ["node", "dist/index.js"]
31.2 Docker Build Scripts Automated image building
▶️ Build Automation Script
#!/bin/bash
# docker-build.sh - Automated Docker build with caching

set -euo pipefail

IMAGE_NAME="${1:-myapp}"
VERSION="${2:-$(git describe --tags --always)}"
REGISTRY="${DOCKER_REGISTRY:-ghcr.io/myorg}"

# Build with BuildKit for better caching
DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build \
    --build-arg BUILDKIT_INLINE_CACHE=1 \
    --cache-from "${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME}:latest" \
    --tag "${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME}:${VERSION}" \
    --tag "${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME}:latest" \
    --label "org.opencontainers.image.revision=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" \
    --label "org.opencontainers.image.created=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
    .

echo "✅ Built: ${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME}:${VERSION}"
31.3 Multi-Stage Build Automation Smaller, secure images
▶️ Multi-Stage Python Build
# Build stage
FROM python:3.12-slim AS builder
WORKDIR /app
RUN pip install --user poetry
COPY pyproject.toml poetry.lock ./
RUN poetry export -f requirements.txt -o requirements.txt
RUN pip install --user -r requirements.txt

# Test stage
FROM builder AS test
COPY . .
RUN pytest tests/

# Production stage
FROM python:3.12-slim AS production
COPY --from=builder /root/.local /root/.local
COPY src/ /app/
ENV PATH=/root/.local/bin:$PATH
CMD ["python", "-m", "app"]
31.4 Image Tagging and Versioning Consistent image naming
▶️ Tagging Strategy
#!/bin/bash
# Semantic versioning tags
VERSION="1.2.3"
IMAGE="myapp"

# Multiple tags for flexibility
docker tag $IMAGE:build $IMAGE:$VERSION      # Full version
docker tag $IMAGE:build $IMAGE:1.2           # Minor version
docker tag $IMAGE:build $IMAGE:1             # Major version
docker tag $IMAGE:build $IMAGE:latest        # Latest

# Git-based tags
docker tag $IMAGE:build $IMAGE:$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)
docker tag $IMAGE:build $IMAGE:$(git branch --show-current)
31.5 Docker Compose Automation Multi-container orchestration
▶️ Compose with Environment Overrides
# docker-compose.yaml
services:
  app:
    build: .
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=${NODE_ENV:-development}
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
        
  db:
    image: postgres:16
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U postgres"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

# Run: docker compose -f docker-compose.yaml -f docker-compose.prod.yaml up
31.6 Container Health Check Scripts Automated health monitoring
▶️ Health Check Script
#!/bin/bash
# check-containers.sh - Monitor container health

check_health() {
    local container=$1
    local status=$(docker inspect --format='{{.State.Health.Status}}' "$container" 2>/dev/null)
    echo "$container: ${status:-not running}"
    [[ "$status" == "healthy" ]]
}

# Check all containers
FAILED=0
for container in $(docker ps -q); do
    if ! check_health "$container"; then
        ((FAILED++))
    fi
done

exit $FAILED
31.7 Image Scanning and Security Container vulnerability scanning
▶️ Trivy Scan Integration
#!/bin/bash
# scan-image.sh - Security scanning with Trivy

IMAGE="${1:-myapp:latest}"
SEVERITY="${SEVERITY:-HIGH,CRITICAL}"

trivy image \
    --severity "$SEVERITY" \
    --exit-code 1 \
    --ignore-unfixed \
    --format table \
    "$IMAGE"

# In CI: trivy image --format sarif -o results.sarif $IMAGE
31.8 Registry Automation Push, pull, and cleanup
▶️ Registry Cleanup Script
#!/bin/bash
# cleanup-registry.sh - Remove old images

REGISTRY="myregistry.azurecr.io"
REPO="myapp"
KEEP_LAST=10

# List tags sorted by date, keep last N
az acr repository show-tags -n $REGISTRY --repository $REPO \
    --orderby time_desc -o tsv | tail -n +$((KEEP_LAST+1)) | \
    xargs -I {} az acr repository delete -n $REGISTRY \
    --image "$REPO:{}" --yes

echo "✅ Cleanup complete"
31.9 Docker-in-Docker for CI Building containers in CI
▶️ DinD in GitHub Actions
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      dind:
        image: docker:dind
        options: --privileged
    steps:
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          push: true
          tags: myapp:${{ github.sha }}

32.1 kubectl Scripting Command-line Kubernetes automation
📋 kubectl Essential Commands 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              KUBECTL QUICK REFERENCE                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│ CONTEXT:                         RESOURCES:                     │
│ kubectl config use-context dev   kubectl get pods -A            │
│ kubectl config current-context   kubectl describe pod <name>    │
│                                  kubectl logs <pod> -f          │
│                                                                  │
│ APPLY:                           DEBUG:                         │
│ kubectl apply -f manifest.yaml   kubectl exec -it <pod> -- bash │
│ kubectl apply -k ./kustomize     kubectl port-forward <pod> 8080│
│                                                                  │
│ OUTPUT FORMATS:                                                 │
│ -o wide        Extended info                                    │
│ -o yaml        Full YAML output                                 │
│ -o json        JSON output                                      │
│ -o jsonpath    JSONPath query                                   │
│ -o custom-columns  Custom table                                 │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ kubectl Automation Script
#!/bin/bash
# k8s-deploy.sh - Kubernetes deployment automation

NAMESPACE="${NAMESPACE:-default}"
DEPLOYMENT="${1:-myapp}"
IMAGE="${2:-myapp:latest}"

# Update image
kubectl set image deployment/$DEPLOYMENT \
    $DEPLOYMENT=$IMAGE -n $NAMESPACE

# Wait for rollout
kubectl rollout status deployment/$DEPLOYMENT \
    -n $NAMESPACE --timeout=300s

# Get pod status
kubectl get pods -n $NAMESPACE -l app=$DEPLOYMENT \
    -o custom-columns='NAME:.metadata.name,STATUS:.status.phase,READY:.status.containerStatuses[0].ready'
32.2 Manifest Generation and Templating Dynamic manifest creation
▶️ Python Manifest Generator
from jinja2 import Template

DEPLOYMENT_TEMPLATE = """
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{ name }}
  namespace: {{ namespace }}
spec:
  replicas: {{ replicas }}
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: {{ name }}
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: {{ name }}
        image: {{ image }}
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "{{ cpu_limit }}"
            memory: "{{ memory_limit }}"
"""

def generate_manifest(name, image, replicas=3):
    template = Template(DEPLOYMENT_TEMPLATE)
    return template.render(
        name=name, image=image, replicas=replicas,
        namespace="default", cpu_limit="500m", memory_limit="512Mi"
    )
32.3 Kustomize for Configuration Management Native K8s configuration
▶️ Kustomize Structure
# base/kustomization.yaml
resources:
  - deployment.yaml
  - service.yaml

# overlays/prod/kustomization.yaml
namespace: production
bases:
  - ../../base
patchesStrategicMerge:
  - increase-replicas.yaml
images:
  - name: myapp
    newTag: v1.2.3

# Apply: kubectl apply -k overlays/prod/
32.4 Automated Deployments Zero-downtime K8s deploys
▶️ Rolling Deployment Script
#!/bin/bash
# deploy-with-rollback.sh

deploy() {
    kubectl apply -f manifests/
    if ! kubectl rollout status deploy/$APP -n $NS --timeout=5m; then
        echo "❌ Deployment failed, rolling back..."
        kubectl rollout undo deploy/$APP -n $NS
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "✅ Deployment successful"
}

deploy
32.5 Namespace and RBAC Automation Access control automation
▶️ Namespace Setup Script
#!/bin/bash
# create-namespace.sh - Create namespace with RBAC

NAMESPACE="$1"
USER="$2"

kubectl create namespace $NAMESPACE --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

kubectl create rolebinding ${USER}-admin \
    --clusterrole=admin \
    --user=$USER \
    --namespace=$NAMESPACE
32.6 ConfigMap and Secret Management Configuration automation
▶️ Config Management
# Create ConfigMap from file
kubectl create configmap app-config \
    --from-file=config.yaml \
    --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

# Create Secret from env file
kubectl create secret generic app-secrets \
    --from-env-file=.env.prod \
    --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

# Sealed Secrets for GitOps
kubeseal --format yaml < secret.yaml > sealed-secret.yaml
32.7 Horizontal Pod Autoscaler Scripts Auto-scaling automation
▶️ HPA Configuration
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: myapp-hpa
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: myapp
  minReplicas: 2
  maxReplicas: 10
  metrics:
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: cpu
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 70
32.8 Pod Disruption Budget Automation Availability during maintenance
▶️ PDB for High Availability
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: myapp-pdb
spec:
  minAvailable: 2  # or maxUnavailable: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: myapp

# Script to check PDB status
# kubectl get pdb -A -o wide

Chapter 33: Helm Chart Automation

Automating Helm deployments and chart management

Chapter Topics

33.1 Helm Basics and Chart Structure
33.2 Chart Development and Templating
33.3 Values File Management
33.4 Helm Repository Automation
33.5 Helm in CI/CD Pipelines
33.6 Chart Testing and Linting
33.7 Helmfile for Multi-Chart Management
33.1 Helm Basics and Chart Structure Understanding Helm charts
📁 Helm Chart Structure 
mychart/
├── Chart.yaml          # Chart metadata
├── values.yaml         # Default configuration values
├── charts/             # Dependency charts
├── templates/          # Kubernetes manifests (Go templates)
│   ├── deployment.yaml
│   ├── service.yaml
│   ├── ingress.yaml
│   ├── _helpers.tpl    # Template helpers
│   └── NOTES.txt       # Post-install notes
└── .helmignore         # Files to ignore

HELM COMMANDS:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ helm create mychart         # Create new chart                  │
│ helm install release ./     # Install chart                     │
│ helm upgrade release ./     # Upgrade release                   │
│ helm rollback release 1     # Rollback to revision              │
│ helm uninstall release      # Remove release                    │
│ helm list -A               # List all releases                   │
│ helm template ./           # Render templates locally            │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
33.2 Chart Development and Templating Go template patterns
▶️ Helm Template Example
# templates/deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{ include "mychart.fullname" . }}
  labels:
    {{- include "mychart.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
spec:
  replicas: {{ .Values.replicaCount }}
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      {{- include "mychart.selectorLabels" . | nindent 6 }}
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: {{ .Chart.Name }}
        image: "{{ .Values.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.image.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}"
        {{- with .Values.resources }}
        resources:
          {{- toYaml . | nindent 10 }}
        {{- end }}
33.3 Values File Management Environment-specific values
▶️ Values Hierarchy
# values.yaml (defaults)
replicaCount: 1
image:
  repository: myapp
  tag: latest

# values-prod.yaml (overrides)
replicaCount: 3
image:
  tag: v1.2.3
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: 1000m
    memory: 1Gi

# Install with multiple values files
# helm install myapp ./ -f values.yaml -f values-prod.yaml --set image.tag=v1.2.4
33.4 Helm Repository Automation Chart distribution
▶️ Chart Repository Setup
#!/bin/bash
# publish-chart.sh

# Package chart
helm package ./mychart

# Generate index
helm repo index . --url https://charts.example.com

# Push to OCI registry (Helm 3.8+)
helm push mychart-1.0.0.tgz oci://ghcr.io/myorg/charts

# Add repo
helm repo add myrepo https://charts.example.com
helm repo update
33.5 Helm in CI/CD Pipelines Automated Helm deployments
▶️ GitHub Actions Helm Deploy
- name: Deploy with Helm
  run: |
    helm upgrade --install myapp ./chart \
      --namespace production \
      --set image.tag=${{ github.sha }} \
      --values ./chart/values-prod.yaml \
      --wait --timeout 10m \
      --atomic  # Rollback on failure
33.6 Chart Testing and Linting Validate charts before deploy
▶️ Chart Validation
# Lint chart
helm lint ./mychart

# Template validation
helm template ./mychart | kubectl apply --dry-run=server -f -

# Chart testing with ct (chart-testing)
ct lint --charts ./charts/
ct install --charts ./charts/

# Unit tests with helm-unittest
helm unittest ./mychart
33.7 Helmfile for Multi-Chart Management Declarative Helm deployments
▶️ Helmfile Example
# helmfile.yaml
repositories:
  - name: bitnami
    url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

releases:
  - name: nginx
    namespace: web
    chart: bitnami/nginx
    version: 15.0.0
    values:
      - values/nginx.yaml
      
  - name: redis
    namespace: cache
    chart: bitnami/redis
    version: 18.0.0
    
# helmfile sync --environment production

Chapter 34: GitOps Automation

Implementing GitOps workflows

Chapter Topics

34.1 GitOps Principles
34.2 ArgoCD Automation
34.3 Flux CD Setup and Scripting
34.4 Application Synchronization
34.5 Drift Detection and Reconciliation
34.6 Progressive Delivery with Argo Rollouts
34.7 GitOps for Infrastructure
34.1 GitOps Principles and Tools Git as the source of truth
🔄 GitOps Architecture 
GITOPS FLOW:
┌────────────┐   Push    ┌────────────┐   Sync    ┌────────────┐
│    Dev     │──────────▶│    Git     │◀─────────▶│   ArgoCD   │
│  Commits   │           │   Repo     │           │   / Flux   │
└────────────┘           └────────────┘           └─────┬──────┘
                                                        │
                                                        ▼ Apply
                                                 ┌────────────┐
                                                 │ Kubernetes │
                                                 │  Cluster   │
                                                 └────────────┘

GITOPS PRINCIPLES:
• Declarative ─── All infrastructure as code
• Versioned ──── Git provides audit trail
• Automated ──── Changes auto-applied
• Reconciled ─── Continuous drift detection
34.2 ArgoCD Application Automation Declarative GitOps
▶️ ArgoCD Application
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: myapp
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  project: default
  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/org/manifests
    targetRevision: main
    path: apps/myapp
  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
    namespace: production
  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: true
      selfHeal: true
    syncOptions:
      - CreateNamespace=true
34.3 Flux CD Configuration CNCF GitOps toolkit
▶️ Flux Kustomization
apiVersion: kustomize.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1
kind: Kustomization
metadata:
  name: myapp
  namespace: flux-system
spec:
  interval: 5m
  path: ./apps/production
  prune: true
  sourceRef:
    kind: GitRepository
    name: app-manifests
  healthChecks:
    - kind: Deployment
      name: myapp
      namespace: production
34.4 Sync and Reconciliation Scripts Manual sync triggers
▶️ ArgoCD Sync Script
#!/bin/bash
# argocd-sync.sh

APP_NAME=$1

# Sync application
argocd app sync $APP_NAME --prune

# Wait for healthy state
argocd app wait $APP_NAME --health --timeout 300

# Get sync status
argocd app get $APP_NAME -o json | jq '.status.sync.status'
34.5 Drift Detection Automation Detect configuration drift
▶️ Drift Detection Script
#!/bin/bash
# detect-drift.sh

# Get ArgoCD apps with drift
DRIFTED=$(argocd app list -o json | \
  jq -r '.[] | select(.status.sync.status != "Synced") | .metadata.name')

if [ -n "$DRIFTED" ]; then
  echo "⚠️ Drift detected in:"
  echo "$DRIFTED"
  # Alert or auto-sync
  for app in $DRIFTED; do
    argocd app sync $app --prune
  done
fi
34.6 Argo Rollouts Integration Canary and blue-green
▶️ Argo Rollout Definition
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Rollout
metadata:
  name: myapp
spec:
  replicas: 5
  strategy:
    canary:
      steps:
        - setWeight: 20
        - pause: {duration: 5m}
        - setWeight: 50
        - pause: {duration: 5m}
        - setWeight: 100
      analysis:
        templates:
          - templateName: success-rate
34.7 GitOps for Infrastructure Terraform + GitOps
▶️ Terraform Controller
# Flux Terraform Controller
apiVersion: infra.contrib.fluxcd.io/v1alpha2
kind: Terraform
metadata:
  name: vpc
  namespace: flux-system
spec:
  path: ./terraform/vpc
  sourceRef:
    kind: GitRepository
    name: infra
  interval: 1h
  approvePlan: auto
  destroyResourcesOnDeletion: false

Chapter 35: Service Mesh Automation

Automating service mesh configurations

Chapter Topics

35.1 Istio Configuration Scripts
35.2 Traffic Management Automation
35.3 mTLS and Security Policies
35.4 Observability Configurations
35.5 Canary Deployments with Service Mesh
35.1 Istio Service Mesh Basics Service mesh fundamentals
🕸️ Service Mesh Architecture 
ISTIO COMPONENTS:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     CONTROL PLANE                            │
│  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐        │
│  │  Pilot  │  │ Citadel │  │ Galley  │  │  istiod │        │
│  └────┬────┘  └────┬────┘  └────┬────┘  └────┬────┘        │
└───────┼────────────┼────────────┼────────────┼──────────────┘
        │            │            │            │
┌───────┼────────────┼────────────┼────────────┼──────────────┐
│       ▼            ▼            ▼            ▼  DATA PLANE  │
│  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐                                   │
│  │ Pod A   │  │ Pod B   │                                   │
│  │ ┌─────┐ │  │ ┌─────┐ │   Envoy Sidecar Proxies          │
│  │ │Envoy│◀┼──┼▶│Envoy│ │                                   │
│  │ └─────┘ │  │ └─────┘ │                                   │
│  └─────────┘  └─────────┘                                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
35.2 Traffic Management Automation VirtualService and DestinationRule
▶️ Traffic Routing
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: myapp
spec:
  hosts:
    - myapp
  http:
    - match:
        - headers:
            x-canary:
              exact: "true"
      route:
        - destination:
            host: myapp
            subset: canary
    - route:
        - destination:
            host: myapp
            subset: stable
---
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: myapp
spec:
  host: myapp
  subsets:
    - name: stable
      labels:
        version: v1
    - name: canary
      labels:
        version: v2
35.3 mTLS and Security Policies Zero-trust networking
▶️ mTLS Configuration
# Enable strict mTLS
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: PeerAuthentication
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: production
spec:
  mtls:
    mode: STRICT
---
# Authorization Policy
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-frontend
  namespace: production
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: backend
  action: ALLOW
  rules:
    - from:
        - source:
            principals: ["cluster.local/ns/production/sa/frontend"]
35.4 Service Mesh Observability Metrics, tracing, visualization
▶️ Istio Observability Script
#!/bin/bash
# mesh-observability.sh

# Get mesh status
istioctl proxy-status

# Analyze configuration
istioctl analyze --all-namespaces

# Get Envoy metrics
kubectl exec -it pod/myapp-xxx -c istio-proxy -- \
  curl localhost:15000/stats/prometheus | grep istio_requests

# Access dashboards
istioctl dashboard kiali
istioctl dashboard jaeger
istioctl dashboard grafana
35.5 Canary Deployments with Service Mesh Weighted traffic splitting
▶️ Canary Traffic Script
#!/bin/bash
# istio-canary.sh

APP=$1
CANARY_WEIGHT=${2:-10}

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: \$APP
spec:
  hosts:
    - \$APP
  http:
    - route:
        - destination:
            host: \$APP
            subset: stable
          weight: \$((100 - CANARY_WEIGHT))
        - destination:
            host: \$APP
            subset: canary
          weight: \$CANARY_WEIGHT
EOF

echo "Canary at \$CANARY_WEIGHT% traffic"

☁️ Part 9: AWS Scripting & Automation

Comprehensive AWS automation with CLI, Boto3, and service-specific scripting.

Chapter 36: AWS CLI Scripting

Mastering AWS CLI for automation

Chapter Topics

36.1 AWS CLI Setup and Configuration
36.2 CLI Output Formats (json, text, table)
36.3 Filtering with --query (JMESPath)
36.4 Pagination Handling
36.5 Profile Management
36.6 Environment Variables for AWS
36.7 Combining AWS CLI with Bash
36.8 AWS CLI Aliases
36.1 AWS CLI Setup and Configuration Install and configure AWS CLI
⚙️ AWS CLI Configuration 
AWS CLI CONFIG FILES:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ~/.aws/credentials          │  ~/.aws/config                   │
│  [default]                   │  [default]                       │
│  aws_access_key_id=AKIA...   │  region=us-east-1                │
│  aws_secret_access_key=...   │  output=json                     │
│                              │                                   │
│  [prod]                      │  [profile prod]                  │
│  aws_access_key_id=AKIA...   │  region=us-west-2                │
│  aws_secret_access_key=...   │  output=table                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

# Configure default profile
aws configure

# List current config
aws configure list

# Configure named profile
aws configure --profile prod
36.2 CLI Output Formats JSON, text, table outputs
▶️ Output Format Examples
# JSON (default - best for parsing)
aws ec2 describe-instances --output json

# Table (human-readable)
aws ec2 describe-instances --output table

# Text (tab-separated - good for shell)
aws ec2 describe-instances --output text

# Set default in config
aws configure set output json --profile default
36.3 Filtering with --query (JMESPath) Server-side filtering
▶️ JMESPath Query Examples
# Get instance IDs only
aws ec2 describe-instances \
  --query 'Reservations[].Instances[].InstanceId' --output text

# Filter by state
aws ec2 describe-instances \
  --query 'Reservations[].Instances[?State.Name==`running`].InstanceId'

# Get specific fields
aws ec2 describe-instances \
  --query 'Reservations[].Instances[].[InstanceId,InstanceType,State.Name]' \
  --output table

# Sort results
aws ec2 describe-instances \
  --query 'sort_by(Reservations[].Instances[], &LaunchTime)[].InstanceId'
36.4 Pagination Handling Handle large result sets
▶️ Pagination Script
#!/bin/bash
# paginate-s3.sh - Handle paginated S3 listings

BUCKET="my-bucket"
NEXT_TOKEN=""

while true; do
  if [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ]; then
    RESULT=$(aws s3api list-objects-v2 --bucket $BUCKET --max-items 1000)
  else
    RESULT=$(aws s3api list-objects-v2 --bucket $BUCKET \
      --starting-token "$NEXT_TOKEN" --max-items 1000)
  fi
  
  echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.Contents[].Key'
  
  NEXT_TOKEN=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.NextToken // empty')
  [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ] && break
done
36.5 Profile Management Multi-account switching
▶️ Profile Usage
# Use specific profile
aws s3 ls --profile prod

# Set profile for session
export AWS_PROFILE=prod

# Assume role profile (~/.aws/config)
# [profile cross-account]
# role_arn = arn:aws:iam::123456789:role/AdminRole
# source_profile = default

aws sts get-caller-identity --profile cross-account
36.6 Environment Variables for AWS CI/CD configuration
▶️ AWS Environment Variables
# Credential variables
export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID="AKIA..."
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY="..."
export AWS_SESSION_TOKEN="..."  # For temp credentials

# Configuration variables
export AWS_DEFAULT_REGION="us-east-1"
export AWS_DEFAULT_OUTPUT="json"
export AWS_PROFILE="prod"

# STS temporary credentials
eval $(aws sts assume-role --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123:role/Role \
  --role-session-name script \
  --query 'Credentials.[AccessKeyId,SecretAccessKey,SessionToken]' \
  --output text | \
  awk '{print "export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID="$1" AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY="$2" AWS_SESSION_TOKEN="$3}')
36.7 Combining AWS CLI with Bash Advanced shell patterns
▶️ AWS CLI Bash Patterns
#!/bin/bash
# aws-batch-operations.sh

# Loop through instances
for instance in $(aws ec2 describe-instances \
  --query 'Reservations[].Instances[].InstanceId' --output text); do
  echo "Processing $instance"
  aws ec2 create-tags --resources $instance --tags Key=Processed,Value=true
done

# Parallel operations
aws ec2 describe-instances --query 'Reservations[].Instances[].InstanceId' \
  --output text | xargs -P 4 -I {} aws ec2 stop-instances --instance-ids {}

# Check command success
if aws s3 cp file.txt s3://bucket/; then
  echo "Upload successful"
fi
36.8 AWS CLI Aliases Custom shortcut commands
▶️ ~/.aws/cli/alias
[toplevel]
# List running instances
running-instances = ec2 describe-instances \
  --query 'Reservations[].Instances[?State.Name==`running`].[InstanceId,Tags[?Key==`Name`].Value|[0]]' \
  --output table

# Who am I
whoami = sts get-caller-identity

# List my buckets with size
bucket-sizes = !f() {
  for bucket in $(aws s3api list-buckets --query 'Buckets[].Name' --output text); do
    size=$(aws s3 ls s3://$bucket --recursive --summarize | tail -1)
    echo "$bucket: $size"
  done
}; f

# Usage: aws running-instances

Chapter 37: EC2 Automation

Automating EC2 instance management

Chapter Topics

37.1 Instance Lifecycle Management
37.2 AMI Creation and Management Scripts
37.3 Security Group Automation
37.4 Key Pair Management
37.5 Elastic IP Automation
37.6 Instance Tagging Scripts
37.7 Cost Optimization (Start/Stop Schedules)
37.8 Auto Scaling Automation
37.9 Launch Templates and AMI Pipeline
37.1 Instance Lifecycle Management Start, stop, terminate instances
▶️ EC2 Lifecycle Script
#!/bin/bash
# ec2-lifecycle.sh - Manage EC2 instance lifecycle

ACTION=$1
INSTANCE_ID=$2

case $ACTION in
  start)
    aws ec2 start-instances --instance-ids $INSTANCE_ID
    aws ec2 wait instance-running --instance-ids $INSTANCE_ID
    echo "Instance $INSTANCE_ID is running"
    ;;
  stop)
    aws ec2 stop-instances --instance-ids $INSTANCE_ID
    aws ec2 wait instance-stopped --instance-ids $INSTANCE_ID
    echo "Instance $INSTANCE_ID is stopped"
    ;;
  terminate)
    aws ec2 terminate-instances --instance-ids $INSTANCE_ID
    echo "Instance $INSTANCE_ID terminated"
    ;;
esac
37.2 AMI Creation and Management Automated AMI scripts
▶️ AMI Backup Script
#!/bin/bash
# create-ami.sh

INSTANCE_ID=$1
DATE=$(date +%Y%m%d)
NAME="backup-${INSTANCE_ID}-${DATE}"

# Create AMI
AMI_ID=$(aws ec2 create-image \
  --instance-id $INSTANCE_ID \
  --name "$NAME" \
  --no-reboot \
  --query 'ImageId' --output text)

echo "Created AMI: $AMI_ID"

# Wait for completion
aws ec2 wait image-available --image-ids $AMI_ID

# Tag AMI
aws ec2 create-tags --resources $AMI_ID \
  --tags Key=Name,Value=$NAME Key=Source,Value=$INSTANCE_ID
37.3 Security Group Automation Create and manage SGs
▶️ Security Group Script
#!/bin/bash
# create-sg.sh

VPC_ID=$1
SG_NAME="web-servers"

# Create security group
SG_ID=$(aws ec2 create-security-group \
  --group-name $SG_NAME \
  --description "Web server security group" \
  --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --query 'GroupId' --output text)

# Add rules
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress --group-id $SG_ID \
  --protocol tcp --port 80 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0

aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress --group-id $SG_ID \
  --protocol tcp --port 443 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0

aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress --group-id $SG_ID \
  --protocol tcp --port 22 --cidr 10.0.0.0/8
37.4 Key Pair Management SSH key automation
▶️ Key Pair Script
#!/bin/bash
# manage-keypair.sh

KEY_NAME="my-key-$(date +%Y%m%d)"

# Create key pair and save locally
aws ec2 create-key-pair \
  --key-name $KEY_NAME \
  --query 'KeyMaterial' --output text > ~/.ssh/$KEY_NAME.pem

chmod 400 ~/.ssh/$KEY_NAME.pem
echo "Key saved to ~/.ssh/$KEY_NAME.pem"

# Import existing public key
# aws ec2 import-key-pair --key-name $KEY_NAME \
#   --public-key-material fileb://~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub
37.5 Elastic IP Automation Allocate and associate EIPs
▶️ EIP Management
#!/bin/bash
# manage-eip.sh

INSTANCE_ID=$1

# Allocate EIP
ALLOCATION=$(aws ec2 allocate-address --domain vpc)
ALLOC_ID=$(echo $ALLOCATION | jq -r '.AllocationId')
EIP=$(echo $ALLOCATION | jq -r '.PublicIp')

# Associate with instance
aws ec2 associate-address \
  --instance-id $INSTANCE_ID \
  --allocation-id $ALLOC_ID

echo "Associated $EIP with $INSTANCE_ID"

# Release unused EIPs
# aws ec2 describe-addresses --query 'Addresses[?!InstanceId].AllocationId' \
#   --output text | xargs -I {} aws ec2 release-address --allocation-id {}
37.6 Instance Tagging Scripts Bulk tagging operations
▶️ Tagging Script
#!/bin/bash
# bulk-tag.sh - Tag all instances in a VPC

VPC_ID=$1
ENV=$2

# Get all instances in VPC
INSTANCES=$(aws ec2 describe-instances \
  --filters "Name=vpc-id,Values=$VPC_ID" \
  --query 'Reservations[].Instances[].InstanceId' --output text)

# Apply tags
for instance in $INSTANCES; do
  aws ec2 create-tags --resources $instance \
    --tags Key=Environment,Value=$ENV Key=ManagedBy,Value=Script
  echo "Tagged $instance"
done
37.7 Cost Optimization Scripts Scheduled start/stop
▶️ Scheduled Instance Control
#!/bin/bash
# schedule-instances.sh - Start/stop by schedule tag

ACTION=$1  # start or stop

# Get instances with matching Schedule tag
INSTANCES=$(aws ec2 describe-instances \
  --filters "Name=tag:Schedule,Values=business-hours" \
            "Name=instance-state-name,Values=$([ $ACTION = 'start' ] && echo 'stopped' || echo 'running')" \
  --query 'Reservations[].Instances[].InstanceId' --output text)

if [ -n "$INSTANCES" ]; then
  aws ec2 ${ACTION}-instances --instance-ids $INSTANCES
  echo "${ACTION}ed: $INSTANCES"
fi

# Run via cron:
# 0 8 * * 1-5 /path/schedule-instances.sh start
# 0 18 * * 1-5 /path/schedule-instances.sh stop
37.8 Auto Scaling Automation ASG management scripts
▶️ Auto Scaling Script
#!/bin/bash
# asg-update.sh

ASG_NAME=$1
DESIRED=$2

# Update capacity
aws autoscaling update-auto-scaling-group \
  --auto-scaling-group-name $ASG_NAME \
  --desired-capacity $DESIRED

# Instance refresh for rolling update
aws autoscaling start-instance-refresh \
  --auto-scaling-group-name $ASG_NAME \
  --preferences '{"MinHealthyPercentage": 90}'

# Monitor refresh
aws autoscaling describe-instance-refreshes \
  --auto-scaling-group-name $ASG_NAME \
  --query 'InstanceRefreshes[0].Status'
37.9 Launch Templates and AMI Pipeline Golden AMI workflow
▶️ Launch Template Creation
#!/bin/bash
# create-launch-template.sh

TEMPLATE_NAME="webapp-template"
AMI_ID=$1

aws ec2 create-launch-template \
  --launch-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --version-description "v1.0" \
  --launch-template-data '{
    "ImageId": "'$AMI_ID'",
    "InstanceType": "t3.medium",
    "KeyName": "my-key",
    "SecurityGroupIds": ["sg-12345678"],
    "TagSpecifications": [{
      "ResourceType": "instance",
      "Tags": [{"Key": "Name", "Value": "webapp"}]
    }]
  }'

# Create new version
aws ec2 create-launch-template-version \
  --launch-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --source-version 1 \
  --launch-template-data '{"ImageId": "'$NEW_AMI'"}'

Chapter 38: S3 Automation

Automating S3 bucket and object operations

Chapter Topics

38.1 Bucket Operations
38.2 Object Upload/Download Automation
38.3 Sync Scripts (aws s3 sync)
38.4 Lifecycle Policy Automation
38.5 Presigned URL Generation
38.6 Cross-Account Copy Scripts
38.7 S3 Event-Triggered Automation
38.8 Versioning and Replication Scripts
38.1 Bucket Operations Create and configure buckets
▶️ Bucket Management
#!/bin/bash
# create-bucket.sh

BUCKET="my-bucket-$(date +%s)"
REGION="us-east-1"

# Create bucket
aws s3api create-bucket --bucket $BUCKET --region $REGION

# Enable versioning
aws s3api put-bucket-versioning --bucket $BUCKET \
  --versioning-configuration Status=Enabled

# Block public access
aws s3api put-public-access-block --bucket $BUCKET \
  --public-access-block-configuration \
  "BlockPublicAcls=true,IgnorePublicAcls=true,BlockPublicPolicy=true,RestrictPublicBuckets=true"

# Enable encryption
aws s3api put-bucket-encryption --bucket $BUCKET \
  --server-side-encryption-configuration \
  '{"Rules":[{"ApplyServerSideEncryptionByDefault":{"SSEAlgorithm":"AES256"}}]}'
38.2 Object Upload/Download Automation File transfer scripts
▶️ Upload Script
#!/bin/bash
# s3-transfer.sh

upload() {
  local file=$1
  local bucket=$2
  aws s3 cp "$file" "s3://$bucket/" \
    --storage-class STANDARD_IA \
    --metadata "uploaded-by=script,date=$(date -I)"
}

# Batch upload
for file in /data/*.csv; do
  upload "$file" "my-data-bucket"
done

# Download with progress
aws s3 cp s3://bucket/large-file.zip . --only-show-errors

# Multi-part upload (automatic for >8MB)
aws s3 cp large-file.tar.gz s3://bucket/ --expected-size 5368709120
38.3 Sync Scripts (aws s3 sync) Bidirectional synchronization
▶️ S3 Sync Patterns
#!/bin/bash
# s3-sync.sh

SOURCE="/var/www/html"
BUCKET="my-website-bucket"

# Sync local to S3
aws s3 sync $SOURCE s3://$BUCKET/ --delete

# Sync with exclusions
aws s3 sync $SOURCE s3://$BUCKET/ \
  --exclude "*.log" \
  --exclude ".git/*" \
  --exclude "node_modules/*"

# Sync between buckets
aws s3 sync s3://source-bucket s3://dest-bucket --source-region us-east-1

# Dry run
aws s3 sync $SOURCE s3://$BUCKET/ --dryrun
38.4 Lifecycle Policy Automation Automated storage tiering
▶️ Lifecycle Policy
#!/bin/bash
# set-lifecycle.sh

BUCKET=$1

aws s3api put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration \
  --bucket $BUCKET \
  --lifecycle-configuration '{
    "Rules": [{
      "ID": "Archive old logs",
      "Status": "Enabled",
      "Filter": {"Prefix": "logs/"},
      "Transitions": [
        {"Days": 30, "StorageClass": "STANDARD_IA"},
        {"Days": 90, "StorageClass": "GLACIER"}
      ],
      "Expiration": {"Days": 365}
    }]
  }'
38.5 Presigned URL Generation Temporary access links
▶️ Presigned URL Script
#!/bin/bash
# generate-presigned.sh

BUCKET=$1
KEY=$2
EXPIRES=${3:-3600}  # 1 hour default

# Download URL
aws s3 presign "s3://$BUCKET/$KEY" --expires-in $EXPIRES

# Upload URL (PUT)
aws s3 presign "s3://$BUCKET/$KEY" \
  --expires-in $EXPIRES \
  --region us-east-1

# Use with curl:
# curl -X PUT -T file.txt "presigned-url"
38.6 Cross-Account Copy Scripts Multi-account S3 operations
▶️ Cross-Account Sync
#!/bin/bash
# cross-account-copy.sh

SOURCE_PROFILE="source-account"
DEST_PROFILE="dest-account"
SOURCE_BUCKET="source-bucket"
DEST_BUCKET="dest-bucket"

# Method 1: Using profiles
aws s3 sync s3://$SOURCE_BUCKET s3://$DEST_BUCKET \
  --source-region us-east-1 \
  --region us-west-2 \
  --acl bucket-owner-full-control

# Method 2: Download then upload
aws s3 sync s3://$SOURCE_BUCKET ./temp --profile $SOURCE_PROFILE
aws s3 sync ./temp s3://$DEST_BUCKET --profile $DEST_PROFILE
rm -rf ./temp
38.7 S3 Event-Triggered Automation Event notifications setup
▶️ S3 Event Configuration
#!/bin/bash
# setup-s3-events.sh

BUCKET=$1
LAMBDA_ARN=$2

aws s3api put-bucket-notification-configuration \
  --bucket $BUCKET \
  --notification-configuration '{
    "LambdaFunctionConfigurations": [{
      "LambdaFunctionArn": "'$LAMBDA_ARN'",
      "Events": ["s3:ObjectCreated:*"],
      "Filter": {
        "Key": {"FilterRules": [{"Name": "prefix", "Value": "uploads/"}]}
      }
    }]
  }'
38.8 Versioning and Replication Data protection automation
▶️ Version Management
#!/bin/bash
# manage-versions.sh

BUCKET=$1
PREFIX=$2

# List versions
aws s3api list-object-versions --bucket $BUCKET --prefix $PREFIX

# Delete old versions (keep latest 3)
aws s3api list-object-versions --bucket $BUCKET --prefix $PREFIX \
  --query 'Versions[3:].{Key:Key,VersionId:VersionId}' --output text | \
while read key version; do
  aws s3api delete-object --bucket $BUCKET --key "$key" --version-id "$version"
done

# Restore previous version
aws s3api copy-object --bucket $BUCKET \
  --copy-source "$BUCKET/$KEY?versionId=$OLD_VERSION" \
  --key "$KEY"

Chapter 39: IAM and Security Automation

Automating IAM and security configurations

Chapter Topics

39.1 User and Group Management Scripts
39.2 Role and Policy Automation
39.3 Access Key Rotation Scripts
39.4 Permission Auditing Scripts
39.5 MFA Enforcement Scripts
39.6 Credential Report Generation
39.7 IAM Policy Validation
39.8 Cross-Account Access Setup
39.1 User and Group Management IAM user automation
▶️ User Management Script
#!/bin/bash
# iam-user-setup.sh

USERNAME=$1
GROUP=$2

# Create user
aws iam create-user --user-name $USERNAME

# Add to group
aws iam add-user-to-group --user-name $USERNAME --group-name $GROUP

# Create access key
KEYS=$(aws iam create-access-key --user-name $USERNAME)
echo "Access Key ID: $(echo $KEYS | jq -r '.AccessKey.AccessKeyId')"
echo "Secret Key: $(echo $KEYS | jq -r '.AccessKey.SecretAccessKey')"

# Create login profile (console access)
TEMP_PASS=$(openssl rand -base64 12)
aws iam create-login-profile --user-name $USERNAME \
  --password "$TEMP_PASS" --password-reset-required
39.2 Role and Policy Automation Create and attach policies
▶️ Role Creation Script
#!/bin/bash
# create-role.sh

ROLE_NAME="LambdaExecutionRole"

# Trust policy
TRUST_POLICY='{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Principal": {"Service": "lambda.amazonaws.com"},
    "Action": "sts:AssumeRole"
  }]
}'

# Create role
aws iam create-role --role-name $ROLE_NAME \
  --assume-role-policy-document "$TRUST_POLICY"

# Attach managed policy
aws iam attach-role-policy --role-name $ROLE_NAME \
  --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole
39.3 Access Key Rotation Scripts Automated key rotation
▶️ Key Rotation Script
#!/bin/bash
# rotate-keys.sh

USERNAME=$1
MAX_AGE_DAYS=90

# Get old keys
OLD_KEYS=$(aws iam list-access-keys --user-name $USERNAME \
  --query "AccessKeyMetadata[?CreateDate<'$(date -d "-$MAX_AGE_DAYS days" -I)'].AccessKeyId" \
  --output text)

# Create new key
NEW_KEY=$(aws iam create-access-key --user-name $USERNAME)
echo "New key created: $(echo $NEW_KEY | jq -r '.AccessKey.AccessKeyId')"

# Disable old keys
for key in $OLD_KEYS; do
  aws iam update-access-key --user-name $USERNAME \
    --access-key-id $key --status Inactive
  echo "Disabled: $key"
done
39.4 Permission Auditing Scripts Audit IAM permissions
▶️ IAM Audit Script
#!/bin/bash
# iam-audit.sh

# Find users with admin access
echo "Users with AdministratorAccess:"
for user in $(aws iam list-users --query 'Users[].UserName' --output text); do
  if aws iam list-attached-user-policies --user-name $user \
    --query "AttachedPolicies[?PolicyArn=='arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AdministratorAccess']" \
    --output text | grep -q .; then
    echo "  - $user"
  fi
done

# Find unused users
echo -e "\nUsers not logged in 90+ days:"
aws iam list-users --query 'Users[?PasswordLastUsed!=`null`].[UserName,PasswordLastUsed]' \
  --output text | while read user date; do
    if [ $(date -d "$date" +%s) -lt $(date -d "-90 days" +%s) ]; then
      echo "  - $user (last: $date)"
    fi
done
39.5 MFA Enforcement Scripts Check and enforce MFA
▶️ MFA Check Script
#!/bin/bash
# check-mfa.sh

echo "Users without MFA enabled:"
for user in $(aws iam list-users --query 'Users[].UserName' --output text); do
  MFA=$(aws iam list-mfa-devices --user-name $user --query 'MFADevices[0]')
  if [ "$MFA" == "null" ]; then
    echo "  - $user"
  fi
done

# MFA enforcement policy
cat > mfa-required-policy.json <<'EOF'
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Sid": "DenyAllExceptMFA",
    "Effect": "Deny",
    "NotAction": ["iam:CreateVirtualMFADevice","iam:EnableMFADevice"],
    "Resource": "*",
    "Condition": {"BoolIfExists": {"aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent": "false"}}
  }]
}
EOF
39.6 Credential Report Generation Security compliance reports
▶️ Credential Report Script
#!/bin/bash
# credential-report.sh

# Generate report
aws iam generate-credential-report

# Wait for completion
while true; do
  STATE=$(aws iam generate-credential-report --query 'State' --output text)
  [ "$STATE" == "COMPLETE" ] && break
  sleep 2
done

# Download report
aws iam get-credential-report --query 'Content' --output text | \
  base64 -d > credential_report.csv

echo "Report saved to credential_report.csv"

# Parse for issues
echo "Users with old passwords (90+ days):"
awk -F, 'NR>1 && $5!="not_supported" && $5!="N/A" {print $1, $5}' credential_report.csv
39.7 IAM Policy Validation Validate policies before apply
▶️ Policy Validation
#!/bin/bash
# validate-policy.sh

POLICY_FILE=$1

# Validate policy syntax
aws accessanalyzer validate-policy \
  --policy-document file://$POLICY_FILE \
  --policy-type IDENTITY_POLICY

# Check for overly permissive
FINDINGS=$(aws accessanalyzer validate-policy \
  --policy-document file://$POLICY_FILE \
  --policy-type IDENTITY_POLICY \
  --query "findings[?findingType=='SECURITY_WARNING']")

if [ "$FINDINGS" != "[]" ]; then
  echo "Security warnings found:"
  echo "$FINDINGS" | jq '.[] | .issueCode'
fi
39.8 Cross-Account Access Setup Multi-account role assumption
▶️ Cross-Account Role
#!/bin/bash
# cross-account-setup.sh

TARGET_ACCOUNT="123456789012"
ROLE_NAME="CrossAccountAccess"

# Trust policy allowing source account
TRUST='{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Principal": {"AWS": "arn:aws:iam::'$SOURCE_ACCOUNT':root"},
    "Action": "sts:AssumeRole",
    "Condition": {"Bool": {"aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent": "true"}}
  }]
}'

# Assume role from source account
aws sts assume-role \
  --role-arn "arn:aws:iam::$TARGET_ACCOUNT:role/$ROLE_NAME" \
  --role-session-name "CrossAccountSession" \
  --serial-number "arn:aws:iam::$SOURCE_ACCOUNT:mfa/user" \
  --token-code 123456

Chapter 40: AWS Automation with Python (Boto3)

Python SDK for AWS automation

Chapter Topics

40.1 Boto3 Setup and Configuration
40.2 Session and Client Management
40.3 Resource vs Client Interface
40.4 Waiters and Polling
40.5 Pagination Handling
40.6 Error Handling and Retries
40.7 Multi-Region Automation
40.8 Cross-Account Automation
40.9 Custom Boto3 Utilities
40.1 Boto3 Setup and Configuration Python AWS SDK basics
🐍 Boto3 Architecture 
BOTO3 COMPONENTS:
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Your Python Code                    │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│    Session    │    Client    │    Resource            │
│  (credentials)│  (low-level) │  (high-level)          │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                    Botocore                           │
│           (HTTP, signing, serialization)              │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                    AWS APIs                           │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

# Install: pip install boto3
import boto3

# Default session uses ~/.aws/credentials
ec2 = boto3.client('ec2')
s3 = boto3.resource('s3')
40.2 Session and Client Management Managing AWS sessions
▶️ Session Configuration
import boto3

# Default session
session = boto3.Session()

# Named profile
session = boto3.Session(profile_name='prod')

# Explicit credentials
session = boto3.Session(
    aws_access_key_id='AKIA...',
    aws_secret_access_key='...',
    region_name='us-east-1'
)

# Create clients from session
ec2 = session.client('ec2')
s3 = session.client('s3')

# Get current identity
sts = session.client('sts')
identity = sts.get_caller_identity()
print(f"Account: {identity['Account']}")
40.3 Resource vs Client Interface Choosing the right interface
▶️ Client vs Resource
import boto3

# CLIENT (low-level, all services)
s3_client = boto3.client('s3')
response = s3_client.list_buckets()
buckets = response['Buckets']

# RESOURCE (high-level, OOP, limited services)
s3_resource = boto3.resource('s3')
for bucket in s3_resource.buckets.all():
    print(bucket.name)

# Resource object manipulation
bucket = s3_resource.Bucket('my-bucket')
for obj in bucket.objects.filter(Prefix='logs/'):
    print(obj.key)
    
# Download with resource
bucket.download_file('key', 'local_file')
40.4 Waiters and Polling Wait for resource states
▶️ Using Waiters
import boto3

ec2 = boto3.client('ec2')

# Start instance
ec2.start_instances(InstanceIds=['i-1234567890abcdef0'])

# Wait for running state
waiter = ec2.get_waiter('instance_running')
waiter.wait(
    InstanceIds=['i-1234567890abcdef0'],
    WaiterConfig={
        'Delay': 15,      # Seconds between checks
        'MaxAttempts': 40  # Max checks before timeout
    }
)
print("Instance is running")

# Available waiters
print(ec2.waiter_names)  # ['instance_running', 'instance_stopped', ...]
40.5 Pagination Handling Handle large result sets
▶️ Paginator Usage
import boto3

s3 = boto3.client('s3')

# Use paginator for large listings
paginator = s3.get_paginator('list_objects_v2')

all_keys = []
for page in paginator.paginate(Bucket='my-bucket', Prefix='data/'):
    if 'Contents' in page:
        for obj in page['Contents']:
            all_keys.append(obj['Key'])

# With JMESPath filtering
page_iterator = paginator.paginate(Bucket='my-bucket')
filtered = page_iterator.search("Contents[?Size > `1000000`].Key")
for key in filtered:
    print(key)
40.6 Error Handling and Retries Robust error handling
▶️ Exception Handling
import boto3
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError, BotoCoreError
from botocore.config import Config

# Configure retries
config = Config(
    retries={
        'max_attempts': 10,
        'mode': 'adaptive'  # or 'standard'
    }
)
s3 = boto3.client('s3', config=config)

# Handle specific errors
try:
    s3.head_object(Bucket='bucket', Key='key')
except ClientError as e:
    error_code = e.response['Error']['Code']
    if error_code == '404':
        print("Object not found")
    elif error_code == 'AccessDenied':
        print("Permission denied")
    else:
        raise
40.7 Multi-Region Automation Cross-region operations
▶️ Multi-Region Script
import boto3

def get_all_instances():
    """Get EC2 instances across all regions"""
    ec2_base = boto3.client('ec2')
    regions = [r['RegionName'] for r in ec2_base.describe_regions()['Regions']]
    
    all_instances = []
    for region in regions:
        ec2 = boto3.client('ec2', region_name=region)
        response = ec2.describe_instances()
        for res in response['Reservations']:
            for inst in res['Instances']:
                all_instances.append({
                    'region': region,
                    'id': inst['InstanceId'],
                    'state': inst['State']['Name']
                })
    return all_instances
40.8 Cross-Account Automation Assume roles in other accounts
▶️ Cross-Account Access
import boto3

def assume_role(account_id, role_name):
    """Assume role in another account"""
    sts = boto3.client('sts')
    
    response = sts.assume_role(
        RoleArn=f'arn:aws:iam::{account_id}:role/{role_name}',
        RoleSessionName='CrossAccountSession',
        DurationSeconds=3600
    )
    
    credentials = response['Credentials']
    
    # Create session with assumed role
    session = boto3.Session(
        aws_access_key_id=credentials['AccessKeyId'],
        aws_secret_access_key=credentials['SecretAccessKey'],
        aws_session_token=credentials['SessionToken']
    )
    return session

# Use assumed role
target_session = assume_role('123456789012', 'AdminRole')
ec2 = target_session.client('ec2')
40.9 Custom Boto3 Utilities Reusable helper functions
▶️ AWS Utility Module
# aws_utils.py
import boto3
from functools import lru_cache

@lru_cache(maxsize=10)
def get_client(service, region='us-east-1'):
    """Cached client creation"""
    return boto3.client(service, region_name=region)

def tag_to_dict(tags):
    """Convert AWS tags to dict"""
    return {t['Key']: t['Value'] for t in (tags or [])}

def dict_to_tags(d):
    """Convert dict to AWS tags"""
    return [{'Key': k, 'Value': v} for k, v in d.items()]

def find_instances_by_tag(tag_key, tag_value, region='us-east-1'):
    """Find EC2 instances by tag"""
    ec2 = get_client('ec2', region)
    response = ec2.describe_instances(
        Filters=[{'Name': f'tag:{tag_key}', 'Values': [tag_value]}]
    )
    return [i['InstanceId'] for r in response['Reservations'] 
            for i in r['Instances']]

Chapter 41: Lambda & Serverless Automation

Automating serverless applications

Chapter Topics

41.1 Lambda Function Deployment Scripts
41.2 Lambda Layers Automation
41.3 Event Source Mapping Scripts
41.4 Lambda as Automation Engine
41.5 Step Functions Orchestration
41.6 Scheduled Lambda Tasks (EventBridge)
41.7 SAM CLI Automation
41.8 Serverless Framework Scripts
41.1 Lambda Function Deployment Deploy Lambda with CLI
⚡ Serverless Architecture 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    LAMBDA EVENT-DRIVEN ARCHITECTURE                  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  EVENT SOURCES:                         LAMBDA                       │
│  ┌──────────────┐                  ┌──────────────┐                 │
│  │ API Gateway  │ ──────────────►  │              │                 │
│  ├──────────────┤                  │   Lambda     │                 │
│  │ S3 Events    │ ──────────────►  │   Function   │                 │
│  ├──────────────┤                  │              │  ┌────────────┐ │
│  │ SQS/SNS      │ ──────────────►  │  • Handler   │──► DynamoDB   │ │
│  ├──────────────┤                  │  • Runtime   │  ├────────────┤ │
│  │ EventBridge  │ ──────────────►  │  • Layers    │──► S3         │ │
│  ├──────────────┤                  │  • Memory    │  ├────────────┤ │
│  │ DynamoDB     │ ──────────────►  │  • Timeout   │──► RDS        │ │
│  │ Streams      │                  │              │  └────────────┘ │
│  └──────────────┘                  └──────────────┘                 │
│                                                                      │
│  DEPLOYMENT METHODS:                                                │
│  ┌─────────────┬───────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │ Method      │ Tool          │ Best For                     │    │
│  ├─────────────┼───────────────┼──────────────────────────────┤    │
│  │ CLI         │ aws lambda    │ Simple functions             │    │
│  │ SAM         │ sam deploy    │ AWS-native serverless        │    │
│  │ Serverless  │ sls deploy    │ Multi-cloud serverless       │    │
│  │ CDK         │ cdk deploy    │ Complex infrastructure       │    │
│  │ Terraform   │ terraform     │ Multi-provider IaC           │    │
│  └─────────────┴───────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Lambda Deploy Script
#!/bin/bash
# deploy-lambda.sh

FUNCTION_NAME=$1
RUNTIME="python3.11"
HANDLER="lambda_function.lambda_handler"
ROLE_ARN=$2

# Package code
zip -r function.zip . -x "*.git*"

# Create or update function
if aws lambda get-function --function-name $FUNCTION_NAME 2>/dev/null; then
  aws lambda update-function-code \
    --function-name $FUNCTION_NAME \
    --zip-file fileb://function.zip
else
  aws lambda create-function \
    --function-name $FUNCTION_NAME \
    --runtime $RUNTIME \
    --handler $HANDLER \
    --role $ROLE_ARN \
    --zip-file fileb://function.zip
fi

rm function.zip
41.2 Lambda Layers Automation Create and publish layers
▶️ Layer Creation
#!/bin/bash
# create-layer.sh

LAYER_NAME="my-python-deps"
RUNTIME="python3.11"

# Build layer (must use python/lib/python3.x/site-packages)
mkdir -p python/lib/python3.11/site-packages
pip install -r requirements.txt -t python/lib/python3.11/site-packages

zip -r layer.zip python

# Publish layer
LAYER_ARN=$(aws lambda publish-layer-version \
  --layer-name $LAYER_NAME \
  --zip-file fileb://layer.zip \
  --compatible-runtimes $RUNTIME \
  --query 'LayerVersionArn' --output text)

echo "Layer ARN: $LAYER_ARN"

# Add layer to function
aws lambda update-function-configuration \
  --function-name my-function \
  --layers $LAYER_ARN
41.3 Event Source Mapping Connect Lambda to event sources
▶️ Event Source Setup
#!/bin/bash
# setup-event-source.sh

FUNCTION_NAME=$1
SQS_ARN=$2

# Create SQS trigger
aws lambda create-event-source-mapping \
  --function-name $FUNCTION_NAME \
  --batch-size 10 \
  --event-source-arn $SQS_ARN

# DynamoDB Streams trigger
aws lambda create-event-source-mapping \
  --function-name $FUNCTION_NAME \
  --event-source-arn $DYNAMO_STREAM_ARN \
  --starting-position LATEST \
  --batch-size 100

# List mappings
aws lambda list-event-source-mappings \
  --function-name $FUNCTION_NAME
41.4 Lambda as Automation Engine Use Lambda for automation
▶️ Automation Lambda
# lambda_function.py - EC2 Snapshot Automation
import boto3
from datetime import datetime

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    ec2 = boto3.client('ec2')
    
    # Find volumes to backup
    volumes = ec2.describe_volumes(
        Filters=[{'Name': 'tag:Backup', 'Values': ['true']}]
    )['Volumes']
    
    for vol in volumes:
        snapshot = ec2.create_snapshot(
            VolumeId=vol['VolumeId'],
            Description=f"Automated backup {datetime.now().isoformat()}"
        )
        ec2.create_tags(
            Resources=[snapshot['SnapshotId']],
            Tags=[{'Key': 'AutomatedBackup', 'Value': 'true'}]
        )
    
    return {'snapshots_created': len(volumes)}
41.5 Step Functions Orchestration State machine automation
▶️ Step Function Definition
{
  "StartAt": "ProcessInput",
  "States": {
    "ProcessInput": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123:function:process",
      "Next": "CheckResult"
    },
    "CheckResult": {
      "Type": "Choice",
      "Choices": [{
        "Variable": "$.status",
        "StringEquals": "success",
        "Next": "NotifySuccess"
      }],
      "Default": "NotifyFailure"
    },
    "NotifySuccess": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::sns:publish",
      "End": true
    }
  }
}
41.6 Scheduled Lambda Tasks EventBridge scheduling
▶️ Schedule Lambda
#!/bin/bash
# schedule-lambda.sh

FUNCTION_NAME=$1
RULE_NAME="daily-${FUNCTION_NAME}"

# Create schedule rule (daily at 6 AM UTC)
aws events put-rule \
  --name $RULE_NAME \
  --schedule-expression "cron(0 6 * * ? *)"

# Add Lambda permission
aws lambda add-permission \
  --function-name $FUNCTION_NAME \
  --statement-id "${RULE_NAME}-invoke" \
  --action lambda:InvokeFunction \
  --principal events.amazonaws.com \
  --source-arn "arn:aws:events:us-east-1:123:rule/$RULE_NAME"

# Add target
aws events put-targets \
  --rule $RULE_NAME \
  --targets "Id=1,Arn=arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123:function:$FUNCTION_NAME"
41.7 SAM CLI Automation AWS SAM deployment
▶️ SAM Commands
#!/bin/bash
# sam-deploy.sh

# Build application
sam build

# Deploy with guided prompts
sam deploy --guided

# Deploy non-interactive
sam deploy \
  --stack-name my-app \
  --s3-bucket my-deployment-bucket \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --parameter-overrides Environment=prod

# Local testing
sam local invoke MyFunction --event events/test.json
sam local start-api
41.8 Serverless Framework Scripts Serverless deployment
▶️ serverless.yml
service: my-api

provider:
  name: aws
  runtime: python3.11
  stage: ${opt:stage, 'dev'}
  
functions:
  hello:
    handler: handler.hello
    events:
      - httpApi:
          path: /hello
          method: get

# Deploy: sls deploy --stage prod
# Remove: sls remove --stage prod

Chapter 42: AWS Networking Automation

Automating VPC and networking

Chapter Topics

42.1 VPC Creation and Management
42.2 Subnet and Route Table Automation
42.3 Security Group Scripts
42.4 NAT Gateway Management
42.5 VPC Peering Automation
42.6 Transit Gateway Scripts
42.7 Route 53 DNS Automation
42.8 CloudFront Distribution Management
42.1 VPC Creation and Management Create complete VPC
▶️ VPC Creation Script
#!/bin/bash
# create-vpc.sh

VPC_CIDR="10.0.0.0/16"
VPC_NAME="my-vpc"

# Create VPC
VPC_ID=$(aws ec2 create-vpc --cidr-block $VPC_CIDR \
  --query 'Vpc.VpcId' --output text)

# Enable DNS
aws ec2 modify-vpc-attribute --vpc-id $VPC_ID --enable-dns-hostnames
aws ec2 modify-vpc-attribute --vpc-id $VPC_ID --enable-dns-support

# Tag VPC
aws ec2 create-tags --resources $VPC_ID \
  --tags Key=Name,Value=$VPC_NAME

# Create Internet Gateway
IGW_ID=$(aws ec2 create-internet-gateway --query 'InternetGateway.InternetGatewayId' --output text)
aws ec2 attach-internet-gateway --internet-gateway-id $IGW_ID --vpc-id $VPC_ID

echo "VPC: $VPC_ID, IGW: $IGW_ID"
42.2 Subnet and Route Table Automation Network topology setup
▶️ Subnet Creation
#!/bin/bash
# create-subnets.sh

VPC_ID=$1
AZS=("us-east-1a" "us-east-1b")

# Create public subnets
for i in "${!AZS[@]}"; do
  SUBNET_ID=$(aws ec2 create-subnet \
    --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
    --cidr-block "10.0.$((i*10)).0/24" \
    --availability-zone ${AZS[$i]} \
    --query 'Subnet.SubnetId' --output text)
  
  aws ec2 modify-subnet-attribute \
    --subnet-id $SUBNET_ID \
    --map-public-ip-on-launch
  
  echo "Public subnet: $SUBNET_ID in ${AZS[$i]}"
done

# Create route table for public subnets
RT_ID=$(aws ec2 create-route-table --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --query 'RouteTable.RouteTableId' --output text)

aws ec2 create-route --route-table-id $RT_ID \
  --destination-cidr-block 0.0.0.0/0 --gateway-id $IGW_ID
42.3 Security Group Scripts Firewall rules automation
▶️ Security Group Setup
#!/bin/bash
# create-security-groups.sh

VPC_ID=$1

# Web tier SG
WEB_SG=$(aws ec2 create-security-group \
  --group-name web-tier \
  --description "Web tier security group" \
  --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --query 'GroupId' --output text)

aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress --group-id $WEB_SG \
  --ip-permissions '[
    {"IpProtocol":"tcp","FromPort":80,"ToPort":80,"IpRanges":[{"CidrIp":"0.0.0.0/0"}]},
    {"IpProtocol":"tcp","FromPort":443,"ToPort":443,"IpRanges":[{"CidrIp":"0.0.0.0/0"}]}
  ]'

# App tier SG (only from web tier)
APP_SG=$(aws ec2 create-security-group \
  --group-name app-tier --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --query 'GroupId' --output text)

aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress --group-id $APP_SG \
  --protocol tcp --port 8080 --source-group $WEB_SG
42.4 NAT Gateway Management Private subnet internet access
▶️ NAT Gateway Script
#!/bin/bash
# create-nat.sh

PUBLIC_SUBNET=$1
PRIVATE_RT=$2

# Allocate Elastic IP
EIP_ALLOC=$(aws ec2 allocate-address --domain vpc \
  --query 'AllocationId' --output text)

# Create NAT Gateway
NAT_ID=$(aws ec2 create-nat-gateway \
  --subnet-id $PUBLIC_SUBNET \
  --allocation-id $EIP_ALLOC \
  --query 'NatGateway.NatGatewayId' --output text)

# Wait for NAT Gateway
aws ec2 wait nat-gateway-available --nat-gateway-ids $NAT_ID

# Add route to private route table
aws ec2 create-route --route-table-id $PRIVATE_RT \
  --destination-cidr-block 0.0.0.0/0 \
  --nat-gateway-id $NAT_ID
42.5 VPC Peering Automation Connect VPCs
▶️ VPC Peering Script
#!/bin/bash
# vpc-peering.sh

VPC1=$1
VPC2=$2

# Create peering connection
PEER_ID=$(aws ec2 create-vpc-peering-connection \
  --vpc-id $VPC1 \
  --peer-vpc-id $VPC2 \
  --query 'VpcPeeringConnection.VpcPeeringConnectionId' --output text)

# Accept peering (same account)
aws ec2 accept-vpc-peering-connection \
  --vpc-peering-connection-id $PEER_ID

# Add routes (both directions)
aws ec2 create-route --route-table-id $RT1 \
  --destination-cidr-block $VPC2_CIDR \
  --vpc-peering-connection-id $PEER_ID
42.6 Transit Gateway Scripts Hub-spoke networking
▶️ Transit Gateway Setup
#!/bin/bash
# transit-gateway.sh

# Create Transit Gateway
TGW_ID=$(aws ec2 create-transit-gateway \
  --description "Central hub" \
  --options "AmazonSideAsn=64512,AutoAcceptSharedAttachments=enable" \
  --query 'TransitGateway.TransitGatewayId' --output text)

# Attach VPC
aws ec2 create-transit-gateway-vpc-attachment \
  --transit-gateway-id $TGW_ID \
  --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
  --subnet-ids $SUBNET_ID

# Add route to TGW
aws ec2 create-route --route-table-id $RT_ID \
  --destination-cidr-block 10.0.0.0/8 \
  --transit-gateway-id $TGW_ID
42.7 Route 53 DNS Automation DNS record management
▶️ Route 53 Script
#!/bin/bash
# route53-update.sh

HOSTED_ZONE_ID=$1
RECORD_NAME=$2
RECORD_VALUE=$3

aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
  --hosted-zone-id $HOSTED_ZONE_ID \
  --change-batch '{
    "Changes": [{
      "Action": "UPSERT",
      "ResourceRecordSet": {
        "Name": "'$RECORD_NAME'",
        "Type": "A",
        "TTL": 300,
        "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "'$RECORD_VALUE'"}]
      }
    }]
  }'

# Create alias to ALB
# "AliasTarget": {"HostedZoneId": "Z...", "DNSName": "alb.elb.amazonaws.com"}
42.8 CloudFront Distribution Management CDN automation
▶️ CloudFront Invalidation
#!/bin/bash
# cloudfront-invalidate.sh

DIST_ID=$1
PATHS=${2:-"/*"}

# Create invalidation
INVALIDATION=$(aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
  --distribution-id $DIST_ID \
  --paths "$PATHS" \
  --query 'Invalidation.Id' --output text)

echo "Invalidation: $INVALIDATION"

# Wait for completion
aws cloudfront wait invalidation-completed \
  --distribution-id $DIST_ID \
  --id $INVALIDATION

echo "Invalidation complete"

Chapter 43: AWS Database Automation

Automating database operations

Chapter Topics

43.1 RDS Instance Management
43.2 Snapshot and Backup Automation
43.3 Parameter Group Scripts
43.4 Read Replica Automation
43.5 DynamoDB Table Management
43.6 ElastiCache Automation
43.7 Database Migration Scripts
43.1 RDS Instance Management Create and manage RDS
▶️ RDS Creation Script
#!/bin/bash
# create-rds.sh

DB_INSTANCE="my-postgres"
DB_NAME="mydb"

aws rds create-db-instance \
  --db-instance-identifier $DB_INSTANCE \
  --db-instance-class db.t3.medium \
  --engine postgres \
  --engine-version 15 \
  --master-username admin \
  --master-user-password "$DB_PASSWORD" \
  --allocated-storage 100 \
  --storage-type gp3 \
  --vpc-security-group-ids $SG_ID \
  --db-subnet-group-name $SUBNET_GROUP \
  --backup-retention-period 7 \
  --multi-az \
  --storage-encrypted

# Wait for available
aws rds wait db-instance-available --db-instance-identifier $DB_INSTANCE
43.2 Snapshot and Backup Automation RDS backup scripts
▶️ RDS Snapshot Script
#!/bin/bash
# rds-snapshot.sh

DB_INSTANCE=$1
DATE=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)
SNAPSHOT_ID="${DB_INSTANCE}-${DATE}"

# Create snapshot
aws rds create-db-snapshot \
  --db-instance-identifier $DB_INSTANCE \
  --db-snapshot-identifier $SNAPSHOT_ID

# Wait for completion
aws rds wait db-snapshot-available \
  --db-snapshot-identifier $SNAPSHOT_ID

# Clean old snapshots (keep last 7)
aws rds describe-db-snapshots \
  --db-instance-identifier $DB_INSTANCE \
  --snapshot-type manual \
  --query 'DBSnapshots | sort_by(@, &SnapshotCreateTime)[:-7].DBSnapshotIdentifier' \
  --output text | xargs -I {} aws rds delete-db-snapshot \
    --db-snapshot-identifier {}
43.3 Parameter Group Scripts Database configuration
▶️ Parameter Group Setup
#!/bin/bash
# rds-params.sh

PG_NAME="custom-postgres15"

# Create parameter group
aws rds create-db-parameter-group \
  --db-parameter-group-name $PG_NAME \
  --db-parameter-group-family postgres15 \
  --description "Custom PostgreSQL parameters"

# Modify parameters
aws rds modify-db-parameter-group \
  --db-parameter-group-name $PG_NAME \
  --parameters "[
    {\"ParameterName\":\"max_connections\",\"ParameterValue\":\"200\",\"ApplyMethod\":\"pending-reboot\"},
    {\"ParameterName\":\"shared_buffers\",\"ParameterValue\":\"{DBInstanceClassMemory/4}\",\"ApplyMethod\":\"pending-reboot\"},
    {\"ParameterName\":\"log_statement\",\"ParameterValue\":\"ddl\",\"ApplyMethod\":\"immediate\"}
  ]"
43.4 Read Replica Automation Scale read workloads
▶️ Create Read Replica
#!/bin/bash
# create-replica.sh

SOURCE_DB=$1
REPLICA_ID="${SOURCE_DB}-replica"

# Create replica
aws rds create-db-instance-read-replica \
  --db-instance-identifier $REPLICA_ID \
  --source-db-instance-identifier $SOURCE_DB \
  --db-instance-class db.t3.medium \
  --availability-zone us-east-1b

# Cross-region replica
aws rds create-db-instance-read-replica \
  --db-instance-identifier $REPLICA_ID \
  --source-db-instance-identifier $SOURCE_DB \
  --source-region us-east-1 \
  --region us-west-2
43.5 DynamoDB Table Management NoSQL automation
▶️ DynamoDB Script
#!/bin/bash
# dynamodb-table.sh

TABLE_NAME=$1

# Create table with on-demand capacity
aws dynamodb create-table \
  --table-name $TABLE_NAME \
  --attribute-definitions \
    AttributeName=pk,AttributeType=S \
    AttributeName=sk,AttributeType=S \
  --key-schema \
    AttributeName=pk,KeyType=HASH \
    AttributeName=sk,KeyType=RANGE \
  --billing-mode PAY_PER_REQUEST

# Enable TTL
aws dynamodb update-time-to-live \
  --table-name $TABLE_NAME \
  --time-to-live-specification "Enabled=true,AttributeName=ttl"

# Enable Point-in-time recovery
aws dynamodb update-continuous-backups \
  --table-name $TABLE_NAME \
  --point-in-time-recovery-specification PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled=true
43.6 ElastiCache Automation Redis/Memcached management
▶️ ElastiCache Script
#!/bin/bash
# create-elasticache.sh

CLUSTER_ID="my-redis"
SUBNET_GROUP="cache-subnets"

# Create Redis cluster
aws elasticache create-cache-cluster \
  --cache-cluster-id $CLUSTER_ID \
  --cache-node-type cache.t3.medium \
  --engine redis \
  --engine-version 7.0 \
  --num-cache-nodes 1 \
  --cache-subnet-group-name $SUBNET_GROUP \
  --security-group-ids $SG_ID

# Create replication group for HA
aws elasticache create-replication-group \
  --replication-group-id my-redis-ha \
  --replication-group-description "Redis HA cluster" \
  --num-cache-clusters 3 \
  --automatic-failover-enabled
43.7 Database Migration Scripts DMS automation
▶️ DMS Migration
#!/bin/bash
# start-dms-migration.sh

TASK_ARN=$1

# Start migration task
aws dms start-replication-task \
  --replication-task-arn $TASK_ARN \
  --start-replication-task-type start-replication

# Monitor progress
while true; do
  STATUS=$(aws dms describe-replication-tasks \
    --filters Name=replication-task-arn,Values=$TASK_ARN \
    --query 'ReplicationTasks[0].Status' --output text)
  
  echo "Status: $STATUS"
  [ "$STATUS" == "stopped" ] && break
  sleep 30
done

Chapter 44: AWS Container Services Automation

Automating ECS, EKS, and ECR

Chapter Topics

44.1 ECS Cluster Management
44.2 Task Definition Automation

44.4 EKS Cluster Automation
44.5 ECR Repository Management

44.1 ECS Cluster Management Create and manage ECS clusters
▶️ ECS Cluster Script
#!/bin/bash
# ecs-cluster.sh

CLUSTER_NAME=$1

# Create Fargate cluster
aws ecs create-cluster \
  --cluster-name $CLUSTER_NAME \
  --capacity-providers FARGATE FARGATE_SPOT \
  --default-capacity-provider-strategy \
    capacityProvider=FARGATE,weight=1 \
    capacityProvider=FARGATE_SPOT,weight=3 \
  --settings name=containerInsights,value=enabled

# List services in cluster
aws ecs list-services --cluster $CLUSTER_NAME

# Get cluster status
aws ecs describe-clusters --clusters $CLUSTER_NAME \
  --include ATTACHMENTS SETTINGS STATISTICS
44.2 Task Definition Automation Register task definitions
▶️ Task Definition
#!/bin/bash
# register-task.sh

cat > task-def.json <<EOF
{
  "family": "webapp",
  "networkMode": "awsvpc",
  "requiresCompatibilities": ["FARGATE"],
  "cpu": "256",
  "memory": "512",
  "executionRoleArn": "$EXEC_ROLE_ARN",
  "containerDefinitions": [{
    "name": "app",
    "image": "$ECR_IMAGE",
    "portMappings": [{"containerPort": 8080}],
    "logConfiguration": {
      "logDriver": "awslogs",
      "options": {
        "awslogs-group": "/ecs/webapp",
        "awslogs-region": "us-east-1",
        "awslogs-stream-prefix": "app"
      }
    }
  }]
}
EOF

aws ecs register-task-definition --cli-input-json file://task-def.json
44.3 ECS Service Scripts Deploy and update services
▶️ ECS Service Deploy
#!/bin/bash
# ecs-service.sh

CLUSTER=$1
SERVICE=$2
TASK_DEF=$3

# Create service
aws ecs create-service \
  --cluster $CLUSTER \
  --service-name $SERVICE \
  --task-definition $TASK_DEF \
  --desired-count 2 \
  --launch-type FARGATE \
  --network-configuration "awsvpcConfiguration={subnets=[$SUBNETS],securityGroups=[$SG],assignPublicIp=ENABLED}" \
  --load-balancers "targetGroupArn=$TG_ARN,containerName=app,containerPort=8080"

# Update service (force new deployment)
aws ecs update-service \
  --cluster $CLUSTER \
  --service $SERVICE \
  --task-definition $TASK_DEF \
  --force-new-deployment
44.4 EKS Cluster Automation Managed Kubernetes
▶️ EKS with eksctl
#!/bin/bash
# eks-cluster.sh

CLUSTER_NAME="my-eks"

# Create cluster with eksctl
eksctl create cluster \
  --name $CLUSTER_NAME \
  --region us-east-1 \
  --version 1.28 \
  --nodegroup-name workers \
  --node-type t3.medium \
  --nodes 3 \
  --nodes-min 2 \
  --nodes-max 5 \
  --managed

# Or with AWS CLI
aws eks create-cluster \
  --name $CLUSTER_NAME \
  --role-arn $CLUSTER_ROLE_ARN \
  --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=$SUBNETS,securityGroupIds=$SG

# Update kubeconfig
aws eks update-kubeconfig --name $CLUSTER_NAME
44.5 ECR Repository Management Container registry automation
▶️ ECR Script
#!/bin/bash
# ecr-push.sh

REPO_NAME=$1
REGION="us-east-1"
ACCOUNT_ID=$(aws sts get-caller-identity --query Account --output text)
ECR_URI="$ACCOUNT_ID.dkr.ecr.$REGION.amazonaws.com"

# Create repository if not exists
aws ecr create-repository --repository-name $REPO_NAME 2>/dev/null

# Login to ECR
aws ecr get-login-password --region $REGION | \
  docker login --username AWS --password-stdin $ECR_URI

# Build, tag, and push
docker build -t $REPO_NAME .
docker tag $REPO_NAME:latest $ECR_URI/$REPO_NAME:latest
docker push $ECR_URI/$REPO_NAME:latest

# Enable image scanning
aws ecr put-image-scanning-configuration \
  --repository-name $REPO_NAME \
  --image-scanning-configuration scanOnPush=true
44.6 Fargate Task Automation Serverless containers
▶️ Run Fargate Task
#!/bin/bash
# run-fargate-task.sh

CLUSTER=$1
TASK_DEF=$2

# Run one-off task
TASK_ARN=$(aws ecs run-task \
  --cluster $CLUSTER \
  --task-definition $TASK_DEF \
  --launch-type FARGATE \
  --network-configuration "awsvpcConfiguration={subnets=[$SUBNETS],securityGroups=[$SG],assignPublicIp=ENABLED}" \
  --query 'tasks[0].taskArn' --output text)

echo "Started task: $TASK_ARN"

# Wait for completion
aws ecs wait tasks-stopped --cluster $CLUSTER --tasks $TASK_ARN

# Get exit code
aws ecs describe-tasks --cluster $CLUSTER --tasks $TASK_ARN \
  --query 'tasks[0].containers[0].exitCode'
44.7 Container Insights Setup Container observability
▶️ Enable Container Insights
#!/bin/bash
# container-insights.sh

CLUSTER=$1

# Enable for ECS
aws ecs update-cluster-settings \
  --cluster $CLUSTER \
  --settings name=containerInsights,value=enabled

# For EKS - install CloudWatch agent
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aws-samples/amazon-cloudwatch-container-insights/latest/k8s-deployment-manifest-templates/deployment-mode/daemonset/container-insights-monitoring/quickstart/cwagent-fluentd-quickstart.yaml

# Query Container Insights metrics
aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
  --namespace ECS/ContainerInsights \
  --metric-name CpuUtilized \
  --dimensions Name=ClusterName,Value=$CLUSTER \
  --start-time $(date -u -d '1 hour ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
  --end-time $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
  --period 300 --statistics Average

Automated metrics, logging, alerting, and distributed tracing.

Chapter 45: Metrics and Monitoring Automation

Automating metric collection and monitoring setup

Chapter Topics

45.1 CloudWatch Metrics Scripts
45.2 Custom Metric Publishing
45.3 Prometheus Configuration Automation
45.4 Grafana Dashboard as Code
45.5 Datadog API Automation
45.6 Resource Monitoring Scripts
