45.1 CloudWatch Metrics Scripts Query and export metrics
▶️ CloudWatch Metrics
#!/bin/bash
# cloudwatch-metrics.sh

# Get EC2 CPU utilization
aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
  --namespace AWS/EC2 \
  --metric-name CPUUtilization \
  --dimensions Name=InstanceId,Value=i-1234567890abcdef0 \
  --start-time $(date -u -d '1 hour ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
  --end-time $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
  --period 300 \
  --statistics Average Maximum

# List available metrics
aws cloudwatch list-metrics \
  --namespace AWS/EC2 \
  --metric-name CPUUtilization

# Get multiple metrics with metric-data-queries
aws cloudwatch get-metric-data \
  --metric-data-queries file://queries.json \
  --start-time ... --end-time ...
45.2 Custom Metric Publishing Publish application metrics
▶️ Publish Custom Metrics
import boto3
from datetime import datetime

cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')

def publish_metric(metric_name, value, unit='Count', dimensions=None):
    """Publish custom metric to CloudWatch"""
    metric_data = {
        'MetricName': metric_name,
        'Value': value,
        'Unit': unit,
        'Timestamp': datetime.utcnow()
    }
    
    if dimensions:
        metric_data['Dimensions'] = [
            {'Name': k, 'Value': v} for k, v in dimensions.items()
        ]
    
    cloudwatch.put_metric_data(
        Namespace='MyApp/Metrics',
        MetricData=[metric_data]
    )

# Usage
publish_metric('OrdersProcessed', 42, 'Count', {'Environment': 'prod'})
publish_metric('ProcessingTime', 1.23, 'Seconds')
45.3 Prometheus Configuration Prometheus automation
▶️ Prometheus Config Generation
import yaml

def generate_prometheus_config(targets):
    """Generate prometheus.yml dynamically"""
    config = {
        'global': {
            'scrape_interval': '15s',
            'evaluation_interval': '15s'
        },
        'scrape_configs': [{
            'job_name': 'kubernetes-pods',
            'kubernetes_sd_configs': [{'role': 'pod'}],
            'relabel_configs': [
                {'source_labels': ['__meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_scrape'],
                 'action': 'keep', 'regex': 'true'}
            ]
        }]
    }
    
    # Add static targets
    for name, hosts in targets.items():
        config['scrape_configs'].append({
            'job_name': name,
            'static_configs': [{'targets': hosts}]
        })
    
    return yaml.dump(config)
45.4 Grafana Dashboard as Code Automated dashboard provisioning
▶️ Grafana Dashboard JSON
import requests
import json

def create_grafana_dashboard(api_url, api_key, dashboard):
    """Create or update Grafana dashboard"""
    headers = {
        'Authorization': f'Bearer {api_key}',
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    }
    
    payload = {
        'dashboard': dashboard,
        'overwrite': True,
        'message': 'Automated update'
    }
    
    response = requests.post(
        f'{api_url}/api/dashboards/db',
        headers=headers,
        json=payload
    )
    return response.json()

# Dashboard definition
dashboard = {
    'title': 'Application Metrics',
    'panels': [{
        'type': 'graph',
        'title': 'Request Rate',
        'targets': [{'expr': 'rate(http_requests_total[5m])'}]
    }]
}
45.5 Datadog API Automation Datadog metric management
▶️ Datadog API Script
from datadog_api_client import ApiClient, Configuration
from datadog_api_client.v1.api.metrics_api import MetricsApi
from datadog_api_client.v1.model.metrics_payload import MetricsPayload
from datadog_api_client.v1.model.series import Series
from datadog_api_client.v1.model.point import Point
import time

configuration = Configuration()

def submit_metric(metric_name, value, tags=None):
    """Submit metric to Datadog"""
    with ApiClient(configuration) as api_client:
        api = MetricsApi(api_client)
        
        body = MetricsPayload(
            series=[Series(
                metric=metric_name,
                type="gauge",
                points=[Point([time.time(), value])],
                tags=tags or []
            )]
        )
        api.submit_metrics(body=body)

submit_metric("app.request.count", 100, ["env:prod", "service:api"])
45.6 Resource Monitoring Scripts System resource tracking
▶️ System Monitor Script
#!/bin/bash
# system-monitor.sh

while true; do
  # CPU usage
  CPU=$(top -bn1 | grep "Cpu(s)" | awk '{print $2}')
  
  # Memory usage
  MEM=$(free | awk '/Mem/{printf "%.1f", $3/$2*100}')
  
  # Disk usage
  DISK=$(df -h / | awk 'NR==2{print $5}' | tr -d '%')
  
  # Publish to CloudWatch
  aws cloudwatch put-metric-data \
    --namespace "Custom/System" \
    --metric-data \
      "MetricName=CPUUsage,Value=$CPU,Unit=Percent" \
      "MetricName=MemoryUsage,Value=$MEM,Unit=Percent" \
      "MetricName=DiskUsage,Value=$DISK,Unit=Percent"
  
  sleep 60
done
45.7 Performance Baseline Automation Establish performance baselines
▶️ Baseline Script
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import statistics

def calculate_baseline(metric_name, dimension, days=7):
    """Calculate baseline metrics over a period"""
    cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')
    
    response = cloudwatch.get_metric_statistics(
        Namespace='AWS/EC2',
        MetricName=metric_name,
        Dimensions=[dimension],
        StartTime=datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=days),
        EndTime=datetime.utcnow(),
        Period=3600,
        Statistics=['Average']
    )
    
    values = [dp['Average'] for dp in response['Datapoints']]
    
    return {
        'mean': statistics.mean(values),
        'median': statistics.median(values),
        'stdev': statistics.stdev(values),
        'p95': sorted(values)[int(len(values) * 0.95)]
    }

baseline = calculate_baseline('CPUUtilization', 
    {'Name': 'InstanceId', 'Value': 'i-123'})

Chapter 46: Logging Automation

Automating log management and analysis

Chapter Topics

46.1 CloudWatch Logs Management
46.2 Log Group and Retention Automation
46.3 Log Aggregation Scripts
46.4 ELK Stack Configuration
46.5 Log Parsing and Analysis Scripts
46.6 Structured Logging Setup
46.7 Log Rotation Automation
46.1 CloudWatch Logs Management Query and manage logs
▶️ CloudWatch Logs Queries
#!/bin/bash
# cloudwatch-logs.sh

LOG_GROUP="/aws/lambda/my-function"

# Start query
QUERY_ID=$(aws logs start-query \
  --log-group-name $LOG_GROUP \
  --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s) \
  --end-time $(date +%s) \
  --query-string 'fields @timestamp, @message | filter @message like /ERROR/ | sort @timestamp desc | limit 100' \
  --query 'queryId' --output text)

# Wait and get results
sleep 5
aws logs get-query-results --query-id $QUERY_ID

# Tail logs in real-time
aws logs tail $LOG_GROUP --follow --filter-pattern "ERROR"
46.2 Log Group and Retention Manage log lifecycle
▶️ Log Group Management
#!/bin/bash
# manage-log-groups.sh

# Create log group
aws logs create-log-group --log-group-name /app/myservice

# Set retention (days: 1,3,5,7,14,30,60,90,120,150,180,365,400,545,731,1827,3653)
aws logs put-retention-policy \
  --log-group-name /app/myservice \
  --retention-in-days 30

# Set retention for all groups without policy
for group in $(aws logs describe-log-groups \
  --query 'logGroups[?!retentionInDays].logGroupName' --output text); do
  aws logs put-retention-policy --log-group-name "$group" --retention-in-days 90
  echo "Set 90-day retention for: $group"
done
46.3 Log Aggregation Scripts Centralized logging
▶️ Log Export to S3
#!/bin/bash
# export-logs.sh

LOG_GROUP=$1
BUCKET="my-logs-archive"
DATE=$(date -d 'yesterday' +%Y/%m/%d)

# Create export task
TASK_ID=$(aws logs create-export-task \
  --log-group-name $LOG_GROUP \
  --from $(date -d 'yesterday 00:00' +%s%3N) \
  --to $(date -d 'today 00:00' +%s%3N) \
  --destination $BUCKET \
  --destination-prefix "logs/$LOG_GROUP/$DATE" \
  --query 'taskId' --output text)

# Wait for completion
while [ "$(aws logs describe-export-tasks --task-id $TASK_ID \
  --query 'exportTasks[0].status.code' --output text)" != "COMPLETED" ]; do
  sleep 10
done
46.4 ELK Stack Configuration Elasticsearch setup
▶️ Logstash Config
# logstash.conf
input {
  beats {
    port => 5044
  }
}

filter {
  grok {
    match => { "message" => "%{TIMESTAMP_ISO8601:timestamp} %{LOGLEVEL:level} %{GREEDYDATA:msg}" }
  }
  date {
    match => ["timestamp", "ISO8601"]
  }
}

output {
  elasticsearch {
    hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
    index => "logs-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
  }
}
46.5 Log Parsing and Analysis Extract insights from logs
▶️ Log Parser Script
import re
from collections import Counter

def parse_apache_logs(log_file):
    """Parse Apache access logs"""
    pattern = r'(\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+).*\[(.+)\] "(\w+) (.+) HTTP.*" (\d+) (\d+)'
    
    stats = {
        'ips': Counter(),
        'status_codes': Counter(),
        'endpoints': Counter()
    }
    
    with open(log_file) as f:
        for line in f:
            match = re.search(pattern, line)
            if match:
                ip, date, method, path, status, size = match.groups()
                stats['ips'][ip] += 1
                stats['status_codes'][status] += 1
                stats['endpoints'][path] += 1
    
    return stats

# Top 10 IPs, error codes, etc.
46.6 Structured Logging Setup JSON logging configuration
▶️ Structured Logger
import json
import logging
from datetime import datetime

class JSONFormatter(logging.Formatter):
    def format(self, record):
        log_record = {
            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            'level': record.levelname,
            'message': record.getMessage(),
            'logger': record.name,
            'module': record.module,
            'function': record.funcName,
            'line': record.lineno
        }
        if hasattr(record, 'extra'):
            log_record.update(record.extra)
        return json.dumps(log_record)

# Setup
handler = logging.StreamHandler()
handler.setFormatter(JSONFormatter())
logger = logging.getLogger('app')
logger.addHandler(handler)

logger.info("User logged in", extra={'user_id': 123, 'ip': '10.0.0.1'})
46.7 Log Rotation Automation Automated log management
▶️ logrotate Config
# /etc/logrotate.d/myapp
/var/log/myapp/*.log {
    daily
    rotate 14
    compress
    delaycompress
    missingok
    notifempty
    create 0640 www-data adm
    sharedscripts
    postrotate
        systemctl reload myapp > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
    endscript
}

# Force rotation
logrotate -f /etc/logrotate.d/myapp

# Test config (debug)
logrotate -d /etc/logrotate.d/myapp

47.1 CloudWatch Alarms Automation Create and manage alarms
▶️ Create CloudWatch Alarm
#!/bin/bash
# create-alarm.sh

INSTANCE_ID=$1
SNS_TOPIC_ARN=$2

aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
  --alarm-name "high-cpu-$INSTANCE_ID" \
  --alarm-description "CPU utilization > 80%" \
  --metric-name CPUUtilization \
  --namespace AWS/EC2 \
  --statistic Average \
  --period 300 \
  --threshold 80 \
  --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
  --evaluation-periods 2 \
  --dimensions Name=InstanceId,Value=$INSTANCE_ID \
  --alarm-actions $SNS_TOPIC_ARN \
  --ok-actions $SNS_TOPIC_ARN \
  --treat-missing-data notBreaching
47.2 SNS Topic and Subscription Notification channels
▶️ SNS Setup Script
#!/bin/bash
# setup-sns.sh

TOPIC_NAME="alerts"

# Create topic
TOPIC_ARN=$(aws sns create-topic --name $TOPIC_NAME \
  --query 'TopicArn' --output text)

# Email subscription
aws sns subscribe \
  --topic-arn $TOPIC_ARN \
  --protocol email \
  --notification-endpoint alerts@example.com

# Lambda subscription
aws sns subscribe \
  --topic-arn $TOPIC_ARN \
  --protocol lambda \
  --notification-endpoint $LAMBDA_ARN

# Publish test message
aws sns publish \
  --topic-arn $TOPIC_ARN \
  --message "Test alert" \
  --subject "Alert: Test"
47.3 PagerDuty Integration On-call alerting
▶️ PagerDuty Alert Script
import requests
import json

PAGERDUTY_KEY = "your-integration-key"

def trigger_pagerduty(summary, severity="critical", details=None):
    """Trigger PagerDuty incident"""
    payload = {
        "routing_key": PAGERDUTY_KEY,
        "event_action": "trigger",
        "payload": {
            "summary": summary,
            "severity": severity,  # critical, error, warning, info
            "source": "monitoring-script",
            "custom_details": details or {}
        }
    }
    
    response = requests.post(
        "https://events.pagerduty.com/v2/enqueue",
        json=payload
    )
    return response.json()

# Trigger alert
trigger_pagerduty("Database connection failed", "critical", 
    {"host": "db-prod", "error": "Connection timeout"})
47.4 Slack/Teams Notifications Chat integrations
▶️ Slack Notification
import requests
import json

def send_slack_alert(webhook_url, message, severity="warning"):
    """Send alert to Slack channel"""
    colors = {"critical": "danger", "warning": "warning", "info": "good"}
    
    payload = {
        "attachments": [{
            "color": colors.get(severity, "warning"),
            "title": f"🚨 Alert: {severity.upper()}",
            "text": message,
            "fields": [
                {"title": "Environment", "value": "Production", "short": True},
                {"title": "Time", "value": "2024-01-15 10:30:00", "short": True}
            ],
            "footer": "Monitoring System"
        }]
    }
    
    requests.post(webhook_url, json=payload)

send_slack_alert(WEBHOOK_URL, "High CPU usage on web-server-01")
47.5 Alert Escalation Scripts Multi-tier alerting
▶️ Escalation Script
import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

class AlertEscalation:
    def __init__(self):
        self.levels = [
            {"delay": 0, "channel": "slack", "team": "on-call"},
            {"delay": 15, "channel": "pagerduty", "team": "on-call"},
            {"delay": 30, "channel": "phone", "team": "manager"},
        ]
    
    def escalate(self, alert, start_time):
        """Escalate alert through tiers"""
        elapsed = (datetime.now() - start_time).total_seconds() / 60
        
        for level in self.levels:
            if elapsed >= level["delay"]:
                if not alert.get(f"notified_{level['channel']}"):
                    self.notify(level["channel"], level["team"], alert)
                    alert[f"notified_{level['channel']}"] = True
    
    def notify(self, channel, team, alert):
        print(f"Notifying {team} via {channel}")
47.6 On-Call Rotation Schedule management
▶️ On-Call Schedule
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def get_oncall_engineer(schedule, timestamp=None):
    """Determine on-call engineer from rotation"""
    timestamp = timestamp or datetime.now()
    
    # Weekly rotation example
    rotation = [
        {"name": "Alice", "email": "alice@example.com"},
        {"name": "Bob", "email": "bob@example.com"},
        {"name": "Carol", "email": "carol@example.com"},
    ]
    
    # Calculate week number from start date
    start_date = datetime(2024, 1, 1)
    weeks = (timestamp - start_date).days // 7
    
    engineer_index = weeks % len(rotation)
    return rotation[engineer_index]

oncall = get_oncall_engineer(schedule)
print(f"On-call: {oncall['name']} ({oncall['email']})")
47.7 Incident Response Automation Automated incident handling
▶️ Incident Handler
class IncidentHandler:
    def __init__(self):
        self.runbooks = {
            "high_cpu": self.handle_high_cpu,
            "disk_full": self.handle_disk_full,
            "service_down": self.handle_service_down
        }
    
    def handle(self, incident_type, context):
        """Route to appropriate runbook"""
        handler = self.runbooks.get(incident_type)
        if handler:
            return handler(context)
        return self.default_handler(context)
    
    def handle_high_cpu(self, ctx):
        # 1. Capture diagnostics
        # 2. Scale if autoscaling
        # 3. Notify team
        return {"action": "scaled_out", "new_instances": 2}
    
    def handle_disk_full(self, ctx):
        # 1. Clean tmp files
        # 2. Archive old logs
        # 3. Expand volume if needed
        return {"action": "cleaned_disk", "freed_gb": 10}

Chapter 48: Distributed Tracing Automation

Automating trace collection and analysis

Chapter Topics

48.1 X-Ray Configuration Scripts
48.2 Jaeger Setup Automation
48.3 Trace Sampling Configuration
48.4 Service Map Generation

48.1 X-Ray Configuration Scripts AWS X-Ray tracing
▶️ X-Ray Setup
# X-Ray SDK setup for Python
from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder
from aws_xray_sdk.core import patch_all

# Patch AWS SDK and requests
patch_all()

# Configure recorder
xray_recorder.configure(
    service='my-service',
    sampling=True,
    context_missing='LOG_ERROR'
)

# Custom subsegment
@xray_recorder.capture('process_order')
def process_order(order_id):
    segment = xray_recorder.current_segment()
    segment.put_annotation('order_id', order_id)
    # Processing logic...
    segment.put_metadata('order', {'items': 5})
48.2 Jaeger Setup Automation Open-source tracing
▶️ Jaeger Docker Compose
# docker-compose.yml
version: '3'
services:
  jaeger:
    image: jaegertracing/all-in-one:latest
    ports:
      - "6831:6831/udp"  # Thrift compact
      - "16686:16686"    # UI
      - "14268:14268"    # HTTP collector
    environment:
      COLLECTOR_ZIPKIN_HOST_PORT: 9411

# Python client setup
from jaeger_client import Config

def init_tracer(service_name):
    config = Config(
        config={'sampler': {'type': 'const', 'param': 1}},
        service_name=service_name,
        validate=True
    )
    return config.initialize_tracer()
48.3 Trace Sampling Configuration Control trace volume
▶️ X-Ray Sampling Rules
{
  "version": 2,
  "rules": [
    {
      "description": "Health checks - minimal sampling",
      "host": "*",
      "http_method": "GET",
      "url_path": "/health*",
      "fixed_target": 0,
      "rate": 0.01
    },
    {
      "description": "API endpoints - higher sampling",
      "host": "*",
      "http_method": "*",
      "url_path": "/api/*",
      "fixed_target": 1,
      "rate": 0.10
    },
    {
      "description": "Default rule",
      "host": "*",
      "http_method": "*",
      "url_path": "*",
      "fixed_target": 1,
      "rate": 0.05
    }
  ],
  "default": {"fixed_target": 1, "rate": 0.05}
}
48.4 Service Map Generation Visualize service dependencies
▶️ Service Map Script
#!/bin/bash
# get-service-map.sh

# Get X-Ray service graph
aws xray get-service-graph \
  --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s) \
  --end-time $(date +%s) \
  --query 'Services[].{Name:Name,Type:Type,ResponseTime:ResponseTimeHistogram}' \
  --output table

# Get trace summaries
aws xray get-trace-summaries \
  --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s) \
  --end-time $(date +%s) \
  --filter-expression 'responseTime > 1' \
  --query 'TraceSummaries[].{Id:Id,Duration:Duration,Http:Http}'
48.5 Performance Analysis Scripts Analyze trace data
▶️ Trace Analyzer
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def analyze_slow_traces(threshold_ms=1000):
    """Find and analyze slow traces"""
    xray = boto3.client('xray')
    
    response = xray.get_trace_summaries(
        StartTime=datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=1),
        EndTime=datetime.utcnow(),
        FilterExpression=f'responseTime > {threshold_ms/1000}'
    )
    
    slow_traces = []
    for trace in response['TraceSummaries']:
        trace_detail = xray.batch_get_traces(TraceIds=[trace['Id']])
        
        # Analyze segments
        for segment in trace_detail['Traces'][0]['Segments']:
            doc = json.loads(segment['Document'])
            slow_traces.append({
                'trace_id': trace['Id'],
                'service': doc.get('name'),
                'duration_ms': trace['Duration'] * 1000
            })
    
    return slow_traces

Chapter 49: Security Scanning Automation

49.1 Container Image Scanning Trivy and Clair scanning
🔐 Security Scanning Pipeline 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SECURITY SCANNING WORKFLOW                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  CODE COMMIT        BUILD              SCAN               DEPLOY     │
│  ──────────        ─────              ────               ──────     │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌──────┐     ┌──────────┐    ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────┐    │
│  │ Git  │ ──► │ Docker   │ ─► │ SAST (Code)  │ ──► │ Pass?    │    │
│  │ Push │     │ Build    │    │ SCA (Deps)   │     │ ↓        │    │
│  └──────┘     └──────────┘    │ Container    │     │ Deploy   │    │
│                               │ IaC          │     └──────────┘    │
│                               │ Secrets      │                      │
│                               └──────────────┘                      │
│                                     │                               │
│                                     ▼                               │
│                            ┌──────────────┐                         │
│                            │ FAIL? Block  │                         │
│                            │ deployment   │                         │
│                            └──────────────┘                         │
│                                                                      │
│  SCAN TYPES:                                                        │
│  ┌────────────┬───────────────────┬─────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Type       │ Tool              │ What it finds               │  │
│  ├────────────┼───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┤  │
│  │ Container  │ Trivy, Clair      │ CVEs in base images         │  │
│  │ SAST       │ Semgrep, Bandit   │ Code vulnerabilities        │  │
│  │ SCA        │ Snyk, Dependabot  │ Vulnerable dependencies     │  │
│  │ IaC        │ Checkov, tfsec    │ Misconfigurations           │  │
│  │ Secrets    │ Gitleaks, TruffleHog │ Exposed credentials      │  │
│  └────────────┴───────────────────┴─────────────────────────────┘  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Trivy Scan Script
#!/bin/bash
# scan-container.sh

IMAGE=$1
SEVERITY="CRITICAL,HIGH"

# Scan image
trivy image --severity $SEVERITY --exit-code 1 $IMAGE

# JSON output for CI
trivy image --format json --output results.json $IMAGE

# Scan in CI pipeline (GitHub Actions)
# - name: Trivy scan
#   uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
#   with:
#     image-ref: ${{ env.IMAGE }}
#     exit-code: '1'
#     severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
49.2 Code Security Scanning (SAST) Static code analysis
▶️ Semgrep Scan
#!/bin/bash
# sast-scan.sh

# Run Semgrep with security rules
semgrep --config=p/security-audit --config=p/owasp-top-ten .

# Bandit for Python
bandit -r . -f json -o bandit-results.json

# ESLint security plugin for JavaScript
npx eslint --ext .js,.ts . --plugin security

# Output SARIF for GitHub
semgrep --config=auto --sarif --output=results.sarif .
49.3 Dependency Vulnerability Scanning SCA scanning
▶️ Dependency Scan
#!/bin/bash
# dependency-scan.sh

# Python - pip-audit
pip-audit --format=json --output=audit.json

# Node.js - npm audit
npm audit --json > npm-audit.json

# Snyk scan
snyk test --json > snyk-results.json

# OWASP Dependency-Check
dependency-check --project "MyApp" --scan . --format JSON

# Check for critical vulnerabilities
if jq -e '.vulnerabilities[] | select(.severity == "CRITICAL")' audit.json; then
  echo "Critical vulnerabilities found!"
  exit 1
fi
49.4 Infrastructure Security Scanning IaC security checks
▶️ IaC Scanning
#!/bin/bash
# iac-scan.sh

# Checkov for Terraform/CloudFormation
checkov -d ./terraform --output json > checkov-results.json

# tfsec for Terraform
tfsec . --format json --out tfsec-results.json

# Terrascan
terrascan scan -t aws -i terraform -d .

# AWS specific - cfn-nag
cfn_nag_scan --input-path ./cloudformation/*.yaml

# Kubernetes manifests
kubesec scan deployment.yaml
49.5 Secret Detection Automation Find leaked secrets
▶️ Secret Scanning
#!/bin/bash
# secret-scan.sh

# Gitleaks - scan git history
gitleaks detect --source . --report-path gitleaks-report.json

# Trufflehog
trufflehog filesystem . --json > trufflehog-results.json

# detect-secrets
detect-secrets scan . --all-files > .secrets.baseline

# Pre-commit hook
# .pre-commit-config.yaml
# - repo: https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks
#   hooks:
#     - id: gitleaks
49.6 Compliance Scanning Scripts CIS benchmark checks
▶️ Compliance Check
#!/bin/bash
# compliance-scan.sh

# Prowler - AWS CIS benchmark
prowler aws --compliance cis_2.0 --output-formats json

# ScoutSuite - multi-cloud
scout aws --report-dir ./scout-report

# Cloud Custodian policy check
custodian run -s output policy.yml

# Generate compliance report
prowler aws --compliance cis_2.0 --output-formats html \
  --output-filename compliance-report

50.1 AWS Secrets Manager Scripts Managing secrets in AWS
🔑 Secrets Management Architecture 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SECRETS MANAGEMENT FLOW                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────┐    │
│  │ Developer   │    │ Secrets Store   │    │ Application      │    │
│  │             │    │                 │    │                  │    │
│  │ Creates/    │ ─► │ AWS Secrets Mgr │ ◄─ │ Retrieves at     │    │
│  │ Rotates     │    │ HashiCorp Vault │    │ runtime          │    │
│  └─────────────┘    │ Azure Key Vault │    └──────────────────┘    │
│                     └─────────────────┘                             │
│                            │                                        │
│                            ▼                                        │
│                   ┌─────────────────┐                               │
│                   │ Auto-Rotation   │                               │
│                   │ (Lambda/Cron)   │                               │
│                   └─────────────────┘                               │
│                                                                      │
│  SECRET TYPES:                                                      │
│  ┌──────────────┬────────────────┬────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │ Type         │ Rotation       │ Use Case                   │    │
│  ├──────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────────────┤    │
│  │ DB Creds     │ 30 days        │ RDS, Aurora connections    │    │
│  │ API Keys     │ 90 days        │ Third-party integrations   │    │
│  │ Certificates │ Before expiry  │ TLS/SSL termination        │    │
│  │ SSH Keys     │ On demand      │ Server access              │    │
│  │ OAuth Tokens │ Auto-refresh   │ Service authentication     │    │
│  └──────────────┴────────────────┴────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                                                      │
│  ❌ NEVER: Store secrets in code, env files, or git                 │
│  ✅ ALWAYS: Encrypt at rest, audit access, rotate regularly         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Secrets Manager Operations
#!/bin/bash
# secrets-manager.sh

# Create secret
aws secretsmanager create-secret \
  --name "prod/database/credentials" \
  --secret-string '{"username":"admin","password":"secret123"}'

# Retrieve secret
SECRET=$(aws secretsmanager get-secret-value \
  --secret-id "prod/database/credentials" \
  --query SecretString --output text)

# Parse JSON secret
DB_USER=$(echo $SECRET | jq -r '.username')
DB_PASS=$(echo $SECRET | jq -r '.password')

# Update secret
aws secretsmanager update-secret \
  --secret-id "prod/database/credentials" \
  --secret-string '{"username":"admin","password":"newpassword"}'
50.2 HashiCorp Vault Automation Vault CLI scripts
▶️ Vault Operations
#!/bin/bash
# vault-automation.sh

export VAULT_ADDR='https://vault.example.com:8200'

# Login with AppRole
vault write auth/approle/login \
  role_id="$ROLE_ID" \
  secret_id="$SECRET_ID"

# Read secret
vault kv get -format=json secret/database | jq -r '.data.data.password'

# Write secret
vault kv put secret/database \
  username="admin" \
  password="$(openssl rand -base64 32)"

# Enable secrets engine
vault secrets enable -path=aws aws

# Dynamic AWS credentials
vault read aws/creds/my-role
50.3 Secret Rotation Automation Automated rotation scripts
▶️ Lambda Rotation
import boto3
import json
import string
import secrets

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    secret_id = event['SecretId']
    step = event['Step']
    
    sm = boto3.client('secretsmanager')
    
    if step == 'createSecret':
        # Generate new password
        chars = string.ascii_letters + string.digits
        new_pass = ''.join(secrets.choice(chars) for _ in range(32))
        
        sm.put_secret_value(
            SecretId=secret_id,
            ClientRequestToken=event['ClientRequestToken'],
            SecretString=json.dumps({'password': new_pass}),
            VersionStages=['AWSPENDING']
        )
    elif step == 'finishSecret':
        sm.update_secret_version_stage(
            SecretId=secret_id,
            VersionStage='AWSCURRENT',
            MoveToVersionId=event['ClientRequestToken']
        )
50.4 Environment Variable Injection Inject secrets into apps
▶️ Secret Injection Script
#!/bin/bash
# inject-secrets.sh

# Export secrets as environment variables
export_secrets() {
  local secret_id=$1
  local secret=$(aws secretsmanager get-secret-value \
    --secret-id "$secret_id" --query SecretString --output text)
  
  # Parse and export each key
  for key in $(echo $secret | jq -r 'keys[]'); do
    value=$(echo $secret | jq -r ".$key")
    export "$key=$value"
  done
}

# Usage
export_secrets "prod/app/config"

# Docker with secrets
docker run -e "$(aws secretsmanager get-secret-value \
  --secret-id prod/db --query SecretString --output text | \
  jq -r 'to_entries | .[] | "\(.key)=\(.value)"')" myapp
50.5 Certificate Management Automation TLS certificate scripts
▶️ Cert Management
#!/bin/bash
# cert-management.sh

# Check certificate expiration
check_cert_expiry() {
  local domain=$1
  local days_before=${2:-30}
  
  expiry=$(echo | openssl s_client -servername $domain -connect $domain:443 2>/dev/null | \
    openssl x509 -noout -enddate | cut -d= -f2)
  
  expiry_epoch=$(date -d "$expiry" +%s)
  current_epoch=$(date +%s)
  days_left=$(( (expiry_epoch - current_epoch) / 86400 ))
  
  if [ $days_left -lt $days_before ]; then
    echo "WARN: $domain expires in $days_left days"
    return 1
  fi
}

# Let's Encrypt renewal
certbot renew --quiet --deploy-hook "systemctl reload nginx"
50.6 API Key Rotation Scripts Rotate API credentials
▶️ API Key Rotation
#!/bin/bash
# rotate-api-keys.sh

# Rotate AWS IAM access keys
rotate_iam_keys() {
  local user=$1
  
  # Create new key
  new_key=$(aws iam create-access-key --user-name $user)
  new_key_id=$(echo $new_key | jq -r '.AccessKey.AccessKeyId')
  
  # Store in Secrets Manager
  aws secretsmanager update-secret \
    --secret-id "iam/$user" \
    --secret-string "$new_key"
  
  # Wait for propagation
  sleep 10
  
  # Delete old keys
  old_keys=$(aws iam list-access-keys --user-name $user | \
    jq -r ".AccessKeyMetadata[] | select(.AccessKeyId != \"$new_key_id\") | .AccessKeyId")
  
  for key in $old_keys; do
    aws iam delete-access-key --user-name $user --access-key-id $key
  done
}

rotate_iam_keys "service-account"

│                  COMPLIANCE AS CODE WORKFLOW                         │

│  DEFINE              DETECT               REMEDIATE                  │
│  ──────              ──────               ─────────                  │
│  ┌──────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐              │
│  │ Policy   │ ─► │ AWS Config   │ ─► │ Auto-Fix via │              │
│  │ Rules    │    │ Evaluates    │    │ SSM/Lambda   │              │
│  └──────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘              │
│                         │                                           │
│                         ▼                                           │

│                  │ Compliance   │                                   │

│                  └──────────────┘                                   │

│  COMPLIANCE FRAMEWORKS:                                             │

│  │ Framework │ Focus          │ Common Rules                    │  │
│  ├───────────┼────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┤  │
│  │ CIS       │ Security       │ MFA, encryption, logging        │  │
│  │ SOC 2     │ Trust          │ Access control, monitoring      │  │
│  │ PCI-DSS   │ Payment data   │ Encryption, network segmentation│  │
│  │ HIPAA     │ Healthcare     │ PHI protection, audit logging   │  │
│  │ GDPR      │ Privacy        │ Data retention, consent         │  │
│  └───────────┴────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────┘  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ AWS Config Rule Script
#!/bin/bash
# config-rules.sh - AWS Config compliance automation

# Deploy managed rule for S3 bucket encryption
aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule '{
  "ConfigRuleName": "s3-bucket-encryption",
  "Source": {
    "Owner": "AWS",
    "SourceIdentifier": "S3_BUCKET_SERVER_SIDE_ENCRYPTION_ENABLED"
  },
  "Scope": {
    "ComplianceResourceTypes": ["AWS::S3::Bucket"]
  }
}'

# Check compliance status
aws configservice get-compliance-details-by-config-rule \
  --config-rule-name s3-bucket-encryption \
  --compliance-types NON_COMPLIANT \
  --query 'EvaluationResults[].EvaluationResultIdentifier.EvaluationResultQualifier.ResourceId'
51.2 Open Policy Agent (OPA) Policy as code engine
▶️ OPA Policy Enforcement
# policy.rego - Kubernetes admission policy
package kubernetes.admission

deny[msg] {
  input.request.kind.kind == "Deployment"
  not input.request.object.spec.template.spec.securityContext.runAsNonRoot
  msg := "Containers must run as non-root"
}

deny[msg] {
  input.request.kind.kind == "Pod"
  container := input.request.object.spec.containers[_]
  not container.resources.limits.memory
  msg := sprintf("Container %v must have memory limits", [container.name])
}
▶️ OPA Evaluation Script
#!/bin/bash
# opa-eval.sh - Evaluate policies

# Test policy locally
opa eval --data policy.rego --input input.json "data.kubernetes.admission.deny"

# Run OPA as server
opa run --server --addr :8181 policy.rego &

# Query OPA
curl -X POST http://localhost:8181/v1/data/kubernetes/admission/deny \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @input.json
51.3 Compliance Report Generation Automated reporting
▶️ Compliance Report Script
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# compliance_report.py

import boto3
import json
from datetime import datetime

def generate_compliance_report():
    config = boto3.client('config')
    
    # Get all rules compliance
    rules = config.describe_compliance_by_config_rule()['ComplianceByConfigRules']
    
    report = {
        'generated_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),
        'summary': {'compliant': 0, 'non_compliant': 0},
        'rules': []
    }
    
    for rule in rules:
        status = rule['Compliance']['ComplianceType']
        report['rules'].append({
            'rule': rule['ConfigRuleName'],
            'status': status
        })
        if status == 'COMPLIANT':
            report['summary']['compliant'] += 1
        else:
            report['summary']['non_compliant'] += 1
    
    return report

if __name__ == '__main__':
    report = generate_compliance_report()
    print(json.dumps(report, indent=2))
51.4 Remediation Automation Auto-fix compliance issues
▶️ Auto-Remediation Lambda
# remediation_lambda.py
import boto3

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """Auto-remediate S3 buckets without encryption"""
    s3 = boto3.client('s3')
    
    # Get non-compliant bucket from Config event
    bucket = event['detail']['resourceId']
    
    # Enable default encryption
    s3.put_bucket_encryption(
        Bucket=bucket,
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration={
            'Rules': [{
                'ApplyServerSideEncryptionByDefault': {
                    'SSEAlgorithm': 'AES256'
                }
            }]
        }
    )
    
    return {'remediated': bucket}
51.5 Audit Trail Scripts CloudTrail analysis
▶️ CloudTrail Audit Script
#!/bin/bash
# audit-trail.sh - Query CloudTrail for security events

# Find IAM policy changes
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=EventName,AttributeValue=PutUserPolicy \
  --start-time $(date -d "7 days ago" +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S) \
  --query 'Events[].{Time:EventTime,User:Username,Event:EventName}'

# Find console logins without MFA
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=EventName,AttributeValue=ConsoleLogin \
  --query 'Events[?contains(CloudTrailEvent, `"MFAUsed":"No"`)]'
51.6 GDPR/HIPAA Compliance Automation Data protection scripts
▶️ Data Retention Policy
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# gdpr_retention.py - GDPR data retention automation

import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def enforce_retention_policy(table_name, retention_days=365):
    """Delete records older than retention period"""
    dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb')
    table = dynamodb.Table(table_name)
    
    cutoff = datetime.now() - timedelta(days=retention_days)
    cutoff_ts = int(cutoff.timestamp())
    
    # Scan for old records
    response = table.scan(
        FilterExpression='created_at < :cutoff',
        ExpressionAttributeValues={':cutoff': cutoff_ts}
    )
    
    # Delete old records
    with table.batch_writer() as batch:
        for item in response['Items']:
            batch.delete_item(Key={'id': item['id']})
            print(f"Deleted: {item['id']}")
    
    return len(response['Items'])

# Run retention policy
deleted = enforce_retention_policy('user_data', retention_days=365)

52.1 Incident Detection Scripts Automated threat detection
🚨 Incident Response Lifecycle 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  INCIDENT RESPONSE AUTOMATION                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│   DETECT ──► TRIAGE ──► CONTAIN ──► ERADICATE ──► RECOVER ──► LEARN │
│     │          │           │            │            │          │    │
│     ▼          ▼           ▼            ▼            ▼          ▼    │
│  ┌─────┐   ┌─────┐    ┌────────┐   ┌────────┐  ┌────────┐ ┌─────┐  │
│  │Guard│   │Page │    │Isolate │   │Remove  │  │Restore │ │Post-│  │
│  │Duty │   │Team │    │Systems │   │Threat  │  │Service │ │mort-│  │
│  │Logs │   │     │    │        │   │        │  │        │ │em   │  │
│  └─────┘   └─────┘    └────────┘   └────────┘  └────────┘ └─────┘  │
│                                                                      │
│  AUTOMATION OPPORTUNITIES:                                          │
│  ┌─────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Phase           │ Automation                                 │  │
│  ├─────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤  │
│  │ Detection       │ GuardDuty, CloudWatch Alarms, SIEM         │  │
│  │ Triage          │ Severity scoring, auto-assignment          │  │
│  │ Containment     │ Security group changes, instance isolation │  │
│  │ Communication   │ PagerDuty, Slack, email notifications      │  │
│  │ Documentation   │ Auto-generated incident reports            │  │
│  └─────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ GuardDuty Detection Lambda
# guardduty_handler.py
import boto3
import json

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """Process GuardDuty findings"""
    finding = event['detail']
    severity = finding['severity']
    
    # High severity - immediate action
    if severity >= 7:
        isolate_instance(finding)
        notify_security_team(finding)
    
    # Log all findings
    log_finding(finding)
    
    return {'processed': finding['id']}

def isolate_instance(finding):
    """Apply restrictive security group"""
    ec2 = boto3.client('ec2')
    instance_id = finding['resource']['instanceDetails']['instanceId']
    
    ec2.modify_instance_attribute(
        InstanceId=instance_id,
        Groups=['sg-isolate']  # Pre-created isolation SG
    )
52.2 Automated Remediation Auto-fix security issues
▶️ Auto-Remediation Script
#!/bin/bash
# auto-remediate.sh - Security incident remediation

INSTANCE_ID=$1
INCIDENT_TYPE=$2

case $INCIDENT_TYPE in
  "compromised_key")
    # Disable access key
    aws iam update-access-key --access-key-id $KEY_ID --status Inactive
    ;;
  "open_port")
    # Remove insecure rule
    aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress \
      --group-id $SG_ID --protocol tcp --port 22 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0
    ;;
  "malware")
    # Isolate instance
    aws ec2 modify-instance-attribute \
      --instance-id $INSTANCE_ID --groups sg-quarantine
    # Create forensic snapshot
    aws ec2 create-snapshot --volume-id $VOL_ID --description "Forensic"
    ;;
esac
52.3 Forensic Data Collection Evidence preservation
▶️ Forensic Collection Script
#!/bin/bash
# forensic-collect.sh - Collect forensic data from compromised instance

INSTANCE_ID=$1
EVIDENCE_BUCKET="forensics-evidence-bucket"
TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)

# Create snapshots of all volumes
for vol in $(aws ec2 describe-volumes --filters "Name=attachment.instance-id,Values=$INSTANCE_ID" --query 'Volumes[].VolumeId' -o text); do
  aws ec2 create-snapshot --volume-id $vol \
    --description "Forensic-$INSTANCE_ID-$TIMESTAMP" \
    --tag-specifications "ResourceType=snapshot,Tags=[{Key=Forensic,Value=true}]"
done

# Collect memory dump (via SSM)
aws ssm send-command \
  --instance-ids $INSTANCE_ID \
  --document-name "AWS-RunShellScript" \
  --parameters 'commands=["dd if=/dev/mem of=/tmp/memdump.raw bs=1M"]'

# Copy logs to S3
aws s3 sync /var/log s3://$EVIDENCE_BUCKET/$INSTANCE_ID/$TIMESTAMP/logs/
52.4 Communication Automation Automated notifications
▶️ Incident Notification
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# incident_notify.py

import boto3
import requests

def notify_incident(incident):
    """Multi-channel incident notification"""
    severity = incident['severity']
    
    # PagerDuty for critical
    if severity == 'critical':
        trigger_pagerduty(incident)
    
    # Slack for all
    post_to_slack(incident)
    
    # Email stakeholders
    send_email(incident)

def post_to_slack(incident):
    webhook = "https://hooks.slack.com/services/XXX/YYY/ZZZ"
    payload = {
        "text": f"🚨 *{incident['severity'].upper()} Incident*",
        "attachments": [{
            "color": "danger" if incident['severity'] == 'critical' else "warning",
            "fields": [
                {"title": "Type", "value": incident['type'], "short": True},
                {"title": "Resource", "value": incident['resource'], "short": True},
                {"title": "Description", "value": incident['description']}
            ]
        }]
    }
    requests.post(webhook, json=payload)
52.5 Post-Incident Report Generation Automated reporting
▶️ Report Generator
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# incident_report.py

from datetime import datetime
from jinja2 import Template

REPORT_TEMPLATE = """
# Incident Report: {{ incident_id }}

## Summary
- **Date:** {{ timestamp }}
- **Severity:** {{ severity }}
- **Type:** {{ incident_type }}
- **Status:** {{ status }}

## Timeline
{% for event in timeline %}
- {{ event.time }}: {{ event.description }}
{% endfor %}

## Impact
{{ impact }}

## Root Cause
{{ root_cause }}

## Remediation Actions
{% for action in actions %}
- {{ action }}
{% endfor %}

## Lessons Learned
{{ lessons }}
"""

def generate_report(incident_data):
    template = Template(REPORT_TEMPLATE)
    return template.render(**incident_data)

53.1 Event-Driven Concepts Architecture fundamentals
⚡ Event-Driven Architecture 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  EVENT-DRIVEN ARCHITECTURE                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  PRODUCERS              EVENT BUS              CONSUMERS             │
│  ─────────              ─────────              ─────────             │
│  ┌─────────┐         ┌───────────────┐       ┌─────────┐            │
│  │ S3      │ ──────► │               │ ────► │ Lambda  │            │
│  ├─────────┤         │  EventBridge  │       ├─────────┤            │
│  │ API GW  │ ──────► │               │ ────► │ SQS     │            │
│  ├─────────┤         │  ┌─────────┐  │       ├─────────┤            │
│  │ CloudWa │ ──────► │  │ Rules   │  │ ────► │ Step Fn │            │
│  │ tch     │         │  │ Filter  │  │       ├─────────┤            │
│  ├─────────┤         │  └─────────┘  │ ────► │ SNS     │            │
│  │ Custom  │ ──────► │               │       └─────────┘            │
│  └─────────┘         └───────────────┘                              │
│                                                                      │
│  EVENT TYPES:                                                       │
│  • AWS Service Events (S3, EC2, RDS state changes)                  │
│  • Custom Application Events (order.placed, user.signup)            │
│  • Scheduled Events (cron expressions)                              │
│  • Partner Events (SaaS integrations)                               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
53.2 AWS EventBridge Automation Event bus scripting
▶️ EventBridge Setup Script
#!/bin/bash
# eventbridge-setup.sh

# Create custom event bus
aws events create-event-bus --name "my-app-events"

# Put custom event
aws events put-events --entries '[
  {
    "Source": "my.application",
    "DetailType": "Order Placed",
    "Detail": "{\"orderId\": \"12345\", \"amount\": 99.99}",
    "EventBusName": "my-app-events"
  }
]'

# List event buses
aws events list-event-buses
53.3 Event Rules and Targets Route events to targets
▶️ Create Event Rule
#!/bin/bash
# create-rule.sh

# Create rule for EC2 state changes
aws events put-rule \
  --name "ec2-state-change" \
  --event-pattern '{
    "source": ["aws.ec2"],
    "detail-type": ["EC2 Instance State-change Notification"],
    "detail": {"state": ["stopped", "terminated"]}
  }'

# Add Lambda target
aws events put-targets \
  --rule "ec2-state-change" \
  --targets '[{
    "Id": "1",
    "Arn": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789:function:handle-ec2-event"
  }]'
53.4 Cross-Account Event Routing Multi-account events
▶️ Cross-Account Setup
#!/bin/bash
# cross-account-events.sh

SOURCE_ACCOUNT="111111111111"
TARGET_ACCOUNT="222222222222" 
TARGET_BUS_ARN="arn:aws:events:us-east-1:$TARGET_ACCOUNT:event-bus/central"

# Source account: Create rule to forward events
aws events put-rule \
  --name "forward-to-central" \
  --event-pattern '{"source": ["my.app"]}'

aws events put-targets \
  --rule "forward-to-central" \
  --targets "[{\"Id\": \"central\", \"Arn\": \"$TARGET_BUS_ARN\"}]"

# Target account: Allow source account
aws events put-permission \
  --event-bus-name "central" \
  --statement-id "allow-source" \
  --action "events:PutEvents" \
  --principal "$SOURCE_ACCOUNT"
53.5 Event Scheduling Cron-based automation
▶️ Scheduled Events
#!/bin/bash
# schedule-events.sh

# Daily at 2 AM UTC
aws events put-rule \
  --name "daily-cleanup" \
  --schedule-expression "cron(0 2 * * ? *)"

# Every 5 minutes
aws events put-rule \
  --name "health-check" \
  --schedule-expression "rate(5 minutes)"

# Add Lambda target
aws events put-targets \
  --rule "daily-cleanup" \
  --targets '[{"Id": "1", "Arn": "arn:aws:lambda:...:cleanup-function"}]'
53.6 Event Pattern Matching Advanced filtering
▶️ Pattern Examples
// Exact match
{"source": ["aws.s3"]}

// Prefix match
{"source": [{"prefix": "aws."}]}

// Numeric comparison
{"detail": {"price": [{"numeric": [">", 100]}]}}

// OR logic (array)
{"detail": {"status": ["success", "completed"]}}

// exists check
{"detail": {"error": [{"exists": true}]}}

// Combined pattern
{
  "source": ["my.app"],
  "detail-type": ["Order"],
  "detail": {
    "amount": [{"numeric": [">=", 100]}],
    "status": ["pending"]
  }
}

54.4 Custom Webhook Handlers
54.5 Webhook Security and Validation
54.1 Webhook Receivers in Python Flask webhook endpoint
▶️ Flask Webhook Server
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# webhook_server.py

from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
import hmac
import hashlib

app = Flask(__name__)
WEBHOOK_SECRET = "your-secret-key"

def verify_signature(payload, signature):
    expected = hmac.new(
        WEBHOOK_SECRET.encode(),
        payload,
        hashlib.sha256
    ).hexdigest()
    return hmac.compare_digest(f"sha256={expected}", signature)

@app.route('/webhook', methods=['POST'])
def handle_webhook():
    signature = request.headers.get('X-Hub-Signature-256')
    
    if not verify_signature(request.data, signature):
        return jsonify({"error": "Invalid signature"}), 401
    
    event = request.json
    process_event(event)
    
    return jsonify({"status": "received"}), 200

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(port=5000)
54.2 GitHub Webhooks Automation Handle GitHub events
▶️ GitHub Webhook Handler
# github_webhook.py

@app.route('/github', methods=['POST'])
def github_webhook():
    event_type = request.headers.get('X-GitHub-Event')
    payload = request.json
    
    if event_type == 'push':
        handle_push(payload)
    elif event_type == 'pull_request':
        handle_pr(payload)
    elif event_type == 'issues':
        handle_issue(payload)
    
    return '', 200

def handle_push(payload):
    repo = payload['repository']['full_name']
    branch = payload['ref'].split('/')[-1]
    commits = len(payload['commits'])
    
    # Trigger CI pipeline
    trigger_build(repo, branch)
    
    # Notify Slack
    slack_notify(f"📦 {commits} commits pushed to {repo}/{branch}")
54.3 Slack Bot Automation Interactive Slack commands
▶️ Slack Slash Command
# slack_bot.py

@app.route('/slack/command', methods=['POST'])
def slack_command():
    command = request.form.get('command')
    text = request.form.get('text')
    user = request.form.get('user_name')
    
    if command == '/deploy':
        env, version = text.split()
        result = trigger_deployment(env, version)
        return jsonify({
            "response_type": "in_channel",
            "text": f"🚀 @{user} deployed {version} to {env}"
        })
    
    elif command == '/status':
        status = get_system_status()
        return jsonify({
            "response_type": "ephemeral",
            "text": f"System status: {status}"
        })
54.4 Custom Webhook Handlers Generic webhook processor
▶️ Lambda Webhook Handler
# lambda_webhook.py

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """API Gateway + Lambda webhook handler"""
    body = json.loads(event['body'])
    source = event['headers'].get('X-Webhook-Source', 'unknown')
    
    # Route to appropriate handler
    handlers = {
        'stripe': handle_stripe,
        'twilio': handle_twilio,
        'datadog': handle_datadog
    }
    
    handler = handlers.get(source, handle_generic)
    result = handler(body)
    
    return {
        'statusCode': 200,
        'body': json.dumps(result)
    }
54.5 Webhook Security and Validation Secure webhook endpoints
▶️ Security Middleware
# webhook_security.py

import time
from functools import wraps

def validate_webhook(f):
    @wraps(f)
    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
        # Check signature
        sig = request.headers.get('X-Signature')
        if not verify_hmac(request.data, sig):
            return jsonify({"error": "Invalid signature"}), 401
        
        # Check timestamp (prevent replay)
        ts = request.headers.get('X-Timestamp')
        if abs(time.time() - float(ts)) > 300:  # 5 min window
            return jsonify({"error": "Expired"}), 401
        
        # Check IP whitelist
        if request.remote_addr not in ALLOWED_IPS:
            return jsonify({"error": "Forbidden"}), 403
        
        return f(*args, **kwargs)
    return decorated

Chapter 55: Message Queue Automation

Automating message-based workflows

Chapter Topics

55.1 SQS Queue Management
55.2 Message Processing Scripts
55.3 Dead Letter Queue Handling
55.4 SNS Fan-out Automation
55.5 Kinesis Stream Automation
55.1 SQS Queue Management Queue operations
📨 Message Queue Architecture 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    MESSAGE QUEUE PATTERNS                            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  POINT-TO-POINT (SQS)              FAN-OUT (SNS + SQS)              │
│  ┌─────────┐    ┌─────┐            ┌─────┐                          │
│  │Producer │──► │Queue│ ──►Consumer │ SNS │                          │
│  └─────────┘    └─────┘            └──┬──┘                          │
│                                       │                              │
│                     ┌─────────────────┼─────────────────┐           │
│                     ▼                 ▼                 ▼           │
│                  ┌─────┐          ┌─────┐          ┌─────┐         │
│                  │SQS-1│          │SQS-2│          │SQS-3│         │
│                  └─────┘          └─────┘          └─────┘         │
│                                                                      │
│  QUEUE TYPES:                                                       │
│  ┌────────────┬───────────────┬────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ Type       │ Ordering      │ Use Case                       │   │
│  ├────────────┼───────────────┼────────────────────────────────┤   │
│  │ Standard   │ Best effort   │ High throughput                │   │
│  │ FIFO       │ Guaranteed    │ Order-sensitive processing     │   │
│  │ DLQ        │ N/A           │ Failed message handling        │   │
│  └────────────┴───────────────┴────────────────────────────────┘   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ SQS Management Script
#!/bin/bash
# sqs-manage.sh

QUEUE_NAME=$1

# Create standard queue
aws sqs create-queue --queue-name $QUEUE_NAME

# Create FIFO queue
aws sqs create-queue --queue-name "${QUEUE_NAME}.fifo" \
  --attributes FifoQueue=true

# Send message
aws sqs send-message \
  --queue-url $QUEUE_URL \
  --message-body '{"action": "process", "id": "123"}'

# Receive messages
aws sqs receive-message \
  --queue-url $QUEUE_URL \
  --max-number-of-messages 10
55.2 Message Processing Scripts Consumer patterns
▶️ Python SQS Consumer
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# sqs_consumer.py

import boto3
import json

sqs = boto3.client('sqs')
QUEUE_URL = "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456/my-queue"

def process_messages():
    while True:
        response = sqs.receive_message(
            QueueUrl=QUEUE_URL,
            MaxNumberOfMessages=10,
            WaitTimeSeconds=20  # Long polling
        )
        
        for msg in response.get('Messages', []):
            try:
                body = json.loads(msg['Body'])
                process(body)
                
                # Delete on success
                sqs.delete_message(
                    QueueUrl=QUEUE_URL,
                    ReceiptHandle=msg['ReceiptHandle']
                )
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"Error: {e}")  # Message returns to queue

def process(data):
    print(f"Processing: {data}")
55.3 Dead Letter Queue Handling Failed message recovery
▶️ DLQ Setup and Replay
#!/bin/bash
# dlq-handler.sh

# Create DLQ
DLQ_ARN=$(aws sqs create-queue --queue-name my-queue-dlq \
  --query 'QueueUrl' --output text)

# Configure source queue with DLQ
aws sqs set-queue-attributes \
  --queue-url $SOURCE_QUEUE \
  --attributes '{
    "RedrivePolicy": "{\"deadLetterTargetArn\":\"'$DLQ_ARN'\",\"maxReceiveCount\":\"3\"}"
  }'

# Replay DLQ messages back to main queue
aws sqs start-message-move-task \
  --source-arn $DLQ_ARN \
  --destination-arn $SOURCE_QUEUE_ARN
55.4 SNS Fan-out Automation Pub/sub pattern
▶️ SNS Fan-out Script
#!/bin/bash
# sns-fanout.sh

# Create topic
TOPIC_ARN=$(aws sns create-topic --name orders-topic --query 'TopicArn' -o text)

# Subscribe multiple queues
aws sns subscribe \
  --topic-arn $TOPIC_ARN \
  --protocol sqs \
  --notification-endpoint $INVENTORY_QUEUE_ARN

aws sns subscribe \
  --topic-arn $TOPIC_ARN \
  --protocol sqs \
  --notification-endpoint $BILLING_QUEUE_ARN

aws sns subscribe \
  --topic-arn $TOPIC_ARN \
  --protocol sqs \
  --notification-endpoint $NOTIFICATION_QUEUE_ARN

# Publish message (delivered to all subscribers)
aws sns publish \
  --topic-arn $TOPIC_ARN \
  --message '{"orderId": "123", "amount": 99.99}'
55.5 Kinesis Stream Automation Real-time streaming
▶️ Kinesis Producer/Consumer
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# kinesis_streaming.py

import boto3
import json

kinesis = boto3.client('kinesis')
STREAM_NAME = 'events-stream'

def put_record(data):
    """Produce to Kinesis"""
    kinesis.put_record(
        StreamName=STREAM_NAME,
        Data=json.dumps(data),
        PartitionKey=data.get('id', 'default')
    )

def consume_stream():
    """Consume from Kinesis"""
    response = kinesis.describe_stream(StreamName=STREAM_NAME)
    shard_id = response['StreamDescription']['Shards'][0]['ShardId']
    
    iterator = kinesis.get_shard_iterator(
        StreamName=STREAM_NAME,
        ShardId=shard_id,
        ShardIteratorType='LATEST'
    )['ShardIterator']
    
    while True:
        response = kinesis.get_records(ShardIterator=iterator, Limit=100)
        for record in response['Records']:
            data = json.loads(record['Data'])
            process(data)
        iterator = response['NextShardIterator']

🔀 Part 13: Workflow Orchestration

Step Functions, Airflow, and workflow automation tools.

Chapter 56: Workflow Automation Tools

Orchestration platforms for complex automation workflows

Chapter Topics

56.1 AWS Step Functions
56.2 Apache Airflow
56.3 Prefect and Dagster
56.4 Temporal Workflows
56.5 Choosing the Right Orchestrator
56.1 AWS Step Functions Serverless orchestration
🔀 Workflow Orchestration Comparison 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 WORKFLOW ORCHESTRATION PLATFORMS                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  ┌──────────────┬─────────────┬─────────────┬──────────────────┐   │
│  │ Platform     │ Type        │ Best For    │ Hosting          │   │
│  ├──────────────┼─────────────┼─────────────┼──────────────────┤   │
│  │Step Functions│ Serverless  │ AWS native  │ Fully managed    │   │
│  │ Airflow      │ DAG-based   │ Data pipes  │ Self/MWAA        │   │
│  │ Prefect      │ Python-first│ ML workflows│ Cloud/Self       │   │
│  │ Temporal     │ Durable     │ Long-running│ Cloud/Self       │   │
│  │ Dagster      │ Asset-based │ Data ops    │ Cloud/Self       │   │
│  │ Argo         │ K8s-native  │ Containers  │ Kubernetes       │   │
│  └──────────────┴─────────────┴─────────────┴──────────────────┘   │
│                                                                      │
│  DECISION MATRIX:                                                   │
│  • AWS-only + serverless → Step Functions                           │
│  • Data engineering + scheduling → Airflow                          │
│  • Python ML pipelines → Prefect/Dagster                            │
│  • Long-running, distributed → Temporal                             │
│  • Kubernetes-native → Argo Workflows                               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Step Functions Example
{
  "StartAt": "ProcessOrder",
  "States": {
    "ProcessOrder": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:...:processOrder",
      "Next": "SendNotification"
    },
    "SendNotification": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:...:notify",
      "End": true
    }
  }
}
56.2 Apache Airflow DAG-based orchestration
▶️ Airflow DAG Example
from airflow import DAG
from airflow.operators.python import PythonOperator
from datetime import datetime

with DAG('etl_pipeline', start_date=datetime(2024, 1, 1), 
         schedule_interval='@daily') as dag:
    
    def extract():
        return fetch_data_from_source()
    
    def transform(data):
        return process_data(data)
    
    def load(data):
        save_to_database(data)
    
    t1 = PythonOperator(task_id='extract', python_callable=extract)
    t2 = PythonOperator(task_id='transform', python_callable=transform)
    t3 = PythonOperator(task_id='load', python_callable=load)
    
    t1 >> t2 >> t3
56.3 Prefect and Dagster Modern Python orchestration
▶️ Prefect Flow
from prefect import flow, task

@task
def extract_data():
    return {"raw": "data"}

@task
def transform_data(data):
    return {"transformed": data}

@task
def load_data(data):
    print(f"Loading: {data}")

@flow(name="ETL Pipeline")
def etl_flow():
    raw = extract_data()
    transformed = transform_data(raw)
    load_data(transformed)

# Deploy with: prefect deployment build etl.py:etl_flow -n prod
if __name__ == "__main__":
    etl_flow()
56.4 Temporal Workflows Durable execution
▶️ Temporal Workflow (Python)
from temporalio import workflow, activity
from datetime import timedelta

@activity.defn
async def process_payment(order_id: str) -> str:
    return f"Processed: {order_id}"

@workflow.defn
class OrderWorkflow:
    @workflow.run
    async def run(self, order_id: str):
        # Durable - survives failures
        result = await workflow.execute_activity(
            process_payment,
            order_id,
            start_to_close_timeout=timedelta(minutes=5)
        )
        return result
56.5 Choosing the Right Orchestrator Decision guide
▶️ Decision Script
#!/bin/bash
# choose-orchestrator.sh - Decision helper

echo "Answer these questions:"
read -p "AWS native? (y/n): " aws
read -p "Data engineering focus? (y/n): " data
read -p "Long-running processes? (y/n): " long
read -p "Kubernetes? (y/n): " k8s

if [[ $aws == "y" && $long != "y" ]]; then
  echo "→ Recommend: AWS Step Functions"
elif [[ $data == "y" ]]; then
  echo "→ Recommend: Apache Airflow (or MWAA)"
elif [[ $long == "y" ]]; then
  echo "→ Recommend: Temporal"
elif [[ $k8s == "y" ]]; then
  echo "→ Recommend: Argo Workflows"
else
  echo "→ Recommend: Prefect or Dagster"
fi

Chapter 57: Step Functions Automation

AWS Step Functions for serverless workflow automation

Chapter Topics

57.1 State Machine Definition
57.2 Task States and Lambda Integration
57.3 Error Handling and Retries
57.4 Parallel and Map States
57.5 Human Approval Workflows
57.6 Long-Running Workflow Patterns
57.1 State Machine Definition Define workflows in ASL
🔀 Step Functions State Machine Architecture 
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                AWS STEP FUNCTIONS WORKFLOW                      │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│    ┌───────────┐     ┌───────────┐     ┌───────────────┐       │
│    │  Start    │────▶│  Choice   │─Yes─▶│  Process A   │       │
│    └───────────┘     └─────┬─────┘     └───────┬───────┘       │
│                            │No                  │               │
│                            ▼                    ▼               │
│                     ┌───────────┐         ┌─────────┐          │
│                     │ Process B │────────▶│  Wait   │          │
│                     └───────────┘         └────┬────┘          │
│                                                │               │
│         ┌───────────────────────────────────────┘               │
│         ▼                                                       │
│    ┌─────────┐    ┌──────────┐    ┌─────────────┐              │
│    │ Parallel │───▶│ Map State│───▶│    End      │              │
│    │ Branch  │    │ (iterate)│    │  (Success)  │              │
│    └─────────┘    └──────────┘    └─────────────┘              │
│                                                                 │
│  STATE TYPES:                                                   │
│  • Task: Execute Lambda/Activity                                │
│  • Choice: Conditional branching                                │
│  • Wait: Delay execution                                        │
│  • Parallel: Concurrent branches                                │
│  • Map: Iterate over arrays                                     │
│  • Pass: Transform data                                         │
│  • Succeed/Fail: Terminal states                                │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Python CDK State Machine
from aws_cdk import aws_stepfunctions as sfn
from aws_cdk import aws_stepfunctions_tasks as tasks

# Define states
process = tasks.LambdaInvoke(self, "Process",
    lambda_function=process_fn,
    output_path="$.Payload"
)

notify = tasks.SnsPublish(self, "Notify",
    topic=topic,
    message=sfn.TaskInput.from_json_path_at("$.message")
)

# Build state machine
definition = process.next(notify)

sfn.StateMachine(self, "OrderMachine",
    definition=definition,
    timeout=Duration.minutes(30)
)
57.2 Task States and Integrations AWS service integrations
▶️ Service Integrations (ASL)
{
  "StartAt": "InvokeLambda",
  "States": {
    "InvokeLambda": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::lambda:invoke",
      "Parameters": {
        "FunctionName": "${ProcessFunction}",
        "Payload.$": "$"
      },
      "Next": "WriteToDynamo"
    },
    "WriteToDynamo": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::dynamodb:putItem",
      "Parameters": {
        "TableName": "${Table}",
        "Item": {
          "id": {"S.$": "$.id"},
          "status": {"S": "complete"}
        }
      },
      "End": true
    }
  }
}
57.3 Error Handling and Retry Resilient workflows
▶️ Retry and Catch Example
{
  "ProcessPayment": {
    "Type": "Task",
    "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:...:processPayment",
    "Retry": [
      {
        "ErrorEquals": ["ServiceException", "Lambda.TooManyRequestsException"],
        "IntervalSeconds": 2,
        "MaxAttempts": 6,
        "BackoffRate": 2
      }
    ],
    "Catch": [
      {
        "ErrorEquals": ["States.ALL"],
        "Next": "HandleError",
        "ResultPath": "$.error"
      }
    ],
    "Next": "Success"
  }
}
57.4 Parallel and Map States Concurrent processing
▶️ Parallel and Map Examples
{
  "ParallelProcessing": {
    "Type": "Parallel",
    "Branches": [
      {"StartAt": "ProcessA", "States": {"ProcessA": {"Type": "Pass", "End": true}}},
      {"StartAt": "ProcessB", "States": {"ProcessB": {"Type": "Pass", "End": true}}}
    ],
    "Next": "Aggregate"
  },
  "MapItems": {
    "Type": "Map",
    "ItemsPath": "$.items",
    "MaxConcurrency": 10,
    "Iterator": {
      "StartAt": "ProcessItem",
      "States": {
        "ProcessItem": {
          "Type": "Task",
          "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:...:process",
          "End": true
        }
      }
    },
    "End": true
  }
}
57.5 Human Approval Workflows Manual intervention
▶️ Callback Pattern
import boto3

sfn = boto3.client('stepfunctions')

# Send task token to external system
def request_approval(event, context):
    task_token = event['TaskToken']
    # Store token, send approval request
    send_email(
        to="approver@company.com",
        approve_url=f"https://api.example.com/approve?token={task_token}",
        reject_url=f"https://api.example.com/reject?token={task_token}"
    )

# Callback from approval
def handle_approval(event, context):
    token = event['queryStringParameters']['token']
    approved = event['path'] == '/approve'
    
    if approved:
        sfn.send_task_success(taskToken=token, output='{"approved": true}')
    else:
        sfn.send_task_failure(taskToken=token, error='Rejected')
57.6 Long-Running Workflow Patterns Extended executions
▶️ Polling Pattern
{
  "StartJob": {
    "Type": "Task",
    "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:...:startJob",
    "Next": "WaitForJob"
  },
  "WaitForJob": {
    "Type": "Wait",
    "Seconds": 30,
    "Next": "CheckStatus"
  },
  "CheckStatus": {
    "Type": "Task",
    "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:...:checkStatus",
    "Next": "IsComplete"
  },
  "IsComplete": {
    "Type": "Choice",
    "Choices": [
      {
        "Variable": "$.status",
        "StringEquals": "COMPLETE",
        "Next": "Success"
      }
    ],
    "Default": "WaitForJob"
  }
}

Chapter 58: Airflow DAG Automation

Apache Airflow DAG development and automation

Chapter Topics

58.1 DAG Definition and Structure
58.2 Operators and Sensors
58.3 Dynamic DAG Generation
58.4 Airflow API Automation
58.5 Connection and Variable Management
58.6 Airflow on Kubernetes (KubernetesPodOperator)
58.1 DAG Definition and Structure Building Airflow DAGs
▶️ Complete DAG Example
from airflow import DAG
from airflow.operators.python import PythonOperator
from airflow.operators.bash import BashOperator
from airflow.utils.dates import days_ago

default_args = {
    'owner': 'data-team',
    'depends_on_past': False,
    'email': ['alerts@example.com'],
    'email_on_failure': True,
    'retries': 3,
    'retry_delay': timedelta(minutes=5),
}

with DAG(
    dag_id='data_pipeline',
    default_args=default_args,
    description='Daily ETL pipeline',
    schedule_interval='0 6 * * *',
    start_date=days_ago(1),
    catchup=False,
    tags=['etl', 'production'],
) as dag:
    
    extract = BashOperator(
        task_id='extract',
        bash_command='python /scripts/extract.py'
    )
    
    transform = PythonOperator(
        task_id='transform',
        python_callable=transform_data
    )
    
    load = PythonOperator(
        task_id='load',
        python_callable=load_to_warehouse
    )
    
    extract >> transform >> load
58.2 Operators and Sensors Task types
▶️ Common Operators
from airflow.providers.amazon.aws.operators.s3 import S3CopyObjectOperator
from airflow.providers.amazon.aws.sensors.s3 import S3KeySensor
from airflow.providers.http.operators.http import SimpleHttpOperator

# Wait for file
wait_for_file = S3KeySensor(
    task_id='wait_for_file',
    bucket_name='data-bucket',
    bucket_key='input/{{ ds }}/data.csv',
    poke_interval=60,
    timeout=3600
)

# HTTP call
api_call = SimpleHttpOperator(
    task_id='call_api',
    http_conn_id='api_connection',
    endpoint='/process',
    method='POST',
    data='{"date": "{{ ds }}"}'
)

# Copy S3
copy_file = S3CopyObjectOperator(
    task_id='archive',
    source_bucket_key='input/{{ ds }}/data.csv',
    dest_bucket_key='archive/{{ ds }}/data.csv',
    source_bucket_name='data-bucket',
    dest_bucket_name='archive-bucket'
)

wait_for_file >> api_call >> copy_file
58.3 Dynamic DAG Generation Programmatic DAGs
▶️ Generate DAGs from Config
import yaml
from airflow import DAG
from airflow.operators.python import PythonOperator

# Load config
with open('/config/pipelines.yaml') as f:
    config = yaml.safe_load(f)

def create_dag(pipeline_config):
    dag = DAG(
        dag_id=f"pipeline_{pipeline_config['name']}",
        schedule_interval=pipeline_config['schedule'],
        catchup=False,
        start_date=days_ago(1)
    )
    
    with dag:
        for i, step in enumerate(pipeline_config['steps']):
            PythonOperator(
                task_id=step['name'],
                python_callable=globals()[step['function']]
            )
    return dag

# Generate DAGs
for pipeline in config['pipelines']:
    globals()[f"dag_{pipeline['name']}"] = create_dag(pipeline)
58.4 Airflow API Automation REST API operations
▶️ Airflow API Client
import requests
from datetime import datetime

class AirflowAPI:
    def __init__(self, base_url, username, password):
        self.base_url = base_url
        self.auth = (username, password)
    
    def trigger_dag(self, dag_id, conf=None):
        """Trigger DAG run"""
        response = requests.post(
            f"{self.base_url}/api/v1/dags/{dag_id}/dagRuns",
            auth=self.auth,
            json={
                "conf": conf or {},
                "logical_date": datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
            }
        )
        return response.json()
    
    def get_dag_runs(self, dag_id, limit=10):
        """Get DAG run history"""
        response = requests.get(
            f"{self.base_url}/api/v1/dags/{dag_id}/dagRuns",
            auth=self.auth,
            params={"limit": limit}
        )
        return response.json()

# Usage
api = AirflowAPI("https://airflow.example.com", "user", "pass")
api.trigger_dag("etl_pipeline", conf={"date": "2024-01-15"})
58.5 Connection and Variable Management Secrets and config
▶️ Manage Connections
#!/bin/bash
# Airflow CLI connection management

# Add connection via CLI
airflow connections add 'aws_default' \
    --conn-type 'aws' \
    --conn-extra '{"region_name": "us-east-1"}'

# Add database connection
airflow connections add 'postgres_dwh' \
    --conn-type 'postgres' \
    --conn-host 'dwh.example.com' \
    --conn-port 5432 \
    --conn-login 'airflow' \
    --conn-password "$DB_PASSWORD" \
    --conn-schema 'analytics'

# Set variables
airflow variables set 'environment' 'production'
airflow variables set 'config' '{"batch_size": 1000}' --serialize-json

# Export/import for backup
airflow connections export /backup/connections.json
airflow variables export /backup/variables.json
58.6 Airflow on Kubernetes KubernetesPodOperator
▶️ KubernetesPodOperator Example
from airflow.providers.cncf.kubernetes.operators.kubernetes_pod import KubernetesPodOperator
from kubernetes.client import models as k8s

run_processor = KubernetesPodOperator(
    task_id='run_processor',
    name='data-processor',
    namespace='airflow',
    image='myregistry/processor:latest',
    cmds=['python', '/app/process.py'],
    arguments=['--date', '{{ ds }}'],
    env_vars={
        'DATABASE_URL': '{{ var.value.database_url }}',
        'AWS_REGION': 'us-east-1'
    },
    secrets=[
        k8s.V1EnvVar(
            name='DB_PASSWORD',
            value_from=k8s.V1EnvVarSource(
                secret_key_ref=k8s.V1SecretKeySelector(
                    name='db-secrets',
                    key='password'
                )
            )
        )
    ],
    resources=k8s.V1ResourceRequirements(
        requests={'memory': '2Gi', 'cpu': '1'},
        limits={'memory': '4Gi', 'cpu': '2'}
    ),
    is_delete_operator_pod=True,
    get_logs=True
)

🧪 Part 14: Testing & Quality Automation

Automated testing for scripts, infrastructure, and code quality.

Chapter 59: Script Testing

Unit testing and quality assurance for scripts

Chapter Topics

59.1 Unit Testing Bash Scripts (bats)
59.2 Python Script Testing (pytest)
59.3 PowerShell Testing (Pester)
59.4 Mocking External Dependencies
