59.1 Unit Testing Bash Scripts (bats) Bash Automated Testing System
🧪 Testing Frameworks Overview 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   SCRIPT TESTING FRAMEWORKS                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│  BASH TESTING:                                                   │
│  ┌───────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ bats      │ TAP-compliant, simple syntax, @test blocks     │ │
│  │ shunit2   │ xUnit-style assertions                         │ │
│  │ shellspec │ BDD-style specifications                        │ │
│  └───────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                  │
│  PYTHON TESTING:                                                 │
│  ┌───────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ pytest    │ Most popular, fixtures, plugins                │ │
│  │ unittest  │ Built-in, xUnit style                          │ │
│  │ nose2     │ unittest extension                             │ │
│  └───────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                  │
│  POWERSHELL TESTING:                                             │
│  ┌───────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ Pester    │ BDD-style, Describe/It/Should                  │ │
│  └───────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Bats Test Example
#!/usr/bin/env bats
# test_functions.bats

setup() {
  source ./functions.sh
  export TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
}

teardown() {
  rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
}

@test "validate_email accepts valid email" {
  run validate_email "user@example.com"
  [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
}

@test "validate_email rejects invalid email" {
  run validate_email "not-an-email"
  [ "$status" -eq 1 ]
  [[ "$output" == *"Invalid"* ]]
}

@test "create_backup creates archive" {
  run create_backup "$TEST_DIR/data"
  [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
  [ -f "$TEST_DIR/data.tar.gz" ]
}

# Run: bats test_functions.bats
59.2 Python Script Testing (pytest) Comprehensive Python testing
▶️ Pytest Examples
import pytest
from automation import DataProcessor, ConfigValidator

class TestDataProcessor:
    @pytest.fixture
    def processor(self):
        return DataProcessor(config={'batch_size': 100})
    
    def test_process_valid_data(self, processor):
        result = processor.process({'name': 'test', 'value': 42})
        assert result['status'] == 'success'
    
    def test_process_invalid_data(self, processor):
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Missing required field"):
            processor.process({'name': 'test'})
    
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("input,expected", [
        ({'value': 10}, 100),
        ({'value': 0}, 0),
        ({'value': -5}, -50),
    ])
    def test_calculate_score(self, processor, input, expected):
        assert processor.calculate_score(input) == expected

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def database():
    """Session-scoped fixture for database"""
    db = setup_test_database()
    yield db
    db.teardown()
59.3 PowerShell Testing (Pester) BDD-style PowerShell tests
▶️ Pester Test Example
# Test-UserManagement.Tests.ps1
BeforeAll {
    . $PSScriptRoot/UserManagement.ps1
}

Describe "New-UserAccount" {
    Context "When creating valid user" {
        It "Should create user with correct properties" {
            $user = New-UserAccount -Name "testuser" -Email "test@example.com"
            $user.Name | Should -Be "testuser"
            $user.Email | Should -Be "test@example.com"
        }
    }
    
    Context "When validation fails" {
        It "Should throw for invalid email" {
            { New-UserAccount -Name "test" -Email "invalid" } |
                Should -Throw "*Invalid email*"
        }
    }
}

Describe "Remove-UserAccount" {
    BeforeEach {
        $script:testUser = New-UserAccount -Name "temp" -Email "t@e.com"
    }
    
    It "Should remove user successfully" {
        Remove-UserAccount -Id $testUser.Id
        { Get-UserAccount -Id $testUser.Id } | Should -Throw
    }
}

# Run: Invoke-Pester -Path ./Test-UserManagement.Tests.ps1
59.4 Mocking External Dependencies Isolating unit tests
▶️ Python Mocking
from unittest.mock import Mock, patch, MagicMock
import pytest

class TestAWSOperations:
    @patch('boto3.client')
    def test_upload_to_s3(self, mock_boto):
        # Setup mock
        mock_s3 = MagicMock()
        mock_boto.return_value = mock_s3
        
        # Test
        from aws_ops import upload_file
        result = upload_file('test.txt', 'my-bucket')
        
        # Verify
        mock_s3.upload_file.assert_called_once_with(
            'test.txt', 'my-bucket', 'test.txt'
        )
        assert result == True
    
    @patch('requests.get')
    def test_api_call(self, mock_get):
        mock_get.return_value = Mock(
            status_code=200,
            json=lambda: {'status': 'ok'}
        )
        
        from api import check_health
        assert check_health() == 'ok'
        mock_get.assert_called_with('https://api.example.com/health')
59.5 Test Coverage Analysis Measuring test quality
▶️ Coverage Tools
#!/bin/bash
# Python coverage with pytest-cov
pytest --cov=src --cov-report=html --cov-report=xml \
    --cov-fail-under=80 tests/

# Generate badge
coverage-badge -o coverage.svg

# Bash script coverage (with kcov)
kcov --include-pattern=".sh" coverage/ ./tests/run_all.sh

# PowerShell coverage with Pester
$config = New-PesterConfiguration
$config.CodeCoverage.Enabled = $true
$config.CodeCoverage.Path = './src/*.ps1'
$config.CodeCoverage.OutputFormat = 'JaCoCo'
Invoke-Pester -Configuration $config

Chapter 60: Infrastructure Testing

Testing IaC and infrastructure configurations

Chapter Topics

60.1 Terraform Testing (terratest, terraform test)
60.2 Ansible Testing (molecule)
60.3 Container Testing (container-structure-test)
60.4 Kubernetes Testing (kubetest)
60.5 Compliance Testing (InSpec)
60.1 Terraform Testing terratest and terraform test
▶️ Terratest (Go)
package test

import (
    "testing"
    "github.com/gruntwork-io/terratest/modules/terraform"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
)

func TestVPCModule(t *testing.T) {
    t.Parallel()
    
    terraformOptions := &terraform.Options{
        TerraformDir: "../modules/vpc",
        Vars: map[string]interface{}{
            "cidr_block": "10.0.0.0/16",
            "environment": "test",
        },
    }
    
    defer terraform.Destroy(t, terraformOptions)
    terraform.InitAndApply(t, terraformOptions)
    
    vpcId := terraform.Output(t, terraformOptions, "vpc_id")
    assert.NotEmpty(t, vpcId)
    assert.Contains(t, vpcId, "vpc-")
}
▶️ Terraform Native Test
# tests/vpc.tftest.hcl
run "create_vpc" {
  command = apply

  variables {
    cidr_block  = "10.0.0.0/16"
    environment = "test"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = aws_vpc.main.cidr_block == "10.0.0.0/16"
    error_message = "VPC CIDR block mismatch"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = length(aws_subnet.public) == 3
    error_message = "Expected 3 public subnets"
  }
}

# Run: terraform test
60.2 Ansible Testing (molecule) Role testing framework
▶️ Molecule Configuration
# molecule/default/molecule.yml
dependency:
  name: galaxy
driver:
  name: docker
platforms:
  - name: ubuntu
    image: ubuntu:22.04
    command: /lib/systemd/systemd
    privileged: true
  - name: centos
    image: centos:stream9
provisioner:
  name: ansible
  playbooks:
    converge: converge.yml
    verify: verify.yml
verifier:
  name: ansible

# molecule/default/verify.yml
- name: Verify
  hosts: all
  gather_facts: false
  tasks:
    - name: Check nginx is running
      ansible.builtin.service_facts:
    - name: Assert nginx is running
      ansible.builtin.assert:
        that:
          - services['nginx.service'].state == 'running'

# Run: molecule test
60.3 Container Testing container-structure-test
▶️ Container Structure Test
# container-tests.yaml
schemaVersion: 2.0.0

metadataTest:
  exposedPorts: ["8080"]
  workdir: /app
  user: appuser
  entrypoint: ["/app/entrypoint.sh"]

fileExistenceTests:
  - name: "App binary exists"
    path: "/app/server"
    shouldExist: true
    permissions: "-rwxr-xr-x"

fileContentTests:
  - name: "Config has correct env"
    path: "/app/config.yaml"
    expectedContents: ["environment: production"]

commandTests:
  - name: "Python version"
    command: "python"
    args: ["--version"]
    expectedOutput: ["Python 3.11"]

# Run: container-structure-test test \
#   --image myapp:latest \
#   --config container-tests.yaml
60.4 Kubernetes Testing kubetest and kuttl
▶️ KUTTL Test Example
# tests/e2e/deployment/00-install.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: test-app
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: test
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: test
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        image: nginx:latest

---
# tests/e2e/deployment/00-assert.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: test-app
status:
  readyReplicas: 2

# Run: kubectl kuttl test ./tests/e2e/
# Or: kuttl test --config kuttl-test.yaml
60.5 Compliance Testing (InSpec) Infrastructure compliance
▶️ InSpec Profile
# controls/security.rb
control 'ssh-hardening' do
  impact 1.0
  title 'SSH Configuration'
  desc 'Ensure SSH is securely configured'
  
  describe sshd_config do
    its('PermitRootLogin') { should eq 'no' }
    its('PasswordAuthentication') { should eq 'no' }
    its('Protocol') { should eq '2' }
  end
end

control 'aws-s3-encryption' do
  impact 0.7
  title 'S3 Bucket Encryption'
  
  aws_s3_buckets.bucket_names.each do |bucket|
    describe aws_s3_bucket(bucket) do
      it { should have_default_encryption_enabled }
    end
  end
end

# Run: inspec exec . -t aws://
# Or: inspec exec . -t ssh://user@host
60.6 Serverspec Server Testing Infrastructure verification
▶️ Serverspec Tests
# spec/web_server_spec.rb
require 'serverspec'
require 'net/ssh'

set :backend, :ssh
set :host, ENV['TARGET_HOST']

describe package('nginx') do
  it { should be_installed }
end

describe service('nginx') do
  it { should be_enabled }
  it { should be_running }
end

describe port(80) do
  it { should be_listening }
end

describe file('/etc/nginx/nginx.conf') do
  it { should exist }
  it { should be_file }
  its(:content) { should match /worker_processes/ }
end

describe command('curl -s http://localhost/health') do
  its(:exit_status) { should eq 0 }
  its(:stdout) { should match /OK/ }
end

Chapter 61: Integration and E2E Testing

End-to-end and integration test automation

Chapter Topics

61.1 API Testing Automation
61.2 Load Testing Scripts (locust, k6)
61.3 Chaos Engineering Automation
61.4 Smoke Test Automation
61.5 Regression Test Scripts
61.1 API Testing Automation Automated endpoint testing
▶️ pytest API Testing
import pytest
import requests
from jsonschema import validate

BASE_URL = "https://api.example.com/v1"

class TestUserAPI:
    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
    def setup(self, auth_token):
        self.headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    
    def test_create_user(self):
        payload = {"name": "Test User", "email": "test@example.com"}
        resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/users", 
                            json=payload, headers=self.headers)
        
        assert resp.status_code == 201
        data = resp.json()
        assert data["name"] == payload["name"]
        assert "id" in data
    
    def test_get_user_schema(self):
        resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/users/1", headers=self.headers)
        
        schema = {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "id": {"type": "integer"},
                "name": {"type": "string"},
                "email": {"type": "string", "format": "email"}
            },
            "required": ["id", "name", "email"]
        }
        validate(instance=resp.json(), schema=schema)
61.2 Load Testing Scripts locust and k6
▶️ Locust Load Test
# locustfile.py
from locust import HttpUser, task, between

class WebsiteUser(HttpUser):
    wait_time = between(1, 3)
    
    def on_start(self):
        resp = self.client.post("/auth/login", json={
            "username": "testuser", "password": "testpass"
        })
        self.token = resp.json()["token"]
    
    @task(3)
    def browse_products(self):
        self.client.get("/products", 
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.token}"})
    
    @task(1)
    def checkout(self):
        self.client.post("/orders", json={"items": [1, 2]},
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.token}"})

# Run: locust -f locustfile.py --host=https://api.example.com
▶️ k6 Load Test
// loadtest.js
import http from 'k6/http';
import { check, sleep } from 'k6';

export const options = {
  stages: [
    { duration: '2m', target: 100 },
    { duration: '5m', target: 100 },
    { duration: '2m', target: 0 },
  ],
  thresholds: {
    http_req_duration: ['p(95)<500'],
    http_req_failed: ['rate<0.01'],
  },
};

export default function () {
  const res = http.get('https://api.example.com/products');
  check(res, { 'status 200': (r) => r.status === 200 });
  sleep(1);
}
// Run: k6 run loadtest.js
61.3 Chaos Engineering Automation Resilience testing
▶️ Chaos Toolkit Experiment
{
  "title": "Pod failure resilience",
  "description": "Verify app survives pod termination",
  "steady-state-hypothesis": {
    "title": "App is healthy",
    "probes": [{
      "name": "service-responds",
      "type": "probe",
      "tolerance": 200,
      "provider": {
        "type": "http",
        "url": "https://api.example.com/health"
      }
    }]
  },
  "method": [{
    "type": "action",
    "name": "terminate-pod",
    "provider": {
      "type": "python",
      "module": "chaosk8s.pod.actions",
      "func": "terminate_pods",
      "arguments": {
        "label_selector": "app=myapp",
        "ns": "production"
      }
    }
  }],
  "rollbacks": []
}
// Run: chaos run experiment.json
61.4 Smoke Test Automation Quick health validation
▶️ Smoke Test Script
#!/bin/bash
# smoke-test.sh - Post-deployment validation

set -e
BASE_URL="${1:-https://api.example.com}"
TIMEOUT=30

echo "Running smoke tests against $BASE_URL"

# Test 1: Health endpoint
echo -n "1. Health check... "
if curl -sf --max-time $TIMEOUT "$BASE_URL/health" | jq -e '.status == "ok"' > /dev/null; then
  echo "✓ PASS"
else
  echo "✗ FAIL" && exit 1
fi

# Test 2: API responds
echo -n "2. API version... "
VERSION=$(curl -sf --max-time $TIMEOUT "$BASE_URL/version" | jq -r '.version')
if [[ $VERSION =~ ^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "✓ PASS (v$VERSION)"
else
  echo "✗ FAIL" && exit 1
fi

# Test 3: Database connectivity
echo -n "3. Database... "
if curl -sf --max-time $TIMEOUT "$BASE_URL/health/db" | jq -e '.connected == true' > /dev/null; then
  echo "✓ PASS"
else
  echo "✗ FAIL" && exit 1
fi

echo "All smoke tests passed!"
61.5 Regression Test Scripts Automated regression suites
▶️ Regression Test Runner
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Regression test suite runner with reporting"""
import subprocess
import json
from datetime import datetime

def run_regression_suite():
    suites = [
        ("unit", "pytest tests/unit -v --tb=short"),
        ("integration", "pytest tests/integration -v"),
        ("e2e", "pytest tests/e2e -v --timeout=300"),
    ]
    
    results = {"timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(), "suites": []}
    
    for name, cmd in suites:
        print(f"\n{'='*50}\nRunning {name} tests\n{'='*50}")
        result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, capture_output=True)
        
        results["suites"].append({
            "name": name,
            "passed": result.returncode == 0,
            "duration": parse_duration(result.stdout),
            "output": result.stdout.decode()[:1000]
        })
    
    # Generate report
    with open(f"reports/regression-{datetime.now():%Y%m%d}.json", "w") as f:
        json.dump(results, f, indent=2)
    
    return all(s["passed"] for s in results["suites"])

if __name__ == "__main__":
    exit(0 if run_regression_suite() else 1)

Chapter 62: Code Quality Automation

Linting, formatting, and quality enforcement

Chapter Topics

62.1 Linting (shellcheck, pylint, yamllint)
62.2 Formatting (black, shfmt)
62.3 Pre-commit Hooks
62.4 Code Review Automation
62.5 Documentation Generation
62.1 Linting Tools shellcheck, pylint, yamllint
▶️ Multi-Language Lint Script
#!/bin/bash
# lint-all.sh - Comprehensive linting

set -e
ERRORS=0

echo "=== Shell Scripts (shellcheck) ==="
find . -name "*.sh" -exec shellcheck -S warning {} + || ((ERRORS++))

echo "=== Python (pylint + ruff) ==="
pylint --disable=C0114,C0115,C0116 src/ || ((ERRORS++))
ruff check src/ || ((ERRORS++))

echo "=== YAML (yamllint) ==="
yamllint -d relaxed . || ((ERRORS++))

echo "=== Terraform (tflint) ==="
find . -name "*.tf" -execdir tflint --recursive {} + || ((ERRORS++))

echo "=== Ansible (ansible-lint) ==="
ansible-lint playbooks/ || ((ERRORS++))

echo "=== JSON (jsonlint) ==="
find . -name "*.json" -exec jsonlint -q {} + || ((ERRORS++))

if [ $ERRORS -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "❌ $ERRORS linter(s) reported issues"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✅ All linters passed"
62.2 Code Formatting black, shfmt, prettier
▶️ Auto-Format Script
#!/bin/bash
# format-code.sh - Auto-format all code

echo "=== Python (black + isort) ==="
black src/ tests/
isort src/ tests/

echo "=== Shell (shfmt) ==="
shfmt -w -i 2 -ci scripts/*.sh

echo "=== JavaScript/TypeScript (prettier) ==="
prettier --write "**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,json,css,md}"

echo "=== Terraform (terraform fmt) ==="
terraform fmt -recursive infra/

echo "=== Go (gofmt) ==="
gofmt -w -s .

echo "=== YAML (prettier) ==="
prettier --write "**/*.{yaml,yml}"

# Check for uncommitted changes
if ! git diff --quiet; then
  echo "⚠️  Files were reformatted. Review changes:"
  git diff --stat
fi
62.3 Pre-commit Hooks Automated checks before commit
▶️ .pre-commit-config.yaml
# .pre-commit-config.yaml
repos:
  - repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks
    rev: v4.5.0
    hooks:
      - id: trailing-whitespace
      - id: end-of-file-fixer
      - id: check-yaml
      - id: check-json
      - id: detect-private-key
      - id: no-commit-to-branch
        args: ['--branch', 'main', '--branch', 'master']

  - repo: https://github.com/psf/black
    rev: 24.1.0
    hooks:
      - id: black

  - repo: https://github.com/pycqa/isort
    rev: 5.13.2
    hooks:
      - id: isort

  - repo: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck-precommit
    rev: v0.9.0
    hooks:
      - id: shellcheck

  - repo: https://github.com/antonbabenko/pre-commit-terraform
    rev: v1.86.0
    hooks:
      - id: terraform_fmt
      - id: terraform_validate
      - id: terraform_tflint

# Install: pre-commit install
# Run all: pre-commit run --all-files
62.4 Code Review Automation Automated PR checks
▶️ GitHub Action for Review
# .github/workflows/code-review.yml
name: Code Review Checks
on: [pull_request]

jobs:
  review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Check PR size
        run: |
          CHANGES=$(git diff --shortstat origin/main | awk '{print $4+$6}')
          if [ "$CHANGES" -gt 500 ]; then
            echo "::warning::PR has $CHANGES changed lines. Consider splitting."
          fi

      - name: Detect sensitive files
        run: |
          SENSITIVE=$(git diff --name-only origin/main | grep -E '\.(env|key|pem)$' || true)
          if [ -n "$SENSITIVE" ]; then
            echo "::error::Sensitive files detected: $SENSITIVE"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Check TODO/FIXME
        run: |
          git diff origin/main | grep -E '^\+.*TODO|FIXME|HACK' && \
            echo "::warning::New TODO/FIXME comments found" || true

      - name: Require tests for src changes
        run: |
          SRC_CHANGED=$(git diff --name-only origin/main -- 'src/' | wc -l)
          TEST_CHANGED=$(git diff --name-only origin/main -- 'tests/' | wc -l)
          if [ "$SRC_CHANGED" -gt 0 ] && [ "$TEST_CHANGED" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "::error::Source changed but no tests added"
            exit 1
          fi
62.5 Documentation Generation Auto-generate docs
▶️ Documentation Generator
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate documentation from code"""
import ast
import os
from pathlib import Path

def extract_docstrings(filepath):
    """Extract function docstrings from Python file"""
    with open(filepath) as f:
        tree = ast.parse(f.read())
    
    docs = []
    for node in ast.walk(tree):
        if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef):
            docstring = ast.get_docstring(node)
            if docstring:
                docs.append({
                    'name': node.name,
                    'args': [a.arg for a in node.args.args],
                    'doc': docstring
                })
    return docs

def generate_markdown(source_dir, output_file):
    """Generate API documentation"""
    with open(output_file, 'w') as out:
        out.write("# API Reference\n\n")
        
        for py_file in Path(source_dir).glob("**/*.py"):
            docs = extract_docstrings(py_file)
            if docs:
                out.write(f"## {py_file.stem}\n\n")
                for func in docs:
                    out.write(f"### `{func['name']}({', '.join(func['args'])})`\n")
                    out.write(f"{func['doc']}\n\n")

generate_markdown("src/", "docs/API.md")

▶️ Platform API Service
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
from pydantic import BaseModel

app = FastAPI(title="Platform API")

class ServiceRequest(BaseModel):
    name: str
    template: str
    team: str
    tier: str = "standard"

@app.post("/services")
async def create_service(request: ServiceRequest):
    """Self-service: Create new microservice"""
    # Validate against policies
    if not validate_name(request.name):
        raise HTTPException(400, "Invalid service name")
    
    # Provision resources
    repo = create_repo(request.name, request.template)
    namespace = create_namespace(request.name, request.team)
    pipeline = setup_cicd(repo, request.tier)
    
    return {"repo": repo, "namespace": namespace, "pipeline": pipeline}
63.2 Backstage Setup and Configuration Spotify's developer portal
▶️ Backstage App Configuration
# app-config.yaml
app:
  title: Platform Portal
  baseUrl: https://portal.company.com

organization:
  name: MyCompany

backend:
  baseUrl: https://portal.company.com
  database:
    client: pg
    connection:
      host: ${POSTGRES_HOST}
      database: backstage

catalog:
  rules:
    - allow: [Component, API, Resource, System, Domain, Group, User]
  locations:
    - type: url
      target: https://github.com/org/*/catalog-info.yaml
      rules:
        - allow: [Component]

techdocs:
  builder: 'external'
  publisher:
    type: 'awsS3'
    awsS3:
      bucketName: 'techdocs-bucket'

scaffolder:
  defaultAuthor:
    name: Platform Team
    email: platform@company.com

# catalog-info.yaml
apiVersion: backstage.io/v1alpha1
kind: Component
metadata:
  name: payment-service
  description: Payment processing microservice
  annotations:
    github.com/project-slug: myorg/payment-service
    backstage.io/techdocs-ref: dir:.
  tags:
    - python
    - payments
    - tier-1
spec:
  type: service
  lifecycle: production
  owner: team-payments
  system: commerce-platform
  providesApis:
    - payment-api
  consumesApis:
    - fraud-detection-api
  dependsOn:
    - resource:default/payments-db
    - resource:default/redis-cache

---
apiVersion: backstage.io/v1alpha1
kind: API
metadata:
  name: payment-api
spec:
  type: openapi
  lifecycle: production
  owner: team-payments
  definition:
    $text: ./openapi.yaml
63.4 Golden Path Templates Standardized starting points
▶️ Backstage Software Template
# template.yaml
apiVersion: scaffolder.backstage.io/v1beta3
kind: Template
metadata:
  name: python-service
  title: Python Microservice
  description: Create a production-ready Python service
spec:
  owner: platform-team
  type: service
  
  parameters:
    - title: Service Details
      required: [name, team]
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
          pattern: '^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$'
        team:
          type: string
          ui:field: OwnerPicker
        tier:
          type: string
          enum: [standard, critical]
          default: standard
  
  steps:
    - id: fetch-base
      action: fetch:template
      input:
        url: ./skeleton
        values:
          name: ${{ parameters.name }}
          team: ${{ parameters.team }}
    
    - id: create-repo
      action: publish:github
      input:
        repoUrl: github.com?owner=myorg&repo=${{ parameters.name }}
    
    - id: register-catalog
      action: catalog:register
      input:
        repoContentsUrl: ${{ steps['create-repo'].output.repoContentsUrl }}
63.5 Developer Portal APIs Platform automation endpoints
▶️ Platform CLI Tool
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Platform CLI - Self-service automation"""
import click
import requests

API_URL = "https://platform-api.company.com"

@click.group()
def cli():
    """Platform Engineering CLI"""
    pass

@cli.command()
@click.option('--name', required=True, help='Service name')
@click.option('--template', default='python-service')
@click.option('--team', required=True)
def create_service(name, template, team):
    """Create new microservice from template"""
    resp = requests.post(f"{API_URL}/services", json={
        "name": name, "template": template, "team": team
    })
    click.echo(f"✅ Created: {resp.json()['repo']}")

@cli.command()
@click.argument('service')
def status(service):
    """Check service status"""
    resp = requests.get(f"{API_URL}/services/{service}/status")
    data = resp.json()
    click.echo(f"Service: {service}")
    click.echo(f"  Health: {data['health']}")
    click.echo(f"  Deployments: {data['deployments']}")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    cli()

64.1 Resource Utilization Analysis Finding waste
▶️ AWS Utilization Scanner
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def analyze_ec2_utilization():
    """Find underutilized EC2 instances"""
    ec2 = boto3.client('ec2')
    cw = boto3.client('cloudwatch')
    
    instances = ec2.describe_instances(
        Filters=[{'Name': 'instance-state-name', 'Values': ['running']}]
    )
    
    underutilized = []
    for r in instances['Reservations']:
        for i in r['Instances']:
            # Get CPU utilization
            cpu = cw.get_metric_statistics(
                Namespace='AWS/EC2',
                MetricName='CPUUtilization',
                Dimensions=[{'Name': 'InstanceId', 'Value': i['InstanceId']}],
                StartTime=datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=14),
                EndTime=datetime.utcnow(),
                Period=86400,
                Statistics=['Average']
            )
            
            avg_cpu = sum(d['Average'] for d in cpu['Datapoints']) / len(cpu['Datapoints']) if cpu['Datapoints'] else 0
            
            if avg_cpu < 10:  # Under 10% average
                underutilized.append({
                    'InstanceId': i['InstanceId'],
                    'Type': i['InstanceType'],
                    'AvgCPU': round(avg_cpu, 2)
                })
    
    return underutilized
64.2 Right-Sizing Recommendations Optimal instance selection
▶️ Right-Sizing Script
def get_rightsizing_recommendations():
    """Generate right-sizing recommendations"""
    ce = boto3.client('ce')
    
    response = ce.get_rightsizing_recommendation(
        Service='AmazonEC2',
        Configuration={
            'RecommendationTarget': 'SAME_INSTANCE_FAMILY',
            'BenefitsConsidered': True
        }
    )
    
    recommendations = []
    for rec in response['RightsizingRecommendations']:
        current = rec['CurrentInstance']
        target = rec['ModifyRecommendationDetail']['TargetInstances'][0]
        
        recommendations.append({
            'instance_id': current['ResourceId'],
            'current_type': current['InstanceType'],
            'recommended_type': target['ExpectedResourceUtilization']['EC2ResourceDetails']['InstanceType'],
            'monthly_savings': float(rec['ModifyRecommendationDetail']['EstimatedMonthlySavings']['Value']),
            'savings_percentage': rec['ModifyRecommendationDetail']['EstimatedMonthlySavingsPercentage']
        })
    
    return sorted(recommendations, key=lambda x: x['monthly_savings'], reverse=True)
64.3 Reserved Instance Automation RI management
▶️ RI Coverage Monitor
def analyze_ri_coverage():
    """Analyze Reserved Instance coverage and recommendations"""
    ce = boto3.client('ce')
    
    # Get RI utilization
    utilization = ce.get_reservation_utilization(
        TimePeriod={
            'Start': (datetime.today() - timedelta(days=30)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d'),
            'End': datetime.today().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
        },
        GroupBy=[{'Type': 'DIMENSION', 'Key': 'SUBSCRIPTION_ID'}]
    )
    
    # Get RI purchase recommendations
    recommendations = ce.get_reservation_purchase_recommendation(
        Service='Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud - Compute',
        TermInYears='ONE_YEAR',
        PaymentOption='PARTIAL_UPFRONT',
        LookbackPeriodInDays='THIRTY_DAYS'
    )
    
    return {
        'current_utilization': utilization['Total']['UtilizationPercentage'],
        'recommendations': [{
            'instance_type': r['InstanceDetails']['EC2InstanceDetails']['InstanceType'],
            'recommended_count': r['RecommendedNumberOfInstancesToPurchase'],
            'estimated_savings': r['EstimatedMonthlySavingsAmount']
        } for r in recommendations['Recommendations'][:5]]
    }
64.4 Spot Instance Management Cost-effective compute
▶️ Spot Fleet Manager
def create_spot_fleet(config):
    """Create diversified Spot Fleet"""
    ec2 = boto3.client('ec2')
    
    response = ec2.request_spot_fleet(
        SpotFleetRequestConfig={
            'IamFleetRole': config['fleet_role'],
            'TargetCapacity': config['capacity'],
            'AllocationStrategy': 'capacityOptimized',  # Best for reliability
            'InstanceInterruptionBehavior': 'terminate',
            'LaunchTemplateConfigs': [
                {
                    'LaunchTemplateSpecification': {
                        'LaunchTemplateId': config['template_id'],
                        'Version': '$Latest'
                    },
                    'Overrides': [
                        {'InstanceType': 'm5.large', 'WeightedCapacity': 1},
                        {'InstanceType': 'm5.xlarge', 'WeightedCapacity': 2},
                        {'InstanceType': 'm4.large', 'WeightedCapacity': 1},
                        {'InstanceType': 'c5.large', 'WeightedCapacity': 1},
                    ]
                }
            ],
            'OnDemandTargetCapacity': 1,  # Keep 1 on-demand as baseline
            'SpotMaintenanceStrategies': {
                'CapacityRebalance': {'ReplacementStrategy': 'launch-before-terminate'}
            }
        }
    )
    return response['SpotFleetRequestId']
64.5 Scheduled Scaling Scripts Time-based optimization
▶️ Schedule-Based Scaling
#!/bin/bash
# scheduled-scaling.sh - Scale down non-prod during off-hours

ENVIRONMENT="${1:-dev}"
ACTION="${2:-scale-down}"

scale_asg() {
  local asg_name=$1
  local min=$2 max=$3 desired=$4
  
  aws autoscaling update-auto-scaling-group \
    --auto-scaling-group-name "$asg_name" \
    --min-size "$min" --max-size "$max" --desired-capacity "$desired"
}

if [[ $ACTION == "scale-down" ]]; then
  echo "Scaling down $ENVIRONMENT environment..."
  
  # Scale ASGs to minimum
  for asg in $(aws autoscaling describe-auto-scaling-groups \
    --query "AutoScalingGroups[?contains(Tags[?Key=='Environment'].Value, '$ENVIRONMENT')].AutoScalingGroupName" \
    --output text); do
    scale_asg "$asg" 0 1 0
  done
  
  # Stop RDS instances
  aws rds describe-db-instances \
    --query "DBInstances[?contains(DBInstanceIdentifier, '$ENVIRONMENT')].DBInstanceIdentifier" \
    --output text | xargs -I {} aws rds stop-db-instance --db-instance-identifier {}
    
elif [[ $ACTION == "scale-up" ]]; then
  echo "Scaling up $ENVIRONMENT environment..."
  # Restore from saved configuration
fi
64.6 Cost Allocation Tag Automation Tagging compliance
▶️ Tag Enforcement Script
REQUIRED_TAGS = ['Environment', 'Team', 'CostCenter', 'Application']

def audit_and_tag_resources():
    """Find untagged resources and apply default tags"""
    tagging = boto3.client('resourcegroupstaggingapi')
    
    # Get all resources
    resources = tagging.get_resources()['ResourceTagMappingList']
    
    untagged = []
    for resource in resources:
        current_tags = {t['Key']: t['Value'] for t in resource.get('Tags', [])}
        missing = [t for t in REQUIRED_TAGS if t not in current_tags]
        
        if missing:
            untagged.append({
                'arn': resource['ResourceARN'],
                'missing': missing
            })
    
    # Generate report
    print(f"Found {len(untagged)} resources with missing tags")
    
    # Auto-tag with defaults (if enabled)
    default_tags = {'Environment': 'unknown', 'Team': 'unassigned'}
    for resource in untagged:
        tags_to_add = {k: default_tags.get(k, 'pending') 
                       for k in resource['missing'] if k in default_tags}
        if tags_to_add:
            tagging.tag_resources(
                ResourceARNList=[resource['arn']],
                Tags=tags_to_add
            )
    
    return untagged

Chapter 65: Disaster Recovery Automation

Backup, replication, and DR automation

Chapter Topics

65.1 Backup Automation Scripts Automated backup systems
🔄 Disaster Recovery Architecture 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 DISASTER RECOVERY AUTOMATION                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  PRIMARY REGION (us-east-1)          DR REGION (us-west-2)          │
│  ┌─────────────────────┐             ┌─────────────────────┐        │
│  │    Production       │             │    DR Standby       │        │
│  │  ┌─────┐  ┌─────┐  │             │  ┌─────┐  ┌─────┐  │        │
│  │  │ App │  │ DB  │  │────────────▶│  │ App │  │ DB  │  │        │
│  │  │     │  │(RDS)│  │  Replication│  │(min)│  │(Read)│  │        │
│  │  └─────┘  └─────┘  │             │  └─────┘  └─────┘  │        │
│  │       │       │    │             │                     │        │
│  │       ▼       ▼    │             │                     │        │
│  │    ┌───────────┐   │             │                     │        │
│  │    │ S3 Backup │───┼────────────▶│    S3 Replica      │        │
│  │    └───────────┘   │             │                     │        │
│  └─────────────────────┘             └─────────────────────┘        │
│                                                                      │
│  RPO: 15 min (async replication)  │  RTO: 30 min (automated)       │
│                                                                      │
│  BACKUP SCHEDULE:                                                    │
│  • Continuous: Database replication                                  │
│  • Hourly: Application snapshots                                     │
│  • Daily: Full system backup                                         │
│  • Weekly: Offsite archive                                           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ AWS Backup Automation
import boto3
from datetime import datetime

def create_backup_plan():
    """Create comprehensive AWS Backup plan"""
    backup = boto3.client('backup')
    
    plan = backup.create_backup_plan(
        BackupPlan={
            'BackupPlanName': 'production-backup',
            'Rules': [
                {
                    'RuleName': 'DailyBackup',
                    'TargetBackupVaultName': 'production-vault',
                    'ScheduleExpression': 'cron(0 5 ? * * *)',
                    'StartWindowMinutes': 60,
                    'CompletionWindowMinutes': 120,
                    'Lifecycle': {
                        'MoveToColdStorageAfterDays': 30,
                        'DeleteAfterDays': 365
                    },
                    'CopyActions': [{
                        'DestinationBackupVaultArn': 'arn:aws:backup:us-west-2:...:vault',
                        'Lifecycle': {'DeleteAfterDays': 90}
                    }]
                }
            ]
        }
    )
    return plan['BackupPlanId']
65.2 Cross-Region Replication Geographic redundancy
▶️ S3 Cross-Region Replication
def setup_s3_replication(source_bucket, dest_bucket, dest_region):
    """Configure S3 cross-region replication"""
    s3 = boto3.client('s3')
    
    # Enable versioning (required for replication)
    s3.put_bucket_versioning(
        Bucket=source_bucket,
        VersioningConfiguration={'Status': 'Enabled'}
    )
    
    # Configure replication
    s3.put_bucket_replication(
        Bucket=source_bucket,
        ReplicationConfiguration={
            'Role': f'arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT:role/s3-replication-role',
            'Rules': [{
                'ID': 'ReplicateAll',
                'Status': 'Enabled',
                'Priority': 1,
                'Filter': {'Prefix': ''},
                'Destination': {
                    'Bucket': f'arn:aws:s3:::{dest_bucket}',
                    'ReplicationTime': {
                        'Status': 'Enabled',
                        'Time': {'Minutes': 15}
                    },
                    'Metrics': {'Status': 'Enabled'}
                },
                'DeleteMarkerReplication': {'Status': 'Enabled'}
            }]
        }
    )
65.3 DR Failover Scripts Automated failover
▶️ DR Failover Automation
#!/bin/bash
# dr-failover.sh - Automated disaster recovery failover

set -e
DR_REGION="us-west-2"
PRIMARY_REGION="us-east-1"

echo "=== INITIATING DR FAILOVER ==="

# 1. Promote RDS Read Replica
echo "Promoting RDS replica in $DR_REGION..."
aws rds promote-read-replica \
    --db-instance-identifier "app-db-replica" \
    --region "$DR_REGION"

# 2. Scale up DR ASG
echo "Scaling up DR infrastructure..."
aws autoscaling update-auto-scaling-group \
    --auto-scaling-group-name "app-asg-dr" \
    --min-size 3 --max-size 10 --desired-capacity 5 \
    --region "$DR_REGION"

# 3. Update Route53 to DR region
echo "Updating DNS..."
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id "$ZONE_ID" \
    --change-batch '{
        "Changes": [{
            "Action": "UPSERT",
            "ResourceRecordSet": {
                "Name": "api.example.com",
                "Type": "CNAME",
                "TTL": 60,
                "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "alb-dr.us-west-2.elb.amazonaws.com"}]
            }
        }]
    }'

echo "=== FAILOVER COMPLETE ==="
65.4 Recovery Testing Automation DR drills
▶️ DR Test Framework
import boto3
import time
from datetime import datetime

class DRTestFramework:
    def __init__(self, config):
        self.config = config
        self.results = []
    
    def run_dr_drill(self):
        """Execute full DR test drill"""
        start = datetime.now()
        
        # Test 1: Restore from backup
        restore_result = self.test_backup_restore()
        self.results.append(('Backup Restore', restore_result))
        
        # Test 2: Database failover
        db_result = self.test_db_failover()
        self.results.append(('DB Failover', db_result))
        
        # Test 3: Application health in DR
        app_result = self.test_dr_application()
        self.results.append(('DR App Health', app_result))
        
        elapsed = (datetime.now() - start).total_seconds()
        
        return {
            'rto_achieved': elapsed,
            'rto_target': self.config['rto_minutes'] * 60,
            'passed': elapsed < self.config['rto_minutes'] * 60,
            'results': self.results
        }
    
    def test_backup_restore(self):
        # Restore latest backup to test instance
        pass
65.5 RTO/RPO Monitoring Recovery metrics
▶️ RTO/RPO Dashboard Metrics
def calculate_rpo_metrics():
    """Calculate current RPO based on replication lag"""
    cw = boto3.client('cloudwatch')
    
    # RDS replication lag
    rds_lag = cw.get_metric_statistics(
        Namespace='AWS/RDS',
        MetricName='ReplicaLag',
        Dimensions=[{'Name': 'DBInstanceIdentifier', 'Value': 'replica'}],
        StartTime=datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=1),
        EndTime=datetime.utcnow(),
        Period=300,
        Statistics=['Maximum']
    )
    
    # S3 replication pending
    s3_pending = cw.get_metric_statistics(
        Namespace='AWS/S3',
        MetricName='ReplicationLatency',
        Dimensions=[{'Name': 'SourceBucket', 'Value': 'primary-bucket'}],
        StartTime=datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=1),
        EndTime=datetime.utcnow(),
        Period=300,
        Statistics=['Maximum']
    )
    
    max_rds_lag = max([p['Maximum'] for p in rds_lag['Datapoints']], default=0)
    max_s3_lag = max([p['Maximum'] for p in s3_pending['Datapoints']], default=0)
    
    current_rpo = max(max_rds_lag, max_s3_lag / 60)  # Convert to minutes
    
    return {
        'current_rpo_minutes': round(current_rpo, 2),
        'target_rpo_minutes': 15,
        'compliant': current_rpo <= 15
    }
65.6 Data Integrity Verification Backup validation
▶️ Backup Integrity Checker
import hashlib
import boto3

def verify_backup_integrity(backup_id):
    """Verify backup integrity via checksum and test restore"""
    backup = boto3.client('backup')
    
    # Get backup metadata
    job = backup.describe_backup_job(BackupJobId=backup_id)
    
    # Start restore to verification environment
    restore = backup.start_restore_job(
        RecoveryPointArn=job['RecoveryPointArn'],
        IamRoleArn='arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT:role/backup-verify-role',
        Metadata={
            'DBInstanceIdentifier': f"verify-{backup_id[:8]}",
            'DBInstanceClass': 'db.t3.micro',  # Minimal for verify
        }
    )
    
    # Wait for restore
    wait_for_restore(restore['RestoreJobId'])
    
    # Run integrity checks
    checks = {
        'restore_successful': True,
        'row_count_match': verify_row_counts(),
        'checksum_valid': verify_checksums(),
        'sample_query_success': run_sample_queries()
    }
    
    # Cleanup verification instance
    cleanup_verification_resources()
    
    return all(checks.values()), checks

Chapter 66: Multi-Cloud Automation

Cloud-agnostic automation patterns

Chapter Topics

66.1 Cloud-Agnostic Scripting
66.2 Terraform Multi-Cloud
66.3 Kubernetes Multi-Cluster
66.4 Cross-Cloud Networking
66.5 Unified Monitoring Setup
66.1 Cloud-Agnostic Scripting Portable automation
☁️ Multi-Cloud Architecture Pattern 
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    MULTI-CLOUD AUTOMATION                          │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                     │
│  ABSTRACTION LAYER                                                  │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │     Terraform     │     Pulumi     │    Crossplane          │   │
│  │    (HCL/JSON)     │   (Python/TS)  │    (K8s CRDs)          │   │
│  └──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                              │                                      │
│                    Provider Plugins                                 │
│           ┌─────────────────┼─────────────────┐                    │
│           ▼                 ▼                 ▼                    │
│  ┌────────────────┐ ┌────────────────┐ ┌────────────────┐         │
│  │      AWS       │ │     Azure      │ │      GCP       │         │
│  │  ┌──────────┐  │ │  ┌──────────┐  │ │  ┌──────────┐  │         │
│  │  │ EC2/EKS  │  │ │  │ VM/AKS   │  │ │  │ GCE/GKE  │  │         │
│  │  │ S3/RDS   │  │ │  │ Blob/SQL │  │ │  │ GCS/SQL  │  │         │
│  │  └──────────┘  │ │  └──────────┘  │ │  └──────────┘  │         │
│  └────────────────┘ └────────────────┘ └────────────────┘         │
│                                                                     │
│  COMMON PATTERNS: VPC=VNet=VPC, LB=LB=LB, K8s=AKS=GKE             │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Cloud-Agnostic Client
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

class CloudProvider(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    def create_vm(self, config): pass
    
    @abstractmethod
    def create_storage(self, config): pass

class AWSProvider(CloudProvider):
    def __init__(self):
        import boto3
        self.ec2 = boto3.client('ec2')
        self.s3 = boto3.client('s3')
    
    def create_vm(self, config):
        return self.ec2.run_instances(**self._map_config(config))

class AzureProvider(CloudProvider):
    def __init__(self):
        from azure.mgmt.compute import ComputeManagementClient
        self.compute = ComputeManagementClient(credential, subscription_id)
    
    def create_vm(self, config):
        return self.compute.virtual_machines.begin_create_or_update(...)

# Usage
provider = AWSProvider() if cloud == 'aws' else AzureProvider()
provider.create_vm({'name': 'web-1', 'size': 'medium'})
66.2 Terraform Multi-Cloud Multi-provider infrastructure
▶️ Multi-Cloud Terraform
# Multi-cloud Kubernetes clusters
provider "aws" {
  region = "us-east-1"
  alias  = "aws"
}

provider "azurerm" {
  features {}
  alias   = "azure"
}

provider "google" {
  project = var.gcp_project
  region  = "us-central1"
  alias   = "gcp"
}

module "eks" {
  source    = "./modules/eks"
  providers = { aws = aws.aws }
  cluster_name = "prod-eks"
}

module "aks" {
  source    = "./modules/aks"
  providers = { azurerm = azurerm.azure }
  cluster_name = "prod-aks"
}

module "gke" {
  source    = "./modules/gke"
  providers = { google = google.gcp }
  cluster_name = "prod-gke"
}

# Shared outputs
output "cluster_endpoints" {
  value = {
    aws   = module.eks.endpoint
    azure = module.aks.endpoint
    gcp   = module.gke.endpoint
  }
}
66.3 Kubernetes Multi-Cluster Federation and management
▶️ Multi-Cluster Management
#!/bin/bash
# multi-cluster-deploy.sh

CLUSTERS=("eks-prod" "aks-prod" "gke-prod")
APP_MANIFEST="deployment.yaml"

for cluster in "${CLUSTERS[@]}"; do
  echo "Deploying to $cluster..."
  
  # Set context
  kubectl config use-context "$cluster"
  
  # Apply manifests
  kubectl apply -f "$APP_MANIFEST"
  
  # Verify deployment
  kubectl rollout status deployment/myapp --timeout=300s
done

# Verify all clusters
echo "=== Cluster Status ==="
for cluster in "${CLUSTERS[@]}"; do
  kubectl config use-context "$cluster"
  echo "$cluster: $(kubectl get deploy myapp -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}')/$(kubectl get deploy myapp -o jsonpath='{.spec.replicas}') ready"
done
66.4 Cross-Cloud Networking VPN and connectivity
▶️ Cross-Cloud VPN Setup
# AWS Transit Gateway to Azure VPN
resource "aws_vpn_gateway" "aws_vpn" {
  vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id
  tags   = { Name = "aws-to-azure-vpn" }
}

resource "aws_customer_gateway" "azure" {
  bgp_asn    = 65000
  ip_address = azurerm_public_ip.vpn.ip_address
  type       = "ipsec.1"
}

resource "aws_vpn_connection" "aws_azure" {
  vpn_gateway_id      = aws_vpn_gateway.aws_vpn.id
  customer_gateway_id = aws_customer_gateway.azure.id
  type                = "ipsec.1"
  static_routes_only  = false
}

# Azure side
resource "azurerm_virtual_network_gateway" "vpn" {
  name                = "azure-to-aws-vpn"
  location            = azurerm_resource_group.main.location
  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.main.name
  type                = "Vpn"
  vpn_type            = "RouteBased"
  sku                 = "VpnGw1"
}
66.5 Unified Monitoring Setup Cross-cloud observability
▶️ Unified Monitoring with Grafana
# grafana-datasources.yaml
apiVersion: 1
datasources:
  - name: AWS-CloudWatch
    type: cloudwatch
    access: proxy
    jsonData:
      authType: arn
      assumeRoleArn: arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT:role/grafana-role
      defaultRegion: us-east-1
  
  - name: Azure-Monitor
    type: grafana-azure-monitor-datasource
    jsonData:
      cloudName: azuremonitor
      tenantId: $AZURE_TENANT_ID
      clientId: $AZURE_CLIENT_ID
  
  - name: GCP-Stackdriver
    type: stackdriver
    jsonData:
      authenticationType: gce
      defaultProject: $GCP_PROJECT

  - name: Prometheus-All
    type: prometheus
    url: http://thanos-query:9090
    jsonData:
      httpMethod: POST
      # Thanos aggregates from all clusters

Chapter 67: AI/ML for Automation

Machine learning powered automation

Chapter Topics

67.1 Predictive Scaling
67.2 Anomaly Detection Scripts
67.3 Log Analysis with ML
67.4 ChatOps with AI
67.5 Auto-Remediation with ML
67.1 Predictive Scaling ML-based capacity planning
▶️ Predictive Autoscaling
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestRegressor
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

class PredictiveScaler:
    def __init__(self):
        self.model = RandomForestRegressor(n_estimators=100)
    
    def train(self, historical_data):
        """Train on historical metrics"""
        df = pd.DataFrame(historical_data)
        df['hour'] = df['timestamp'].dt.hour
        df['day_of_week'] = df['timestamp'].dt.dayofweek
        
        X = df[['hour', 'day_of_week', 'current_load']]
        y = df['required_instances']
        
        self.model.fit(X, y)
    
    def predict_capacity(self, hours_ahead=1):
        """Predict required capacity"""
        future = datetime.now() + timedelta(hours=hours_ahead)
        
        features = [[
            future.hour,
            future.weekday(),
            get_current_load()
        ]]
        
        predicted = self.model.predict(features)[0]
        return int(predicted)
    
    def auto_scale(self):
        """Execute predictive scaling"""
        predicted = self.predict_capacity(hours_ahead=1)
        current = get_current_instances()
        
        if predicted > current:
            scale_up(predicted - current)
        elif predicted < current * 0.7:
            scale_down(current - predicted)
67.2 Anomaly Detection Scripts Automated issue detection
▶️ Anomaly Detection
from sklearn.ensemble import IsolationForest
import numpy as np

class MetricAnomalyDetector:
    def __init__(self, contamination=0.1):
        self.model = IsolationForest(
            contamination=contamination,
            random_state=42
        )
    
    def train(self, normal_metrics):
        """Train on normal behavior"""
        self.model.fit(normal_metrics)
    
    def detect(self, current_metrics):
        """Detect anomalies in current metrics"""
        predictions = self.model.predict(current_metrics)
        anomalies = np.where(predictions == -1)[0]
        
        if len(anomalies) > 0:
            return {
                'is_anomaly': True,
                'anomaly_indices': anomalies.tolist(),
                'score': self.model.score_samples(current_metrics).mean()
            }
        return {'is_anomaly': False}

# Usage
detector = MetricAnomalyDetector()
detector.train(historical_cpu_memory_data)

# Real-time detection
current = [[cpu_pct, memory_pct, disk_io, network_io]]
result = detector.detect(current)
if result['is_anomaly']:
    send_alert(f"Anomaly detected: {result}")
67.3 Log Analysis with ML Intelligent log parsing
▶️ ML Log Analyzer
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

class LogAnalyzer:
    def __init__(self):
        self.vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(max_features=1000)
        self.clusterer = KMeans(n_clusters=10)
    
    def analyze_logs(self, log_lines):
        """Cluster similar log patterns"""
        vectors = self.vectorizer.fit_transform(log_lines)
        clusters = self.clusterer.fit_predict(vectors)
        
        # Group logs by cluster
        grouped = {}
        for i, cluster_id in enumerate(clusters):
            if cluster_id not in grouped:
                grouped[cluster_id] = []
            grouped[cluster_id].append(log_lines[i])
        
        return grouped
    
    def find_error_patterns(self, logs):
        """Identify common error patterns"""
        error_logs = [l for l in logs if 'ERROR' in l or 'Exception' in l]
        
        if not error_logs:
            return []
        
        clusters = self.analyze_logs(error_logs)
        
        # Return top error patterns
        return sorted(
            [(len(v), v[0][:100]) for v in clusters.values()],
            reverse=True
        )[:5]
67.4 ChatOps with AI AI-powered operations
▶️ AI Operations Bot
import openai
from slack_sdk import WebClient

class AIOpsBot:
    def __init__(self, slack_token, openai_key):
        self.slack = WebClient(token=slack_token)
        openai.api_key = openai_key
        
        self.system_prompt = """You are an DevOps assistant.
        You can help with: deployments, monitoring, troubleshooting.
        Available commands: deploy, status, logs, rollback, scale"""
    
    def process_message(self, message, channel):
        """Process user message with AI"""
        response = openai.ChatCompletion.create(
            model="gpt-4",
            messages=[
                {"role": "system", "content": self.system_prompt},
                {"role": "user", "content": message}
            ],
            functions=[
                {"name": "deploy", "parameters": {"service": "string"}},
                {"name": "get_status", "parameters": {"service": "string"}},
                {"name": "scale", "parameters": {"service": "string", "replicas": "int"}}
            ]
        )
        
        if response.choices[0].finish_reason == "function_call":
            func = response.choices[0].message.function_call
            result = self.execute_function(func.name, func.arguments)
            self.slack.chat_postMessage(channel=channel, text=result)
67.5 Auto-Remediation with ML Self-healing systems
▶️ Auto-Remediation Engine
class AutoRemediationEngine:
    def __init__(self):
        self.remediation_history = []
        self.patterns = self.load_remediation_patterns()
    
    def analyze_and_remediate(self, alert):
        """Analyze alert and execute remediation"""
        # Match alert to known patterns
        pattern = self.match_pattern(alert)
        
        if pattern and pattern['confidence'] > 0.8:
            # Auto-remediate high confidence matches
            result = self.execute_remediation(pattern['action'], alert)
            self.log_remediation(alert, pattern, result)
            return result
        
        # For low confidence, suggest action
        return {'action': 'manual_review', 'suggested': pattern}
    
    def execute_remediation(self, action, alert):
        """Execute remediation action"""
        actions = {
            'restart_pod': lambda: restart_pod(alert['pod']),
            'scale_up': lambda: scale_deployment(alert['deployment'], +2),
            'clear_disk': lambda: cleanup_logs(alert['node']),
            'failover_db': lambda: trigger_db_failover(alert['db']),
        }
        
        if action in actions:
            return actions[action]()
        
    def learn_from_resolution(self, alert, resolution):
        """Update patterns from manual resolutions"""
        self.remediation_history.append({
            'alert': alert, 'resolution': resolution,
            'timestamp': datetime.now()
        })
        self.retrain_model()

68.1 Requirements and Architecture Project planning
🏗️ Server Provisioning Pipeline Architecture 
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│            COMPLETE SERVER PROVISIONING PIPELINE                    │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                     │
│  REQUEST           PROVISION           CONFIGURE         DEPLOY    │
│  ┌───────┐       ┌───────────┐       ┌───────────┐    ┌─────────┐ │
│  │ Form/ │──────▶│ Terraform │──────▶│  Ansible  │───▶│ Health  │ │
│  │ API   │       │ (Infra)   │       │ (Config)  │    │ Check   │ │
│  └───────┘       └───────────┘       └───────────┘    └─────────┘ │
│      │                │                    │               │       │
│      ▼                ▼                    ▼               ▼       │
│  ┌───────┐       ┌───────────┐       ┌───────────┐    ┌─────────┐ │
│  │Validate│      │ VPC, SG,  │       │ Packages, │    │ Smoke   │ │
│  │ Input │       │ EC2, EBS  │       │ Users,SSL │    │ Tests   │ │
│  └───────┘       └───────────┘       └───────────┘    └─────────┘ │
│                                                                     │
│  PIPELINE: GitHub Actions / Jenkins / GitLab CI                    │
│                                                                     │
│  OUTPUTS:                                                           │
│  • Server ready with standard configuration                        │
│  • Monitoring enabled (CloudWatch, Prometheus)                     │
│  • Backup configured                                                │
│  • Documentation auto-generated                                     │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Project Structure
server-provisioning/
├── terraform/
│   ├── main.tf
│   ├── variables.tf

│   │   ├── vpc/
│   │   ├── ec2/

│       └── prod.tfvars

68.2 Terraform Infrastructure Code IaC implementation
▶️ Terraform Main
# terraform/main.tf
terraform {
  required_providers {
    aws = { source = "hashicorp/aws", version = "~> 5.0" }
  }
  backend "s3" {
    bucket = "terraform-state-bucket"
    key    = "server-provisioning/terraform.tfstate"
  }
}

module "vpc" {
  source     = "./modules/vpc"
  cidr_block = var.vpc_cidr
  azs        = var.availability_zones
}

module "security" {
  source = "./modules/security"
  vpc_id = module.vpc.vpc_id
  allowed_ssh_cidrs = var.ssh_cidrs
}

module "server" {
  source            = "./modules/ec2"
  for_each          = var.servers
  
  name              = each.key
  instance_type     = each.value.instance_type
  ami_id            = data.aws_ami.ubuntu.id
  subnet_id         = module.vpc.private_subnets[0]
  security_groups   = [module.security.app_sg_id]
  key_name          = var.ssh_key_name
  
  tags = merge(var.common_tags, each.value.tags)
}

output "server_ips" {
  value = { for k, v in module.server : k => v.private_ip }
}
68.3 Ansible Configuration Playbooks Server configuration
▶️ Base Configuration Playbook
# ansible/playbooks/base-config.yml
- name: Base Server Configuration
  hosts: all
  become: true
  vars:
    packages:
      - docker.io
      - nginx
      - fail2ban
      - unattended-upgrades
  
  roles:
    - common
    - users
    - security-hardening
  
  tasks:
    - name: Install required packages
      apt:
        name: "{{ packages }}"
        state: present
        update_cache: yes
    
    - name: Configure automatic updates
      copy:
        content: |
          APT::Periodic::Update-Package-Lists "1";
          APT::Periodic::Unattended-Upgrade "1";
        dest: /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/20auto-upgrades
    
    - name: Start and enable Docker
      systemd:
        name: docker
        state: started
        enabled: yes
    
    - name: Deploy monitoring agent
      include_role:
        name: monitoring-agent
68.4 CI/CD Pipeline Integration Automated provisioning
▶️ GitHub Actions Pipeline
# .github/workflows/provision.yml
name: Server Provisioning
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      environment:
        required: true
        type: choice
        options: [dev, staging, prod]
      server_count:
        required: true
        type: number

jobs:
  terraform:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Terraform Init
        run: terraform init
        working-directory: terraform
      
      - name: Terraform Plan
        run: terraform plan -var-file="${{ inputs.environment }}.tfvars"
        working-directory: terraform
      
      - name: Terraform Apply
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: terraform apply -auto-approve
        working-directory: terraform
      
      - name: Export Inventory
        run: terraform output -json > ../ansible/inventory.json
  
  configure:
    needs: terraform
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Run Ansible
        run: ansible-playbook -i inventory.json playbooks/base-config.yml
68.5 Testing and Validation Infrastructure tests
▶️ Terratest + Integration
// tests/terraform/server_test.go
package test

import (
    "testing"
    "github.com/gruntwork-io/terratest/modules/terraform"
    "github.com/gruntwork-io/terratest/modules/ssh"
)

func TestServerProvisioning(t *testing.T) {
    opts := &terraform.Options{
        TerraformDir: "../../terraform",
        Vars: map[string]interface{}{
            "environment": "test",
        },
    }
    
    defer terraform.Destroy(t, opts)
    terraform.InitAndApply(t, opts)
    
    // Verify server is accessible
    serverIP := terraform.Output(t, opts, "server_ip")
    
    sshHost := ssh.Host{
        Hostname: serverIP,
        Username: "ubuntu",
    }
    
    // Test SSH connectivity
    ssh.CheckSshConnection(t, sshHost)
    
    // Test services running
    output := ssh.CheckSshCommand(t, sshHost, "systemctl is-active docker")
    assert.Equal(t, "active", strings.TrimSpace(output))
}
68.6 Documentation and Runbooks Operational docs
▶️ Auto-Generate Runbook
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate runbook from Terraform and Ansible"""

def generate_runbook(tf_output, ansible_inventory):
    """Create operational runbook"""
    
    runbook = f"""# Server Provisioning Runbook

## Infrastructure Summary
- VPC: {tf_output['vpc_id']}
- Servers: {len(tf_output['server_ips'])}

## Server Details
| Name | IP | Type | Purpose |
|------|----|----- |---------|
"""
    
    for name, ip in tf_output['server_ips'].items():
        runbook += f"| {name} | {ip} | {get_type(name)} | {get_purpose(name)} |\n"
    
    runbook += """
## Common Operations

### SSH Access
```bash
ssh -i ~/.ssh/key.pem ubuntu@
```

### Service Management
```bash
sudo systemctl status docker
sudo systemctl restart nginx
```

### Logs
```bash
sudo journalctl -u docker -f
sudo tail -f /var/log/nginx/access.log
```
"""
    
    return runbook

69.1 Account Structure and Organization AWS Organizations setup
🏢 AWS Multi-Account Structure 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 AWS MULTI-ACCOUNT FRAMEWORK                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│                        Management Account                            │
│                    ┌─────────────────────────┐                       │
│                    │  AWS Organizations      │                       │
│                    │  Control Tower          │                       │
│                    │  Billing & Costs        │                       │
│                    └───────────┬─────────────┘                       │
│                                │                                     │
│        ┌───────────────────────┼───────────────────────┐            │
│        │                       │                       │            │
│  ┌─────▼─────┐          ┌──────▼──────┐         ┌─────▼─────┐       │
│  │ Security  │          │  Shared     │         │ Workload  │       │
│  │   OU      │          │ Services OU │         │   OU      │       │
│  └─────┬─────┘          └──────┬──────┘         └─────┬─────┘       │
│        │                       │                      │             │
│  ┌─────▼─────┐          ┌──────▼──────┐    ┌─────────┴────────┐    │
│  │Log Archive│          │  Shared     │    │  Dev │ Stage│Prod│    │
│  │Security   │          │  Infra      │    └──────┴──────┴────┘    │
│  │Audit      │          │  Network    │                            │
│  └───────────┘          └─────────────┘                            │
│                                                                      │
│  GOVERNANCE: SCPs, Permission Boundaries, Guardrails               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Organization Setup
# AWS Organizations Terraform
resource "aws_organizations_organization" "org" {
  feature_set = "ALL"
  enabled_policy_types = [
    "SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY",
    "TAG_POLICY"
  ]
}

resource "aws_organizations_organizational_unit" "security" {
  name      = "Security"
  parent_id = aws_organizations_organization.org.roots[0].id
}

resource "aws_organizations_organizational_unit" "workloads" {
  name      = "Workloads"
  parent_id = aws_organizations_organization.org.roots[0].id
}

resource "aws_organizations_account" "dev" {
  name      = "dev-account"
  email     = "aws-dev@company.com"
  parent_id = aws_organizations_organizational_unit.workloads.id
  tags      = { Environment = "dev" }
}
69.2 Cross-Account Role Setup IAM federation
▶️ Cross-Account Automation Role
# Role in target account
resource "aws_iam_role" "automation" {
  name = "CrossAccountAutomation"
  
  assume_role_policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Action = "sts:AssumeRole"
      Effect = "Allow"
      Principal = {
        AWS = "arn:aws:iam::${var.management_account}:root"
      }
      Condition = {
        StringEquals = {
          "sts:ExternalId" = var.external_id
        }
      }
    }]
  })
}

resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "automation" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.automation.name
  policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/PowerUserAccess"
}

# Usage from management account
data "aws_iam_policy_document" "assume_roles" {
  statement {
    actions   = ["sts:AssumeRole"]
    resources = ["arn:aws:iam::*:role/CrossAccountAutomation"]
  }
}
69.3 Centralized Automation Scripts Multi-account operations
▶️ Multi-Account Runner
import boto3

class MultiAccountRunner:
    def __init__(self, management_session):
        self.org = management_session.client('organizations')
        self.sts = management_session.client('sts')
    
    def get_all_accounts(self):
        """List all accounts in organization"""
        accounts = []
        paginator = self.org.get_paginator('list_accounts')
        for page in paginator.paginate():
            accounts.extend(page['Accounts'])
        return [a for a in accounts if a['Status'] == 'ACTIVE']
    
    def assume_role(self, account_id, role_name='CrossAccountAutomation'):
        """Assume role in target account"""
        response = self.sts.assume_role(
            RoleArn=f'arn:aws:iam::{account_id}:role/{role_name}',
            RoleSessionName='MultiAccountAutomation'
        )
        return boto3.Session(
            aws_access_key_id=response['Credentials']['AccessKeyId'],
            aws_secret_access_key=response['Credentials']['SecretAccessKey'],
            aws_session_token=response['Credentials']['SessionToken']
        )
    
    def run_across_accounts(self, operation):
        """Execute operation across all accounts"""
        results = {}
        for account in self.get_all_accounts():
            session = self.assume_role(account['Id'])
            results[account['Id']] = operation(session)
        return results
69.4 Compliance and Governance SCPs and guardrails
▶️ Service Control Policy
# Deny non-approved regions
resource "aws_organizations_policy" "deny_regions" {
  name = "DenyNonApprovedRegions"
  type = "SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY"
  
  content = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Sid    = "DenyNonApprovedRegions"
      Effect = "Deny"
      NotAction = [
        "iam:*",
        "organizations:*",
        "support:*"
      ]
      Resource = "*"
      Condition = {
        StringNotEquals = {
          "aws:RequestedRegion" = ["us-east-1", "us-west-2", "eu-west-1"]
        }
      }
    }]
  })
}

# Require encryption
resource "aws_organizations_policy" "require_encryption" {
  name = "RequireS3Encryption"
  type = "SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY"
  
  content = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Sid      = "DenyUnencryptedS3Uploads"
      Effect   = "Deny"
      Action   = "s3:PutObject"
      Resource = "*"
      Condition = {
        Null = { "s3:x-amz-server-side-encryption" = "true" }
      }
    }]
  })
}
69.5 Cost Management Automation Organization-wide costs
▶️ Organization Cost Report
def generate_org_cost_report():
    """Generate cost report across all accounts"""
    ce = boto3.client('ce')
    
    response = ce.get_cost_and_usage(
        TimePeriod={
            'Start': (datetime.today() - timedelta(days=30)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d'),
            'End': datetime.today().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
        },
        Granularity='MONTHLY',
        Metrics=['UnblendedCost'],
        GroupBy=[
            {'Type': 'DIMENSION', 'Key': 'LINKED_ACCOUNT'},
            {'Type': 'DIMENSION', 'Key': 'SERVICE'}
        ]
    )
    
    report = {}
    for result in response['ResultsByTime']:
        for group in result['Groups']:
            account = group['Keys'][0]
            service = group['Keys'][1]
            cost = float(group['Metrics']['UnblendedCost']['Amount'])
            
            if account not in report:
                report[account] = {'total': 0, 'services': {}}
            report[account]['total'] += cost
            report[account]['services'][service] = cost
    
    # Sort by total cost
    return dict(sorted(report.items(), 
                       key=lambda x: x[1]['total'], reverse=True))
69.6 Cost Allocation and Reporting Financial automation
▶️ Cost Allocation Dashboard
import boto3
import pandas as pd
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

class CostAllocationReport:
    def __init__(self):
        self.ce = boto3.client('cost-explorer')
        self.org = boto3.client('organizations')
        
    def generate_allocation_report(self, months=3):
        end = datetime.now()
        start = end - timedelta(days=months*30)
        
        response = self.ce.get_cost_and_usage(
            TimePeriod={'Start': start.strftime('%Y-%m-%d'),
                        'End': end.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')},
            Granularity='MONTHLY',
            Metrics=['UnblendedCost'],
            GroupBy=[
                {'Type': 'DIMENSION', 'Key': 'LINKED_ACCOUNT'},
                {'Type': 'TAG', 'Key': 'CostCenter'}
            ]
        )
        
        df = pd.DataFrame([
            {'Account': g['Keys'][0], 
             'CostCenter': g['Keys'][1] or 'Untagged',
             'Cost': float(g['Metrics']['UnblendedCost']['Amount'])}
            for r in response['ResultsByTime']
            for g in r['Groups']
        ])
        
        return df.pivot_table(index='Account', columns='CostCenter', 
                              values='Cost', aggfunc='sum', fill_value=0)

70.1 EKS Cluster Automation Kubernetes infrastructure
☸️ GitOps Platform Architecture 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   KUBERNETES GITOPS PLATFORM                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  GIT REPOSITORY                    KUBERNETES CLUSTER                │
│  ┌─────────────────┐              ┌──────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ app-config/     │              │         EKS Cluster           │  │
│  │ ├── base/       │   Sync      │  ┌────────────────────────┐   │  │
│  │ ├── overlays/   │────────────▶│  │      ArgoCD            │   │  │
│  │ │   ├── dev/    │              │  └──────────┬─────────────┘   │  │
│  │ │   ├── stage/  │              │             │                 │  │
│  │ │   └── prod/   │              │  ┌──────────▼─────────────┐   │  │
│  │ └── helm/       │              │  │   App Deployments      │   │  │
│  └─────────────────┘              │  │  ┌─────┐ ┌─────┐      │   │  │
│                                    │  │  │ web │ │ api │      │   │  │
│  CICD PIPELINE                     │  │  └─────┘ └─────┘      │   │  │
│  ┌─────────────────┐              │  └────────────────────────┘   │  │
│  │ Build → Test →  │              │                               │  │
│  │ Push → Update   │──────────   │  MONITORING                    │  │
│  │ Git Manifests   │   │         │  ┌──────────────────────────┐  │  │
│  └─────────────────┘   │         │  │ Prometheus │ Grafana     │  │  │
│                        │         │  └──────────────────────────┘  │  │
│                        ▼         └──────────────────────────────┘  │
│               Image Registry                                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ EKS Terraform Module
module "eks" {
  source  = "terraform-aws-modules/eks/aws"
  version = "~> 19.0"

  cluster_name    = "gitops-platform"
  cluster_version = "1.28"
  
  vpc_id     = module.vpc.vpc_id
  subnet_ids = module.vpc.private_subnets
  
  cluster_endpoint_public_access = true
  
  eks_managed_node_groups = {
    general = {
      min_size     = 2
      max_size     = 10
      desired_size = 3
      instance_types = ["m5.large"]
    }
  }
  
  # Enable IRSA for service accounts
  enable_irsa = true
  
  # Cluster addons
  cluster_addons = {
    coredns    = { most_recent = true }
    kube-proxy = { most_recent = true }
    vpc-cni    = { most_recent = true }
  }
}
70.2 ArgoCD Configuration GitOps controller
▶️ ArgoCD Setup
# argocd-install.yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: argocd
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  project: default
  source:
    repoURL: https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm
    chart: argo-cd
    targetRevision: 5.51.0
    helm:
      values: |
        server:
          ingress:
            enabled: true
            annotations:
              kubernetes.io/ingress.class: alb
              alb.ingress.kubernetes.io/scheme: internet-facing
            hosts:
              - argocd.example.com
        configs:
          repositories:
            app-repo:
              url: https://github.com/org/app-config
              password: ${GITHUB_TOKEN}
              username: git
  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
    namespace: argocd
  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: true
      selfHeal: true
70.3 Application Deployment Pipelines CI/CD with GitOps
▶️ GitOps CI Pipeline
# .github/workflows/build-deploy.yml
name: Build and Deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      image_tag: ${{ steps.tag.outputs.tag }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Build and Push
        run: |
          docker build -t $ECR_REPO:${{ github.sha }} .
          docker push $ECR_REPO:${{ github.sha }}
      
      - id: tag
        run: echo "tag=${{ github.sha }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

  update-manifests:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          repository: org/app-config
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Update Image Tag
        run: |
          cd overlays/prod
          kustomize edit set image app=$ECR_REPO:${{ needs.build.outputs.image_tag }}
      
      - name: Commit and Push
        run: |
          git config user.name "CI Bot"
          git commit -am "Update prod to ${{ needs.build.outputs.image_tag }}"
          git push
70.4 Monitoring and Alerting Setup Observability stack
▶️ Prometheus Stack
# monitoring/kube-prometheus-stack.yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: monitoring
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  project: default
  source:
    repoURL: https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
    chart: kube-prometheus-stack
    targetRevision: 55.0.0
    helm:
      values: |
        grafana:
          adminPassword: ${GRAFANA_PASSWORD}
          ingress:
            enabled: true
            hosts: [grafana.example.com]
        prometheus:
          prometheusSpec:
            retention: 30d
            storageSpec:
              volumeClaimTemplate:
                spec:
                  storageClassName: gp3
                  resources:
                    requests:
                      storage: 100Gi
        alertmanager:
          config:
            receivers:
              - name: slack
                slack_configs:
                  - channel: '#alerts'
                    api_url: ${SLACK_WEBHOOK}
  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
    namespace: monitoring
70.5 Disaster Recovery Plan K8s backup and recovery
▶️ Velero Backup Setup
#!/bin/bash
# Setup Velero for K8s backup

# Install Velero
velero install \
    --provider aws \
    --plugins velero/velero-plugin-for-aws:v1.8.0 \
    --bucket velero-backups \
    --backup-location-config region=us-east-1 \
    --snapshot-location-config region=us-east-1 \
    --secret-file ./credentials-velero

# Create scheduled backup
velero schedule create daily-backup \
    --schedule="0 2 * * *" \
    --ttl 720h \
    --include-namespaces production,staging

# Create DR procedure
cat > dr-restore.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
BACKUP_NAME=$1
echo "Restoring from backup: $BACKUP_NAME"

# Restore cluster state
velero restore create --from-backup $BACKUP_NAME

# Verify apps synced
kubectl get applications -n argocd

# Force ArgoCD sync
argocd app sync --all
EOF
chmod +x dr-restore.sh
70.6 Observability Stack Deployment Monitoring infrastructure
▶️ Prometheus + Grafana Stack
# observability/kustomization.yaml
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization

namespace: monitoring
resources:
  - namespace.yaml
  - prometheus/
  - grafana/
  - alertmanager/

helmCharts:
  - name: kube-prometheus-stack
    repo: https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
    version: 45.7.1
    releaseName: prometheus
    namespace: monitoring
    valuesFile: prometheus-values.yaml
    
  - name: loki-stack
    repo: https://grafana.github.io/helm-charts
    version: 2.9.10
    releaseName: loki
    namespace: monitoring
    valuesInline:
      promtail:
        enabled: true
      loki:
        persistence:
          enabled: true
          size: 50Gi

│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │                           SOURCE CONTROL                                 │    │

│  │  │  Feature  │───▶│   Pull    │───▶│   Code    │───▶│  Merge    │      │    │
│  │  │  Branch   │    │  Request  │    │  Review   │    │  to Main  │      │    │

│                                      │                                           │
