│  │  │   Build   │    │   Test    │    │   to ECR  │    │   Scan    │      │    │
│  │  └───────────┘    └───────────┘    └───────────┘    └───────────┘      │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                      │                                           │
│                                      ▼                                           │

│  │  │  Deploy   │    │  Deploy   │    │   Gate    │    │  Deploy   │      │    │

└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Section 71.1: Source Control and Branching Strategy

Scenario: Establish Git branching strategy with automated workflows for Django project using GitFlow with environment-based deployments.

# ============================================================================
# GitLab CI/CD Configuration for Django Application
# File: .gitlab-ci.yml
# Branching Strategy: GitFlow (main, develop, feature/*, release/*, hotfix/*)
# ============================================================================

# Global variables
variables:
  DOCKER_DRIVER: overlay2
  DOCKER_TLS_CERTDIR: "/certs"
  APP_NAME: "django-jobpost"
  PYTHON_VERSION: "3.11"
  AWS_REGION: "us-east-1"
  ECR_REGISTRY: "${AWS_ACCOUNT_ID}.dkr.ecr.${AWS_REGION}.amazonaws.com"

# Stages definition
stages:
  - lint
  - test
  - security
  - build
  - deploy-dev
  - deploy-staging
  - approve
  - deploy-production
  - rollback

# Default image and services
default:
  image: python:${PYTHON_VERSION}
  cache:
    key: ${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}
    paths:
      - .cache/pip
      - venv/

# Branch workflow rules
.feature_branch: &feature_branch
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH =~ /^feature\/.*$/'
      when: always

.develop_branch: &develop_branch
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "develop"'
      when: always

.release_branch: &release_branch
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH =~ /^release\/.*$/'
      when: always

.main_branch: &main_branch
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
      when: always

.hotfix_branch: &hotfix_branch
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH =~ /^hotfix\/.*$/'
      when: always

# ============================================================================
# Branch Protection Script
# File: scripts/setup_branch_protection.sh
# ============================================================================

#!/bin/bash
# Configure GitLab branch protection via API

set -euo pipefail

GITLAB_URL="${GITLAB_URL:-https://gitlab.com}"
PROJECT_ID="${CI_PROJECT_ID}"
PRIVATE_TOKEN="${GITLAB_TOKEN}"

# Function to protect branch
protect_branch() {
    local branch_name="$1"
    local push_level="$2"
    local merge_level="$3"
    local allow_force_push="${4:-false}"
    
    echo "🔒 Protecting branch: ${branch_name}"
    
    curl --request POST \
        --header "PRIVATE-TOKEN: ${PRIVATE_TOKEN}" \
        --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
        --data "{
            \"name\": \"${branch_name}\",
            \"push_access_level\": ${push_level},
            \"merge_access_level\": ${merge_level},
            \"allow_force_push\": ${allow_force_push},
            \"code_owner_approval_required\": true
        }" \
        "${GITLAB_URL}/api/v4/projects/${PROJECT_ID}/protected_branches" || true
}

# Protect main branch (no direct push, only maintainers merge)
protect_branch "main" 0 40 false

# Protect develop (developers can push, developers can merge)
protect_branch "develop" 30 30 false

# Protect release branches
protect_branch "release/*" 40 40 false

# Protect hotfix branches
protect_branch "hotfix/*" 40 40 false

echo "✅ Branch protection configured"

# ============================================================================
# Merge Request Template
# File: .gitlab/merge_request_templates/default.md
# ============================================================================

## Description


## Type of Change
- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change fixing an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change adding functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature causing existing functionality to change)
- [ ] 📝 Documentation update
- [ ] 🔧 Configuration change
- [ ] ♻️ Refactoring (no functional changes)

## Related Issues


## Django Model Changes
- [ ] No model changes
- [ ] New models added
- [ ] Existing models modified
- [ ] Migrations included

## Testing Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated
- [ ] Integration tests added/updated
- [ ] All tests pass locally
- [ ] Manual testing completed

## Deployment Notes


## Screenshots (if applicable)
Section 71.2: Build and Test Automation

Scenario: Comprehensive test automation for Django including unit tests, integration tests, and coverage reporting in CI pipeline.

# ============================================================================
# GitLab CI/CD - Lint and Test Stages
# Part of .gitlab-ci.yml
# ============================================================================

# Lint stage jobs
lint:python:
  stage: lint
  script:
    - pip install flake8 black isort mypy
    - echo "🔍 Running flake8..."
    - flake8 --config=setup.cfg .
    - echo "🎨 Checking Black formatting..."
    - black --check --diff .
    - echo "📦 Checking import sorting..."
    - isort --check-only --diff .
    - echo "🔎 Running mypy type checking..."
    - mypy --config-file=mypy.ini .
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"'
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH =~ /^(feature|develop|release|hotfix).*$/'

lint:security:
  stage: lint
  script:
    - pip install bandit safety
    - echo "🛡️ Running Bandit security linter..."
    - bandit -r . -c bandit.yaml -f json -o bandit-report.json || true
    - echo "🔐 Checking dependencies for vulnerabilities..."
    - safety check -r requirements.txt --full-report
  artifacts:
    reports:
      sast: bandit-report.json
    expire_in: 1 week

# Test stage jobs
test:unit:
  stage: test
  services:
    - postgres:15-alpine
    - redis:7-alpine
  variables:
    POSTGRES_DB: test_jobpost
    POSTGRES_USER: test_user
    POSTGRES_PASSWORD: test_password
    DATABASE_URL: "postgresql://test_user:test_password@postgres:5432/test_jobpost"
    REDIS_URL: "redis://redis:6379/0"
    DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: "anihpj.settings.test"
  before_script:
    - pip install -r requirements.txt -r requirements-test.txt
    - python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
  script:
    - echo "🧪 Running unit tests..."
    - pytest tests/unit/ -v --cov=jobpost --cov-report=xml --cov-report=html
  coverage: '/^TOTAL.+?(\d+\%)$/'
  artifacts:
    reports:
      coverage_report:
        coverage_format: cobertura
        path: coverage.xml
    paths:
      - htmlcov/
    expire_in: 1 week

test:integration:
  stage: test
  services:
    - postgres:15-alpine
    - redis:7-alpine
  variables:
    POSTGRES_DB: test_jobpost
    POSTGRES_USER: test_user
    POSTGRES_PASSWORD: test_password
    DATABASE_URL: "postgresql://test_user:test_password@postgres:5432/test_jobpost"
    REDIS_URL: "redis://redis:6379/0"
    DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: "anihpj.settings.test"
  before_script:
    - pip install -r requirements.txt -r requirements-test.txt
    - python manage.py migrate --noinput
    - python manage.py loaddata tests/fixtures/test_data.json
  script:
    - echo "🔗 Running integration tests..."
    - pytest tests/integration/ -v --tb=short
  needs:
    - test:unit

# ============================================================================
# Django Test Configuration
# File: pytest.ini
# ============================================================================

[pytest]
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = anihpj.settings.test
python_files = test_*.py
python_classes = Test*
python_functions = test_*
addopts = 
    -v
    --strict-markers
    --tb=short
    --cov=jobpost
    --cov-branch
    --cov-report=term-missing
    --cov-fail-under=80
markers =
    unit: Unit tests
    integration: Integration tests
    slow: Slow tests
    api: API tests
filterwarnings =
    ignore::DeprecationWarning
    ignore::PendingDeprecationWarning

# ============================================================================
# Example Django Unit Test
# File: tests/unit/test_models.py
# ============================================================================

import pytest
from django.test import TestCase
from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError
from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, Author, Location

@pytest.mark.unit
class TestJobPostModel(TestCase):
    """Unit tests for JobPost model"""
    
    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
    def setup_data(self, db):
        """Set up test fixtures"""
        self.author = Author.objects.create(
            name="Test Author",
            email="test@example.com"
        )
        self.location = Location.objects.create(
            city="New York",
            state="NY",
            country="USA"
        )
        self.skill = Skills.objects.create(
            name="Python",
            category="Programming"
        )
    
    def test_jobpost_creation(self):
        """Test JobPost model creation"""
        job = JobPost.objects.create(
            title="Senior Django Developer",
            description="Looking for experienced Django developer",
            author=self.author,
            location=self.location,
            salary_min=100000,
            salary_max=150000
        )
        job.required_skills.add(self.skill)
        
        assert job.pk is not None
        assert job.title == "Senior Django Developer"
        assert job.required_skills.count() == 1
    
    def test_jobpost_salary_validation(self):
        """Test salary validation (min should be less than max)"""
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            job = JobPost(
                title="Invalid Job",
                description="Test",
                author=self.author,
                location=self.location,
                salary_min=150000,
                salary_max=100000
            )
            job.full_clean()
    
    def test_jobpost_str_representation(self):
        """Test string representation"""
        job = JobPost.objects.create(
            title="Python Developer",
            description="Test",
            author=self.author,
            location=self.location
        )
        assert str(job) == "Python Developer"

# ============================================================================
# Integration Test Example
# File: tests/integration/test_api.py
# ============================================================================

import pytest
from rest_framework.test import APIClient
from rest_framework import status
from django.urls import reverse

@pytest.mark.integration
@pytest.mark.api
class TestJobPostAPI:
    """Integration tests for JobPost API endpoints"""
    
    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
    def setup(self, db, django_user_model):
        """Set up API client and test user"""
        self.client = APIClient()
        self.user = django_user_model.objects.create_user(
            username='testuser',
            password='testpass123',
            email='test@example.com'
        )
        self.client.force_authenticate(user=self.user)
    
    def test_list_jobposts(self, create_sample_jobposts):
        """Test listing all job posts"""
        url = reverse('jobpost-list')
        response = self.client.get(url)
        
        assert response.status_code == status.HTTP_200_OK
        assert len(response.data['results']) > 0
    
    def test_create_jobpost(self, sample_author, sample_location):
        """Test creating a new job post"""
        url = reverse('jobpost-list')
        data = {
            'title': 'New Django Position',
            'description': 'Exciting opportunity',
            'author': sample_author.pk,
            'location': sample_location.pk,
            'salary_min': 80000,
            'salary_max': 120000
        }
        
        response = self.client.post(url, data, format='json')
        
        assert response.status_code == status.HTTP_201_CREATED
        assert response.data['title'] == 'New Django Position'
Section 71.3: Container Image Pipeline

Scenario: Build optimized Docker images for Django with multi-stage builds, security scanning, and push to AWS ECR.

# ============================================================================
# Multi-Stage Dockerfile for Django Application
# File: Dockerfile
# ============================================================================

# Stage 1: Build stage
FROM python:3.11-slim as builder

WORKDIR /app

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    libpq-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy requirements and install dependencies
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip wheel --no-cache-dir --no-deps --wheel-dir /app/wheels -r requirements.txt

# Stage 2: Production stage
FROM python:3.11-slim as production

# Create non-root user
RUN groupadd -r django && useradd -r -g django django

WORKDIR /app

# Install runtime dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    libpq5 \
    curl \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy wheels from builder and install
COPY --from=builder /app/wheels /wheels
RUN pip install --no-cache /wheels/*

# Copy application code
COPY --chown=django:django . .

# Collect static files
RUN python manage.py collectstatic --noinput

# Set environment variables
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.production

# Switch to non-root user
USER django

# Health check
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD curl -f http://localhost:8000/health/ || exit 1

# Expose port
EXPOSE 8000

# Run gunicorn
CMD ["gunicorn", "--bind", "0.0.0.0:8000", "--workers", "4", "--threads", "2", "anihpj.wsgi:application"]

# ============================================================================
# GitLab CI/CD - Build Stage
# Part of .gitlab-ci.yml
# ============================================================================

build:docker:
  stage: build
  image: docker:24-dind
  services:
    - docker:24-dind
  variables:
    DOCKER_HOST: tcp://docker:2376
    DOCKER_TLS_CERTDIR: "/certs"
  before_script:
    # Install AWS CLI
    - apk add --no-cache python3 py3-pip
    - pip3 install awscli
    # Configure AWS credentials
    - aws configure set aws_access_key_id $AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
    - aws configure set aws_secret_access_key $AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
    - aws configure set region $AWS_REGION
    # Login to ECR
    - aws ecr get-login-password --region $AWS_REGION | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin $ECR_REGISTRY
  script:
    # Build Docker image with cache
    - echo "🐳 Building Docker image..."
    - |
      docker build \
        --cache-from ${ECR_REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:latest \
        --build-arg BUILDKIT_INLINE_CACHE=1 \
        --tag ${ECR_REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:${CI_COMMIT_SHA} \
        --tag ${ECR_REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG} \
        --tag ${ECR_REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:latest \
        .
    # Push all tags
    - echo "📤 Pushing to ECR..."
    - docker push ${ECR_REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:${CI_COMMIT_SHA}
    - docker push ${ECR_REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}
    - docker push ${ECR_REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:latest
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "develop"'
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH =~ /^release\/.*$/'

security:container-scan:
  stage: security
  image: 
    name: aquasec/trivy:latest
    entrypoint: [""]
  variables:
    TRIVY_NO_PROGRESS: "true"
    TRIVY_CACHE_DIR: ".trivycache/"
  script:
    - echo "🔒 Scanning container for vulnerabilities..."
    - trivy image --exit-code 0 --severity LOW,MEDIUM ${ECR_REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:${CI_COMMIT_SHA}
    - trivy image --exit-code 1 --severity HIGH,CRITICAL ${ECR_REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:${CI_COMMIT_SHA}
  cache:
    paths:
      - .trivycache/
  needs:
    - build:docker
  allow_failure: false

# ============================================================================
# Container Structure Test Configuration
# File: container-structure-test.yaml
# ============================================================================

schemaVersion: 2.0.0

metadataTest:
  user: "django"
  workdir: "/app"
  exposedPorts: ["8000"]
  
commandTests:
  - name: "Python version check"
    command: "python"
    args: ["--version"]
    expectedOutput: ["Python 3.11"]
    
  - name: "Django installed"
    command: "python"
    args: ["-c", "import django; print(django.VERSION)"]
    exitCode: 0
    
  - name: "Gunicorn installed"
    command: "which"
    args: ["gunicorn"]
    exitCode: 0

fileExistenceTests:
  - name: "Application code exists"
    path: "/app/manage.py"
    shouldExist: true
    
  - name: "Static files collected"
    path: "/app/staticfiles"
    shouldExist: true
    
  - name: "No .git directory"
    path: "/app/.git"
    shouldExist: false

fileContentTests:
  - name: "Requirements installed"
    path: "/app/requirements.txt"
    expectedContents: ["Django>=4.2", "gunicorn", "psycopg2-binary"]
Section 71.4: Multi-Environment Deployment

Scenario: Deploy Django application to dev, staging, and production environments on AWS ECS with environment-specific configurations.

# ============================================================================
# GitLab CI/CD - Deployment Stages
# Part of .gitlab-ci.yml
# ============================================================================

# Deploy templates
.deploy_template: &deploy_template
  image: 
    name: amazon/aws-cli:2.13.0
    entrypoint: [""]
  before_script:
    - yum install -y jq
    - aws configure set aws_access_key_id $AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
    - aws configure set aws_secret_access_key $AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
    - aws configure set region $AWS_REGION
  script:
    - echo "🚀 Deploying to ${ENVIRONMENT}..."
    - |
      # Update ECS task definition with new image
      TASK_DEFINITION=$(aws ecs describe-task-definition \
        --task-definition ${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT} \
        --query 'taskDefinition' \
        --output json)
      
      NEW_TASK_DEF=$(echo $TASK_DEFINITION | jq --arg IMAGE "${ECR_REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:${CI_COMMIT_SHA}" \
        '.containerDefinitions[0].image = $IMAGE | 
        del(.taskDefinitionArn) | 
        del(.revision) | 
        del(.status) | 
        del(.requiresAttributes) | 
        del(.compatibilities) | 
        del(.registeredAt) | 
        del(.registeredBy)')
      
      NEW_TASK_INFO=$(aws ecs register-task-definition \
        --cli-input-json "$NEW_TASK_DEF")
      
      NEW_REVISION=$(echo $NEW_TASK_INFO | jq -r '.taskDefinition.revision')
      
      echo "📋 New task revision: ${NEW_REVISION}"
    - |
      # Update ECS service
      aws ecs update-service \
        --cluster ${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}-cluster \
        --service ${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}-service \
        --task-definition ${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}:${NEW_REVISION} \
        --force-new-deployment
    - |
      # Wait for deployment to complete
      echo "⏳ Waiting for deployment to stabilize..."
      aws ecs wait services-stable \
        --cluster ${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}-cluster \
        --services ${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}-service
      echo "✅ Deployment to ${ENVIRONMENT} complete!"

# Development environment
deploy:dev:
  <<: *deploy_template
  stage: deploy-dev
  variables:
    ENVIRONMENT: dev
  environment:
    name: development
    url: https://dev.jobpost.example.com
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "develop"'
      when: always

# Staging environment
deploy:staging:
  <<: *deploy_template
  stage: deploy-staging
  variables:
    ENVIRONMENT: staging
  environment:
    name: staging
    url: https://staging.jobpost.example.com
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH =~ /^release\/.*$/'
      when: always
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
      when: manual

# Manual approval gate
approve:production:
  stage: approve
  script:
    - echo "✋ Waiting for production deployment approval..."
  environment:
    name: production
    action: prepare
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
      when: manual
  allow_failure: false

# Production environment
deploy:production:
  <<: *deploy_template
  stage: deploy-production
  variables:
    ENVIRONMENT: production
  environment:
    name: production
    url: https://jobpost.example.com
  needs:
    - approve:production
    - deploy:staging
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
      when: on_success

# ============================================================================
# Terraform ECS Module for Environment
# File: terraform/modules/ecs-service/main.tf
# ============================================================================

resource "aws_ecs_cluster" "main" {
  name = "${var.app_name}-${var.environment}-cluster"
  
  setting {
    name  = "containerInsights"
    value = "enabled"
  }
  
  tags = merge(var.common_tags, {
    Environment = var.environment
  })
}

resource "aws_ecs_task_definition" "app" {
  family                   = "${var.app_name}-${var.environment}"
  network_mode             = "awsvpc"
  requires_compatibilities = ["FARGATE"]
  cpu                      = var.task_cpu
  memory                   = var.task_memory
  execution_role_arn       = aws_iam_role.ecs_execution.arn
  task_role_arn            = aws_iam_role.ecs_task.arn
  
  container_definitions = jsonencode([
    {
      name  = var.app_name
      image = "${var.ecr_repository}:${var.image_tag}"
      
      portMappings = [
        {
          containerPort = 8000
          hostPort      = 8000
          protocol      = "tcp"
        }
      ]
      
      environment = [
        { name = "DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE", value = "anihpj.settings.${var.environment}" },
        { name = "ALLOWED_HOSTS", value = var.allowed_hosts },
        { name = "DEBUG", value = tostring(var.debug_enabled) }
      ]
      
      secrets = [
        { name = "DATABASE_URL", valueFrom = aws_ssm_parameter.db_url.arn },
        { name = "SECRET_KEY", valueFrom = aws_ssm_parameter.secret_key.arn },
        { name = "REDIS_URL", valueFrom = aws_ssm_parameter.redis_url.arn }
      ]
      
      logConfiguration = {
        logDriver = "awslogs"
        options = {
          awslogs-group         = aws_cloudwatch_log_group.app.name
          awslogs-region        = var.aws_region
          awslogs-stream-prefix = var.app_name
        }
      }
      
      healthCheck = {
        command     = ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -f http://localhost:8000/health/ || exit 1"]
        interval    = 30
        timeout     = 10
        retries     = 3
        startPeriod = 60
      }
    }
  ])
}

resource "aws_ecs_service" "app" {
  name            = "${var.app_name}-${var.environment}-service"
  cluster         = aws_ecs_cluster.main.id
  task_definition = aws_ecs_task_definition.app.arn
  desired_count   = var.desired_count
  launch_type     = "FARGATE"
  
  deployment_configuration {
    maximum_percent         = 200
    minimum_healthy_percent = 100
  }
  
  deployment_circuit_breaker {
    enable   = true
    rollback = true
  }
  
  network_configuration {
    subnets          = var.private_subnet_ids
    security_groups  = [aws_security_group.ecs_tasks.id]
    assign_public_ip = false
  }
  
  load_balancer {
    target_group_arn = aws_lb_target_group.app.arn
    container_name   = var.app_name
    container_port   = 8000
  }
}

# ============================================================================
# Environment-specific Django Settings
# File: anihpj/settings/production.py
# ============================================================================

from .base import *
import os

DEBUG = False
ALLOWED_HOSTS = os.environ.get('ALLOWED_HOSTS', '').split(',')

# Security settings
SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT = True
SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS = 31536000
SECURE_HSTS_INCLUDE_SUBDOMAINS = True
SECURE_HSTS_PRELOAD = True
SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE = True
CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE = True
SECURE_BROWSER_XSS_FILTER = True
SECURE_CONTENT_TYPE_NOSNIFF = True
X_FRAME_OPTIONS = 'DENY'

# Database
DATABASES = {
    'default': {
        'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql',
        'NAME': os.environ.get('DB_NAME', 'jobpost'),
        'USER': os.environ.get('DB_USER'),
        'PASSWORD': os.environ.get('DB_PASSWORD'),
        'HOST': os.environ.get('DB_HOST'),
        'PORT': os.environ.get('DB_PORT', '5432'),
        'CONN_MAX_AGE': 600,
        'OPTIONS': {
            'sslmode': 'require',
        }
    }
}

# Cache
CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django.core.cache.backends.redis.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': os.environ.get('REDIS_URL'),
        'OPTIONS': {
            'ssl_cert_reqs': None,
        }
    }
}

# Logging
LOGGING = {
    'version': 1,
    'disable_existing_loggers': False,
    'formatters': {
        'json': {
            '()': 'pythonjsonlogger.jsonlogger.JsonFormatter',
            'format': '%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s %(message)s'
        }
    },
    'handlers': {
        'console': {
            'class': 'logging.StreamHandler',
            'formatter': 'json',
        }
    },
    'root': {
        'handlers': ['console'],
        'level': 'INFO',
    },
    'loggers': {
        'django': {
            'handlers': ['console'],
            'level': 'INFO',
            'propagate': False,
        },
        'jobpost': {
            'handlers': ['console'],
            'level': 'INFO',
            'propagate': False,
        }
    }
}
Section 71.5: Database Migration Automation

Scenario: Automate Django database migrations with safety checks, rollback capabilities, and zero-downtime deployment strategies.

# ============================================================================
# Database Migration Automation Script
# File: scripts/migrate.sh
# ============================================================================

#!/bin/bash
# Automated Django database migration with safety checks

set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
ENVIRONMENT="${ENVIRONMENT:-development}"
DRY_RUN="${DRY_RUN:-false}"
BACKUP_BEFORE_MIGRATE="${BACKUP_BEFORE_MIGRATE:-true}"
MAX_MIGRATION_TIME=300  # 5 minutes timeout

# Logging
log() { echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $*"; }
error() { log "ERROR: $*" >&2; }

# Validate environment
validate_environment() {
    log "🔍 Validating environment: ${ENVIRONMENT}"
    
    # Check database connectivity
    python manage.py dbshell <<< "SELECT 1;" >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
        error "Cannot connect to database"
        exit 1
    }
    
    log "✅ Database connection successful"
}

# Create database backup
create_backup() {
    if [[ "${BACKUP_BEFORE_MIGRATE}" == "true" ]]; then
        log "💾 Creating database backup..."
        
        BACKUP_FILE="backup_${ENVIRONMENT}_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).sql"
        
        if [[ "${ENVIRONMENT}" == "production" ]]; then
            # AWS RDS snapshot for production
            aws rds create-db-snapshot \
                --db-instance-identifier "jobpost-${ENVIRONMENT}" \
                --db-snapshot-identifier "pre-migration-$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
        else
            # pg_dump for non-production
            pg_dump "${DATABASE_URL}" > "/backups/${BACKUP_FILE}"
        fi
        
        log "✅ Backup created: ${BACKUP_FILE}"
    fi
}

# Check pending migrations
check_migrations() {
    log "📋 Checking pending migrations..."
    
    PENDING=$(python manage.py showmigrations --plan | grep -c "\[ \]" || true)
    
    if [[ "${PENDING}" -eq 0 ]]; then
        log "✅ No pending migrations"
        exit 0
    fi
    
    log "📝 Found ${PENDING} pending migration(s)"
    python manage.py showmigrations --plan | grep "\[ \]"
}

# Analyze migration safety
analyze_migrations() {
    log "🔬 Analyzing migration safety..."
    
    # Check for potentially dangerous operations
    python manage.py migrate --plan 2>&1 | while read -r line; do
        if echo "$line" | grep -qi "drop\|delete\|remove\|alter.*type"; then
            log "⚠️  Potentially dangerous operation detected: $line"
        fi
    done
    
    # Generate SQL preview
    log "📜 Migration SQL preview:"
    python manage.py sqlmigrate --verbosity 2 jobpost <latest_migration> 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Run migrations
run_migrations() {
    if [[ "${DRY_RUN}" == "true" ]]; then
        log "🧪 Dry run mode - would execute:"
        python manage.py migrate --plan
        return 0
    fi
    
    log "🚀 Running migrations..."
    
    # Run with timeout
    timeout "${MAX_MIGRATION_TIME}" python manage.py migrate --noinput || {
        error "Migration timed out or failed"
        return 1
    }
    
    log "✅ Migrations completed successfully"
}

# Verify migrations
verify_migrations() {
    log "🔍 Verifying migrations..."
    
    # Check for unapplied migrations
    UNAPPLIED=$(python manage.py showmigrations --plan | grep -c "\[ \]" || true)
    
    if [[ "${UNAPPLIED}" -gt 0 ]]; then
        error "Found ${UNAPPLIED} unapplied migration(s) after migration"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Run Django system checks
    python manage.py check --deploy || {
        error "Django deployment checks failed"
        return 1
    }
    
    log "✅ All migrations verified"
}

# Main execution
main() {
    log "🏁 Starting migration process for ${ENVIRONMENT}"
    
    validate_environment
    check_migrations
    create_backup
    analyze_migrations
    run_migrations
    verify_migrations
    
    log "🎉 Migration process completed successfully"
}

main "$@"

# ============================================================================
# GitLab CI/CD - Migration Job
# Part of .gitlab-ci.yml
# ============================================================================

migrate:database:
  stage: deploy-dev
  image: python:${PYTHON_VERSION}
  before_script:
    - pip install -r requirements.txt
    - apt-get update && apt-get install -y postgresql-client
  script:
    - echo "🗃️ Running database migrations..."
    - ./scripts/migrate.sh
  variables:
    ENVIRONMENT: dev
    DRY_RUN: "false"
    BACKUP_BEFORE_MIGRATE: "true"
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "develop"'
      changes:
        - "**/migrations/*.py"
  needs:
    - test:integration

# ============================================================================
# Zero-Downtime Migration Strategy (Python)
# File: scripts/zero_downtime_migrate.py
# ============================================================================

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Zero-downtime database migration strategy for Django.
Implements expand-contract pattern for safe schema changes.
"""

import os
import sys
import django
from django.core.management import call_command
from django.db import connection, transaction

os.environ.setdefault('DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE', 'anihpj.settings.production')
django.setup()

class ZeroDowntimeMigrator:
    """Implements expand-contract migration pattern"""
    
    def __init__(self, environment: str):
        self.environment = environment
        self.dangerous_operations = [
            'DROP TABLE', 'DROP COLUMN', 'ALTER COLUMN',
            'RENAME TABLE', 'RENAME COLUMN'
        ]
    
    def analyze_migration_sql(self, app: str, migration: str) -> dict:
        """Analyze migration SQL for dangerous operations"""
        from io import StringIO
        out = StringIO()
        
        call_command('sqlmigrate', app, migration, stdout=out)
        sql = out.getvalue()
        
        analysis = {
            'sql': sql,
            'is_safe': True,
            'warnings': []
        }
        
        for op in self.dangerous_operations:
            if op in sql.upper():
                analysis['is_safe'] = False
                analysis['warnings'].append(f"Contains {op}")
        
        return analysis
    
    def create_nullable_column(self, table: str, column: str, 
                               data_type: str) -> str:
        """Generate SQL for adding nullable column (expand phase)"""
        return f"""
        ALTER TABLE {table} 
        ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS {column} {data_type} NULL;
        """
    
    def backfill_column(self, table: str, column: str, 
                        source_column: str = None, 
                        default_value: str = None) -> str:
        """Generate SQL for backfilling column data"""
        if source_column:
            return f"""
            UPDATE {table} SET {column} = {source_column}
            WHERE {column} IS NULL;
            """
        elif default_value:
            return f"""
            UPDATE {table} SET {column} = {default_value}
            WHERE {column} IS NULL;
            """
    
    def add_constraint(self, table: str, column: str) -> str:
        """Generate SQL for adding NOT NULL constraint (contract phase)"""
        return f"""
        ALTER TABLE {table} 
        ALTER COLUMN {column} SET NOT NULL;
        """
    
    def run_safe_migration(self, migrations: list):
        """Run migrations with safety checks"""
        print(f"🚀 Starting zero-downtime migration")
        
        for migration in migrations:
            app, name = migration.split('.')
            
            # Analyze migration
            analysis = self.analyze_migration_sql(app, name)
            
            if not analysis['is_safe']:
                print(f"⚠️  Warning for {migration}:")
                for warning in analysis['warnings']:
                    print(f"   - {warning}")
                
                if self.environment == 'production':
                    response = input("Continue? [y/N]: ")
                    if response.lower() != 'y':
                        print("Migration aborted")
                        sys.exit(1)
            
            # Run migration
            print(f"📋 Applying {migration}...")
            
            with transaction.atomic():
                call_command('migrate', app, name, '--noinput')
            
            print(f"✅ {migration} applied successfully")
        
        print("🎉 All migrations completed")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    env = os.getenv('ENVIRONMENT', 'development')
    migrator = ZeroDowntimeMigrator(env)
    
    # Get pending migrations
    from io import StringIO
    out = StringIO()
    call_command('showmigrations', '--plan', stdout=out)
    
    pending = [
        line.split()[-1] 
        for line in out.getvalue().split('\n') 
        if '[ ]' in line
    ]
    
    if pending:
        migrator.run_safe_migration(pending)
    else:
        print("✅ No pending migrations")
Section 71.6: Rollback and Recovery Scripts

Scenario: Implement automated rollback mechanisms for failed deployments and database migrations with state recovery.

# ============================================================================
# Deployment Rollback Script
# File: scripts/rollback.sh
# ============================================================================

#!/bin/bash
# Automated rollback for Django application deployment

set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
ENVIRONMENT="${ENVIRONMENT:-development}"
ROLLBACK_TYPE="${ROLLBACK_TYPE:-deployment}"  # deployment, migration, full
APP_NAME="django-jobpost"
AWS_REGION="${AWS_REGION:-us-east-1}"

# Logging
log() { echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] ROLLBACK: $*"; }
error() { log "ERROR: $*" >&2; }

# Get previous deployment
get_previous_deployment() {
    log "🔍 Finding previous stable deployment..."
    
    # Get task definition revisions
    REVISIONS=$(aws ecs list-task-definitions \
        --family-prefix "${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}" \
        --sort DESC \
        --max-items 5 \
        --query 'taskDefinitionArns' \
        --output json)
    
    # Current revision
    CURRENT=$(aws ecs describe-services \
        --cluster "${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}-cluster" \
        --services "${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}-service" \
        --query 'services[0].taskDefinition' \
        --output text)
    
    # Find previous stable revision
    PREVIOUS=$(echo "$REVISIONS" | jq -r ".[] | select(. != \"${CURRENT}\")" | head -1)
    
    if [[ -z "$PREVIOUS" ]]; then
        error "No previous deployment found for rollback"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    echo "$PREVIOUS"
}

# Rollback ECS deployment
rollback_deployment() {
    local target_revision="${1:-}"
    
    if [[ -z "$target_revision" ]]; then
        target_revision=$(get_previous_deployment)
    fi
    
    log "🔄 Rolling back to: ${target_revision}"
    
    # Update ECS service to previous task definition
    aws ecs update-service \
        --cluster "${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}-cluster" \
        --service "${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}-service" \
        --task-definition "${target_revision}" \
        --force-new-deployment
    
    # Wait for rollback deployment to stabilize
    log "⏳ Waiting for rollback deployment to stabilize..."
    aws ecs wait services-stable \
        --cluster "${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}-cluster" \
        --services "${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}-service"
    
    log "✅ Deployment rollback completed"
}

# Rollback database migration
rollback_migration() {
    local target_migration="${1:-}"
    
    log "🗃️ Rolling back database migration..."
    
    if [[ -z "$target_migration" ]]; then
        # Rollback last migration
        LAST_MIGRATION=$(python manage.py showmigrations --plan | \
            grep "\[X\]" | tail -2 | head -1 | awk '{print $2}')
        
        APP_NAME_DJANGO=$(echo "$LAST_MIGRATION" | cut -d. -f1)
        MIGRATION_NAME=$(echo "$LAST_MIGRATION" | cut -d. -f2)
        
        target_migration="${APP_NAME_DJANGO} ${MIGRATION_NAME}"
    fi
    
    log "📋 Rolling back to: ${target_migration}"
    
    # Run reverse migration
    python manage.py migrate ${target_migration} --noinput
    
    log "✅ Migration rollback completed"
}

# Restore from database backup
restore_database() {
    local snapshot_id="${1:-}"
    
    log "💾 Restoring database from backup..."
    
    if [[ -z "$snapshot_id" ]]; then
        # Find latest snapshot
        snapshot_id=$(aws rds describe-db-snapshots \
            --db-instance-identifier "jobpost-${ENVIRONMENT}" \
            --query 'DBSnapshots | sort_by(@, &SnapshotCreateTime) | [-1].DBSnapshotIdentifier' \
            --output text)
    fi
    
    log "📦 Using snapshot: ${snapshot_id}"
    
    # Create new instance from snapshot
    aws rds restore-db-instance-from-db-snapshot \
        --db-instance-identifier "jobpost-${ENVIRONMENT}-restored" \
        --db-snapshot-identifier "${snapshot_id}" \
        --db-instance-class db.t3.medium
    
    # Wait for restore
    aws rds wait db-instance-available \
        --db-instance-identifier "jobpost-${ENVIRONMENT}-restored"
    
    log "✅ Database restored successfully"
}

# Full rollback (deployment + database)
full_rollback() {
    log "🔄 Starting full rollback..."
    
    # Stop current deployment first
    aws ecs update-service \
        --cluster "${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}-cluster" \
        --service "${APP_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}-service" \
        --desired-count 0
    
    # Restore database
    restore_database
    
    # Rollback deployment
    rollback_deployment
    
    log "✅ Full rollback completed"
}

# Send notification
send_notification() {
    local status="$1"
    local message="$2"
    
    # Slack notification
    curl -X POST "${SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL}" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d "{
            \"text\": \"🔄 Rollback ${status}: ${ENVIRONMENT}\",
            \"attachments\": [{
                \"color\": \"$([ \"$status\" == \"SUCCESS\" ] && echo 'good' || echo 'danger')\",
                \"text\": \"${message}\"
            }]
        }"
}

# Main execution
main() {
    log "🏁 Starting rollback process"
    log "Environment: ${ENVIRONMENT}"
    log "Rollback Type: ${ROLLBACK_TYPE}"
    
    case "${ROLLBACK_TYPE}" in
        deployment)
            rollback_deployment "${TARGET_REVISION:-}"
            ;;
        migration)
            rollback_migration "${TARGET_MIGRATION:-}"
            ;;
        database)
            restore_database "${SNAPSHOT_ID:-}"
            ;;
        full)
            full_rollback
            ;;
        *)
            error "Unknown rollback type: ${ROLLBACK_TYPE}"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
    
    send_notification "SUCCESS" "Rollback completed for ${ENVIRONMENT}"
    log "🎉 Rollback process completed"
}

main "$@"

# ============================================================================
# GitLab CI/CD - Rollback Job
# Part of .gitlab-ci.yml
# ============================================================================

rollback:production:
  stage: rollback
  image: 
    name: amazon/aws-cli:2.13.0
    entrypoint: [""]
  before_script:
    - yum install -y jq curl
    - aws configure set aws_access_key_id $AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
    - aws configure set aws_secret_access_key $AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
    - aws configure set region $AWS_REGION
  script:
    - echo "🔄 Initiating production rollback..."
    - ./scripts/rollback.sh
  variables:
    ENVIRONMENT: production
    ROLLBACK_TYPE: deployment
  environment:
    name: production
    action: stop
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
      when: manual
  allow_failure: false

# ============================================================================
# Python Rollback Manager
# File: scripts/rollback_manager.py
# ============================================================================

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Comprehensive rollback manager for Django application.
Tracks deployment state and manages rollback procedures.
"""

import os
import json
import boto3
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional, Dict, List
from dataclasses import dataclass, asdict

@dataclass
class DeploymentState:
    """Represents a deployment state snapshot"""
    deployment_id: str
    timestamp: str
    environment: str
    task_definition: str
    image_tag: str
    migration_state: str
    config_version: str
    status: str

class RollbackManager:
    """Manages deployment rollbacks with state tracking"""
    
    def __init__(self, environment: str):
        self.environment = environment
        self.ecs = boto3.client('ecs')
        self.s3 = boto3.client('s3')
        self.state_bucket = f"jobpost-{environment}-deployment-state"
    
    def save_deployment_state(self, state: DeploymentState):
        """Save deployment state to S3"""
        key = f"deployments/{state.deployment_id}.json"
        
        self.s3.put_object(
            Bucket=self.state_bucket,
            Key=key,
            Body=json.dumps(asdict(state)),
            ContentType='application/json'
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Saved deployment state: {state.deployment_id}")
    
    def get_deployment_history(self, limit: int = 10) -> List[DeploymentState]:
        """Get recent deployment history"""
        response = self.s3.list_objects_v2(
            Bucket=self.state_bucket,
            Prefix='deployments/',
            MaxKeys=limit
        )
        
        states = []
        for obj in sorted(response.get('Contents', []), 
                         key=lambda x: x['LastModified'], reverse=True):
            data = self.s3.get_object(
                Bucket=self.state_bucket, 
                Key=obj['Key']
            )
            state_dict = json.loads(data['Body'].read())
            states.append(DeploymentState(**state_dict))
        
        return states
    
    def find_last_stable_deployment(self) -> Optional[DeploymentState]:
        """Find the last successful deployment"""
        history = self.get_deployment_history(limit=20)
        
        for state in history:
            if state.status == 'SUCCESS':
                return state
        
        return None
    
    def rollback_to_state(self, state: DeploymentState):
        """Rollback to a specific deployment state"""
        print(f"🔄 Rolling back to deployment: {state.deployment_id}")
        
        # Update ECS service
        self.ecs.update_service(
            cluster=f"jobpost-{self.environment}-cluster",
            service=f"jobpost-{self.environment}-service",
            taskDefinition=state.task_definition,
            forceNewDeployment=True
        )
        
        # Wait for deployment
        waiter = self.ecs.get_waiter('services_stable')
        waiter.wait(
            cluster=f"jobpost-{self.environment}-cluster",
            services=[f"jobpost-{self.environment}-service"]
        )
        
        # Record rollback
        rollback_state = DeploymentState(
            deployment_id=f"rollback-{datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y%m%d%H%M%S')}",
            timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            environment=self.environment,
            task_definition=state.task_definition,
            image_tag=state.image_tag,
            migration_state=state.migration_state,
            config_version=state.config_version,
            status='ROLLBACK'
        )
        self.save_deployment_state(rollback_state)
        
        print(f"✅ Rollback completed to {state.deployment_id}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import argparse
    
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Deployment Rollback Manager')
    parser.add_argument('--environment', required=True)
    parser.add_argument('--action', choices=['history', 'rollback', 'find-stable'])
    parser.add_argument('--deployment-id', help='Specific deployment to rollback to')
    
    args = parser.parse_args()
    
    manager = RollbackManager(args.environment)
    
    if args.action == 'history':
        for state in manager.get_deployment_history():
            print(f"{state.timestamp} | {state.deployment_id} | {state.status}")
    
    elif args.action == 'find-stable':
        stable = manager.find_last_stable_deployment()
        if stable:
            print(f"Last stable: {stable.deployment_id} ({stable.timestamp})")
    
    elif args.action == 'rollback':
        if args.deployment_id:
            # Find specific deployment
            history = manager.get_deployment_history(limit=50)
            target = next((s for s in history if s.deployment_id == args.deployment_id), None)
        else:
            # Rollback to last stable
            target = manager.find_last_stable_deployment()
        
        if target:
            manager.rollback_to_state(target)
        else:
            print("❌ No suitable deployment found for rollback")
